020101.cherokee: ᎠᏓᎴᏂᏍᎬ ᏱᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ, ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
020101.english: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God;
020101.phonetic: A-da-le-ni-s-gv yi-s-dv ka-no-he-dv, Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi u-tse-li-ga.

020102.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎬᏅ ᏥᎪᏪᎳ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩᏱ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ, ᏥᏅᎵ ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᏣᎧᏛ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏣᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓᏁᎵ ᏣᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ.
020102.english: As it is written in the prophets, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
020102.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya hi-a tsi-ni-gv-nv tsi-go-we-la a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi-yi; Gv-ni-yu-quo, tsi-nv-li tsi-nv-si-da-s-di tsa-ka-dv i-gv-yi-di-tlv, na-s-gi da-tsa-dv-nv-i-s-da-ne-li tsa-lo-hi-s-di-yi i-gv-yi-di-tlv.

020103.cherokee: ᎤᏪᎷᎦ ᎩᎶ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᎭ; ᎡᏣᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓᏏ ᏱᎰᏩ ᎤᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᏚᏅᏅ ᏗᏥᏥᏃᎯᏍᏓ.
020103.english: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
020103.phonetic: U-we-lu-ga gi-lo i-na-ge-i, hi-a ni-ga-we-ha; E-tsa-dv-nv-i-s-da-si Yi-ho-wa u-lo-hi-s-di-yi, du-nv-nv di-tsi-tsi-no-hi-s-da.

020104.cherokee: ᏣᏂ ᏓᏓᏬᏍᎨ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎨ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎨ ᏗᏓᏪᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎦᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗᏱ ᎣᏓᏅᏛ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏴᏓᏘᏁᏗᏱ ᎠᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ.
020104.english: John did baptize in the wilderness, and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
020104.phonetic: Tsa-ni da-da-wo-s-ge i-na-ge-i, a-le a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-ge ka-no-he-s-ge di-da-we-s-di ge-sv ga-ne-tli-yv-s-di-yi o-da-nv-dv u-gv-wa-li na-s-gi ga-yv-da-ti-ne-di-yi a-s-ga-nv-tsv-i.

020105.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏫᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᏧᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᎾᏬᎡ ᏦᏓᏂ ᎤᏪᏴᎢ, ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎠᏂᏃᎲᏍᎨᎢ.
020105.english: And there went out unto him all the land of Judaea, and they of Jerusalem, and were all baptized of him in the river of Jordan, confessing their sins.
020105.phonetic: A-le wi-gv-wa-lu-tse-le ni-gv-na-dv Tsu-di-yi a-le Tsi-lu-si-li-mi a-ne-hi, a-le ni-ga-dv na-s-gi du-na-wo-e Tso-da-ni u-we-yv-i, u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv a-ni-no-hv-s-ge-i.

020106.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏃ ᎤᏄᏪ ᎨᎻᎵ ᎤᏍᏗᏰᏅᎯ, ᎦᏃᏥᏃ ᎤᏓᏠᏍᏕᎢ; ᎥᎴᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏩᏚᎵᏏ ᎢᎾᎨ ᎡᎯ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
020106.english: And John was clothed with camel's hair, and with a girdle of a skin about his loins; and he did eat locusts and wild honey;
020106.phonetic: Tsa-ni-no u-nu-we ge-mi-li u-s-di-ye-nv-hi, ga-no-tsi-no u-da-tlo-s-de-i; v-le-no a-le wa-du-li-si i-na-ge e-hi a-li-s-da-yv-di-s-ge-i.

020107.cherokee: ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᎤᏟ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗᏳ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎠᏴ ᎣᏂ ᏓᏯᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎳᏑᎶᎩᎯ ᏓᎧᏁᏌᏛ ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᏱᏂᎪᎢ ᎠᏆᏗᏌᏓᏗᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎩᎧᏁᏴᏗᏱ.
020107.english: And preached, saying, There cometh one mightier than I after me, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to stoop down and unloose.
020107.phonetic: A-li-tsi-do-hv-s-ge-no hi-a ni-ga-we-s-ge-i; U-tli u-li-ni-gi-di-yu e-s-ga-quo a-yv o-ni da-ya-i, na-s-gi tsu-la-su-lo-gi-hi da-ka-ne-sa-dv v-tla ye-li yi-ni-go-i a-qua-di-sa-da-di-s-di-yi a-le di-gi-ka-ne-yv-di-yi.

020108.cherokee: ᎠᏴ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎠᎹ ᏕᏨᏯᏬᏍᏔᏅ, ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᏙᏓᏣᏬᏍᏔᏂ.
020108.english: I indeed have baptized you with water: but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
020108.phonetic: A-yv u-do-hi-yu-hi a-ma de-tsv-ya-wo-s-ta-nv, na-s-gi-s-gi-ni Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do do-da-tsa-wo-s-ta-ni.

020109.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᏥᏌ ᎾᏎᎵᏗ ᏧᎶᏎ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᎵᎵ ᏥᎦᏚᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏂ ᎤᏓᏬᎡ ᏦᏓᏂ.
020109.english: And it came to pass in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized of John in Jordan.
020109.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-no hi-a nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, Tsi-sa Na-se-li-di tsu-lo-se na-s-gi Ge-li-li tsi-ga-du-ha, a-le Tsa-ni u-da-wo-e Tso-da-ni.

020110.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏧᎿᎭᎷᏒ ᎠᎹᏱ ᏧᏓᏅᏒ, ᎤᎪᎮ ᎦᎸᎶᎢ ᎤᎵᏍᏚᎢᏛ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎫᎴᏗᏍᎪᏂᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᏧᏠᎠᏏᏗᏎ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏪᏯᎸᏤᎢ.
020110.english: And straightway coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon him:
020110.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv tsu-hna-lu-sv a-ma-yi tsu-da-nv-sv, u-go-he ga-lv-lo-i u-li-s-du-i-dv ge-se-i, a-le A-da-nv-do gu-le-di-s-go-ni-hi na-s-gi-ya-i, tsu-tlo-a-si-di-se na-s-gi u-we-ya-lv-tse-i.

020111.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏅᏧᏪᏎ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ, ᏂᎯ ᎬᎨᏳᎢ ᎠᏇᏥ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎬᏰᎸᎢ ᏥᎩ.
020111.english: And there came a voice from heaven, saying, Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
020111.phonetic: A-le hi-a nv-tsu-we-se ga-lv-la-di, ni-hi gv-ge-yu-i A-que-tsi, na-s-gi o-si-yu gv-ye-lv-i tsi-gi.

020112.english: And immediately the Spirit driveth him into the wilderness.

020113.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎢᎾᎨ ᎡᏙᎮ ᏅᎦᏍᎪᎯ ᏧᏒᎯᏛ, ᏎᏓᏂ ᎤᎪᎵᏰᏍᎨᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎢᎾᎨ ᎡᎿᎭᎢ ᏕᎨᎳᏗᏙᎮᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎬᏩᏍᏕᎸᎯᏙᎮᎢ.
020113.english: And he was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted of Satan; and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered unto him.
020113.phonetic: Na-hna-no i-na-ge e-do-he nv-ga-s-go-hi tsu-sv-hi-dv, Se-da-ni u-go-li-ye-s-ge-i; a-le i-na-ge e-hna-i de-ge-la-di-do-he-i; a-le di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi gv-wa-s-de-lv-hi-do-he-i.

020114.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏃ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ ᎾᏥᏴᏔᏁᎢ, ᏥᏌ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎤᎷᏤᎢ, ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎨ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ,
020114.english: Now after that John was put in prison, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God,
020114.phonetic: Tsa-ni-no di-da-s-du-di-yi na-tsi-yv-ta-ne-i, Tsi-sa Ge-li-li u-lu-tse-i, a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-ge o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv u-gv-wa-li,

020115.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎨᎢ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᎵᏱᎶᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎡᏍᎦᏂᏳ ᏓᏯᎢ; ᏗᏥᏁᏟᏴᎾ ᏕᏣᏓᏅᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏦᎯᏳᎲᎦ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ.
020115.english: And saying, The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel.
020115.phonetic: A-le hi-a ni-ga-we-s-ge-i, Hna-quo a-li-yi-lo-ga, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv e-s-ga-ni-yu da-ya-i; di-tsi-ne-tli-yv-na de-tsa-da-nv-dv-i, a-le i-tso-hi-yu-hv-ga o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv.

020116.cherokee: ᎨᎵᎵᏃ ᎥᏓᎵ ᎤᎶᏗ ᎠᎢᏒᎢ, ᏚᎪᎮ ᏌᏩᏂ ᎠᎴ ᎡᏂᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ, ᎥᏓᎵ ᎠᏂᎦᏯᎷᎥᏍᎨᎢ; ᎠᏂᎦᏯᎷᎥᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎨᏎᎢ.
020116.english: Now as he walked by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.
020116.phonetic: Ge-li-li-no v-da-li u-lo-di a-i-sv-i, du-go-he Sa-wa-ni a-le E-ni-di na-s-gi di-na-da-nv-tli, v-da-li a-ni-ga-ya-lu-v-s-ge-i; a-ni-ga-ya-lu-v-s-gi-ye-no ge-se-i.

020117.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏍᎩᏂᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦ, ᏴᏫᏃ ᏗᏍᏗᎦᏯᎷᎥᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᏍᏛᏴᏁᎵ.
020117.english: And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.
020117.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; S-gi-ni-s-da-wa-du-ga, yv-wi-no di-s-di-ga-ya-lu-v-s-gi nv-da-s-dv-yv-ne-li.

020118.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᏂᏲᏎ ᏧᏂᎦᏯᎷᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ.
020118.english: And straightway they forsook their nets, and followed him.
020118.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv du-ni-yo-se tsu-ni-ga-ya-lu-di, a-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-se-i.

020119.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏫᎤᏪᏅ ᎤᏍᏗᎩᏛ, ᏚᎪᎮ ᏥᎻ, ᏤᏈᏗ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᏣᏂᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᎾᏣᎡ ᏥᏳᎯ ᏓᏃᏢᎯᏏᏍᎨ ᏧᏂᎦᏯᎷᏗ.
020119.english: And when he had gone a little further thence, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the ship mending their nets.
020119.phonetic: Na-hna-no wi-u-we-nv u-s-di-gi-dv, du-go-he Tsi-mi, Tse-qui-di u-we-tsi, Tsa-ni-no na-s-gi di-na-da-nv-tli, na-s-gi-no na-s-quo u-na-tsa-e tsi-yu-hi da-no-tlv-hi-si-s-ge tsu-ni-ga-ya-lu-di.

020120.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎮᎢ; ᎤᏅᏕᏨᏃ ᎤᏂᏙᏓ ᏤᏈᏗ ᎨᎦᎫᏴᎡᎯ ᏧᏂᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᎾᏣᎥᎢ, ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ.
020120.english: And straightway he called them: and they left their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired servants, and went after him.
020120.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv na-s-gi wi-du-ya-nv-he-i; u-nv-de-tsv-no u-ni-do-da Tse-qui-di ge-ga-gu-yv-e-hi tsu-ni-nv-si-da-s-di i-tsu-la-ha tsi-yu-hi u-na-tsa-v-i, gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-se-i.

020121.cherokee: ᎨᏆᏂᏃ ᏭᏂᏴᎴᎢ; ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏭᏴᎸ ᏚᏕᏲᏁ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ.
020121.english: And they went into Capernaum; and straightway on the sabbath day he entered into the synagogue, and taught.
020121.phonetic: Ge-qua-ni-no wu-ni-yv-le-i; gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi wu-yv-lv du-de-yo-ne u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga.

020122.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᏃ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬᎢ, ᏚᏕᏲᏁᏰᏃ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᏧᎰ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᎥᏝᏃ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ.
020122.english: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for he taught them as one that had authority, and not as the scribes.
020122.phonetic: U-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-no nu-s-dv da-de-yo-hv-s-gv-i, du-de-yo-ne-ye-no u-li-ni-gi-dv tsu-ho na-s-gi-ya-i, v-tla-no di-no-we-li-s-gi da-na-de-yo-hv-s-gv na-s-gi-ya yi-ge-se-i.

020123.cherokee: ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱᏃ ᎠᏯᎡ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏯᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᎷᏁ,
020123.english: And there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
020123.phonetic: Tsu-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi-no a-ya-e a-s-ga-ya ga-da-ha a-da-nv-do u-ya-i, a-le u-we-lu-ne,

020124.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎣᎦᏁᎳᎩ; ᎦᏙ ᏗᎦᏓᏛᏙᏗ ᏂᎯ, ᏥᏌ, ᎾᏎᎵᏗ ᎮᎯ?  ᏥᎪ ᏍᎩᏛᏔᏂᎸ?  ᎬᎦᏔᎭ, ᏂᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
020124.english: Saying, Let us alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Holy One of God.
020124.phonetic: Hi-a nu-we-se-i, O-ga-ne-la-gi; ga-do di-ga-da-dv-do-di ni-hi, Tsi-sa, Na-se-li-di he-hi?  Tsi-go s-gi-dv-ta-ni-lv?  Gv-ga-ta-ha, ni-hi na-s-gi ge-sv-i; na-s-gi A-tsi-lv-quo-di U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga.

020125.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏍᎦᏤᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎡᎳᏪ ᎲᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᏄᎪᎢ.
020125.english: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.
020125.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-s-ga-tse-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; E-la-we hv-na, a-le hi-nu-go-i.

020126.cherokee: ᎦᏓᎭᏃ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᎸᏕᏍᏔᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎤᏪᎷᏅ, ᎤᏄᎪᏤᎢ.
020126.english: And when the unclean spirit had torn him, and cried with a loud voice, he came out of him.
020126.phonetic: Ga-da-ha-no a-da-nv-do u-lv-de-s-ta-nv, a-le a-s-da-ya u-we-lu-nv, u-nu-go-tse-i.

020127.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎨᏒ ᏚᎾᏓᏛᏛᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᎦᏙ ᎤᏰᎸᏗ ᎯᎠ?  ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏤ ᏗᏕᏲᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ?  ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛᏰᏃ ᎤᎲ ᎬᏗ ᏕᎧᏁᏤᎰ ᎠᏂᎦᏓᎭ ᏗᏓᏅᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏬᎯᏳᎲᏍᎪᎢ.
020127.english: And they were all amazed, insomuch that they questioned among themselves, saying, What thing is this? what new doctrine is this? for with authority commandeth he even the unclean spirits, and they do obey him.
020127.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i; a-le u-nv-sv ge-sv du-na-da-dv-dv-ne-i, hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i; Ga-do u-ye-lv-di hi-a?  Ga-do u-s-di hi-a i-tse di-de-yo-di ge-sv-i?  U-li-ni-gi-dv-ye-no u-hv gv-di de-ka-ne-tse-ho a-ni-ga-da-ha di-da-nv-do, a-le gv-wo-hi-yu-hv-s-go-i.

020128.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎦᏃᏣᎸ ᏚᏰᎵᏎ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎾᎿᎭᎬᏩᏚᏫᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020128.english: And immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about Galilee.
020128.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv na-s-gi de-ga-no-tsa-lv du-ye-li-se Ge-li-li na-hna gv-wa-du-wi-dv ge-sv-i.

020129.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏧᏂᏄᎪᏨ, ᏌᏩᏂ ᎠᎴ ᎡᏂᏗ ᎠᏂᏁᎸ ᏭᏂᏴᎴᎢ, ᎠᏁᎮ ᏥᎻ ᎠᎴ ᏣᏂ.
020129.english: And forthwith, when they were come out of the synagogue, they entered into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
020129.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi tsu-ni-nu-go-tsv, Sa-wa-ni a-le E-ni-di a-ni-ne-lv wu-ni-yv-le-i, a-ne-he Tsi-mi a-le Tsa-ni.

020130.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂᏃ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ ᎤᏥ ᎦᏅᎨ ᎤᏢᎨ ᎤᏗᎴᎲᏍᎨᎢ; ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎬᏩᏃᏁᎴᎢ.
020130.english: But Simon's wife's mother lay sick of a fever, and anon they tell him of her.
020130.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni-no u-da-li-i u-tsi ga-nv-ge u-tlv-ge u-di-le-hv-s-ge-i; gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv gv-wa-no-ne-le-i.

020131.cherokee: ᎤᎷᏨᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏬᏯᏁᏒ, ᏚᎴᏔᏁᎢ; ᎤᏗᎴᎲᏍᎬᏃ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏗᏩᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏍᏕᎸᎯᏙᎴᎢ.
020131.english: And he came and took her by the hand, and lifted her up; and immediately the fever left her, and she ministered unto them.
020131.phonetic: U-lu-tsv-no a-le u-wo-ya-ne-sv, du-le-ta-ne-i; u-di-le-hv-s-gv-no gi-la-quo i-yv-dv u-di-wa-se-i, a-le du-s-de-lv-hi-do-le-i.

020132.cherokee: ᎤᏒᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏅᏙ ᏭᏕᎵᏨ, ᏕᎬᏩᏘᏃᎮᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏯᎢ;
020132.english: And at even, when the sun did set, they brought unto him all that were diseased, and them that were possessed with devils.
020132.phonetic: U-sv-no hna-quo nv-do wu-de-li-tsv, de-gv-wa-ti-no-he-le ni-ga-dv tsu-ni-tlv-gi, a-le a-ni-s-gi-na di-gv-wa-ni-ya-i;

020133.english: And all the city was gathered together at the door.

020134.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᏕᎤᏅᏩᏁ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎥᏳᎩ ᎤᏁᎯ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏚᏄᎪᏫᏎᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏩᏂᏁᎩ ᏱᏕᎵᏎᎮ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ, ᎤᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᎬᏬᎵᎬᎢ.
020134.english: And he healed many that were sick of divers diseases, and cast out many devils; and suffered not the devils to speak, because they knew him.
020134.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no de-u-nv-wa-ne tsu-ni-tlv-gi tsu-da-le-nv-dv v-yu-gi u-ne-hi; a-le u-ni-tsa-ti a-ni-s-gi-na du-nu-go-wi-se-i; a-le v-tla wa-ni-ne-gi yi-de-li-se-he a-ni-s-gi-na, u-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge gv-wo-li-gv-i.

020135.cherokee: ᏑᎾᎴᏃ, ᎠᏏ ᎪᎯᏗᏳ ᎬᏩᎩᏨᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏗᏛᎮ ᎤᏄᎪᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏭᎶᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
020135.english: And in the morning, rising up a great while before day, he went out, and departed into a solitary place, and there prayed.
020135.phonetic: Su-na-le-no, a-si go-hi-di-yu gv-wa-gi-tsv-di ge-sv u-di-dv-he u-nu-go-tse-i, a-le i-na-ge wu-lo-se-i, a-le na-hna u-da-do-li-s-ta-ne-i.

020136.english: And Simon and they that were with him followed after him.

020137.cherokee: ᏫᎬᏩᏩᏛᎲᏃ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏂᎦᏛᏉ ᏴᏫ ᎨᏣᏲᏎ.
020137.english: And when they had found him, they said unto him, All men seek for thee.
020137.phonetic: Wi-gv-wa-wa-dv-hv-no, hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i, ni-ga-dv-quo yv-wi ge-tsa-yo-se.

020138.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏗᏐᎢ ᏙᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᏫᏗᎶᎯ, ᎾᎿᎭᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᎵᏥᏙᏂᏙᎸᎭ; ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎩᎷᏥᎸ.
020138.english: And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.
020138.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, Di-so-i do-di-ga-du-hv wi-di-lo-hi, na-hna na-s-quo ga-li-tsi-do-ni-do-lv-ha; na-s-gi-ye-no i-yu-s-di a-gi-lu-tsi-lv.

020139.cherokee: ᎠᎵᏥᏙᏂᏙᎮᏃ ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎨᎵᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏕᎦᏄᎪᏫᏍᎨᎢ.
020139.english: And he preached in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and cast out devils.
020139.phonetic: A-li-tsi-do-ni-do-he-no tsu-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi ni-gv-na-dv Ge-li-li, a-le a-ni-s-gi-na de-ga-nu-go-wi-s-ge-i.

020140.cherokee: ᎠᏓᏰᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏢᎩ ᎤᎷᏤᎴᎢ, ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᏚᎵᏂᏆᏅᏁᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᏣᏰᎸᏅ, ᏰᎵᏉ ᏱᏍᎩᏅᎦᎸ.
020140.english: And there came a leper to him, beseeching him, and kneeling down to him, and saying unto him, If thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
020140.phonetic: A-da-ye-s-gi-no u-tlv-gi u-lu-tse-le-i, u-da-do-li-s-da-ne-le du-li-ni-qua-nv-ne-le-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, I-yu-no o-si-yu yi-tsa-ye-lv-nv, ye-li-quo yi-s-gi-nv-ga-lv.

020141.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ, ᎦᏙᎵᎬ ᎤᏙᏯᏅᎯᏕᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏒᏂᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎣᏏᏳ ᏥᏰᎸᏅ; ᏣᏓᏅᎦᎸᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
020141.english: And Jesus, moved with compassion, put forth his hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, I will; be thou clean.
020141.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no, ga-do-li-gv u-do-ya-nv-hi-de-i, a-le u-sv-ni-le-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, O-si-yu tsi-ye-lv-nv; tsa-da-nv-ga-lv-dv ge-se-s-di.

020142.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏉᏃ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᏓᏰᏍᎩ ᎤᏢᎬ ᎤᎵᏛᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏅᎦᎸᏛ ᎨᏎᎢ.
020142.english: And as soon as he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed.
020142.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-quo-no gi-la-quo i-yv-dv a-da-ye-s-gi u-tlv-gv u-li-dv-ta-ne-i, a-le u-da-nv-ga-lv-dv ge-se-i.

020143.cherokee: ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛᏃ ᎤᏁᏤᎸ, ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏁᏤᎴ ᎤᏓᏅᏍᏗᏱ;
020143.english: And he straitly charged him, and forthwith sent him away;
020143.phonetic: U-li-ni-gi-dv-no u-ne-tse-lv, gi-la-quo i-yv-dv u-ne-tse-le u-da-nv-s-di-yi;

020144.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎮᏯᏔᎮᏍᏗ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏥᎯᏃᏁᎵ; ᎮᎾᏉᏍᎩᏂ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏫᏂᏯᏛᏂᏏ ᎠᏥᎸᎨᎶᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏩᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓ ᏣᏓᏅᎦᎸᎲ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎼᏏ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎠᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏙᎯᏳᎾᏁᏗᏱ.
020144.english: And saith unto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
020144.phonetic: A-le hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, He-ya-ta-he-s-di tle-s-di gi-lo go-hu-s-di tsi-hi-no-ne-li; he-na-quo-s-gi-ni, gv-ni-ge-sv wi-ni-ya-dv-ni-si a-tsi-lv-ge-lo-hi, a-le wa-li-s-go-lv-da tsa-da-nv-ga-lv-hv u-gv-wa-li, na-s-gi Mo-si u-ne-tsv a-li-s-go-lv-do-di-yi, na-s-gi u-na-do-hi-yu-na-ne-di-yi.

020145.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏄᎪᏨ, ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏚᏰᎵᎯᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏃᏣᎳᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏥᏌ ᎥᏝ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏫᎬᏩᏴᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᏙᏱᏗᏢᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏕᎨᏌᏗᏒ ᎡᏙᎮᎢ; ᏂᎬᎾᏛᏉᏃ ᏂᏙᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎮᎢ.
020145.english: But he went out, and began to publish it much, and to blaze abroad the matter, insomuch that Jesus could no more openly enter into the city, but was without in desert places: and they came to him from every quarter.
020145.phonetic: A-se-no u-nu-go-tsv, u-le-nv-he u-tsa-ti du-ye-li-hi-s-ta-ne-i, a-le du-no-tsa-la-ne na-s-gi, na-s-gi-no i-yu-s-di Tsi-sa v-tla hna-quo gv-ni-ge-sv wi-gv-wa-yv-s-di yi-ge-se ga-du-hv-i, do-yi-di-tlv-quo-s-gi-ni i-na-ge de-ge-sa-di-sv e-do-he-i; ni-gv-na-dv-quo-no ni-do-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi gv-wa-lu-tse-he-i.

020201.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏁᏃ ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᏫᏄᏒᎸ ᎨᏆᏂ ᏭᏴᎴᎢ; ᏚᏃᏣᎶᏤᏃ ᎦᎵᏦᏕ ᎠᏯᎥᎢ.
020201.english: And again he entered into Capernaum, after some days; and it was noised that he was in the house.
020201.phonetic: Ta-li-ne-no i-lv-s-gi wi-nu-sv-lv Ge-qua-ni wu-yv-le-i; du-no-tsa-lo-tse-no ga-li-tso-de a-ya-v-i.

020202.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏚᏂᎳᏫᏦᎴᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏜᏓᏅᏕ, ᎥᏝ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏳᎶᏗ; ᎧᏃᎮᏛᏃ ᏚᎵᏥᏙᏁᎴᎢ.
020202.english: And straightway many were gathered together, insomuch that there was no room to receive them, no, not so much as about the door: and he preached the word unto them.
020202.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv u-ni-tsa-ti du-ni-la-wi-tso-le-i, na-s-gi i-yu-s-di v-tla yu-dla-nv-de, v-tla na-s-quo ga-lo-hi-s-di-yu-lo-di; ka-no-he-dv-no du-li-tsi-do-ne-le-i.

020203.cherokee: ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏁᎮᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᎩ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎬᏩᏃᎴᎢ.
020203.english: And they come unto him, bringing one sick of the palsy, which was borne of four.
020203.phonetic: Gv-wa-lu-tse-le-no gi-lo i-yu-s-di u-lv-da-lv-v-s-gi a-ni-ne-he-i, na-s-gi nv-gi i-ya-ni-dv gv-wa-no-le-i.

020204.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏄᎸᏅᏃ ᎦᏙᎬ ᎾᎥ ᎤᏂᎷᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏂᏁᏄᎸ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎤᏅᏁᏎ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏯᎥᎢ; ᎤᏅᏁᏒᏃ, ᎡᎳᏗ ᏄᏅᏁᎴ ᎠᏤᏍᏙ ᎾᎿ ᎭᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎩ ᎦᏅᎬᎢ.
020204.english: And when they could not come nigh unto him for the press, they uncovered the roof where he was: and when they had broken it up, they let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay.
020204.phonetic: U-ni-nu-lv-nv-no ga-do-gv na-v u-ni-lu-hi-s-di-yi a-ni-ne-nu-lv i-yu-s-di, u-nv-ne-se na-hna a-ya-v-i; u-nv-ne-sv-no, e-la-di nu-nv-ne-le a-tse-s-do na-hna u-lv-da-lv-v-s-gi ga-nv-gv-i.

020205.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏒᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎩ, ᎠᏯᏥ, ᏣᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎡᏣᏙᎵᎩ.
020205.english: When Jesus saw their faith, he said unto the sick of the palsy, Son, thy sins be forgiven thee.
020205.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-go-hv u-no-hi-yu-sv-i, hi-a nu-we-se-le u-lv-da-lv-v-s-gi, A-ya-tsi, tsa-s-ga-nv-tsv e-tsa-do-li-gi.

020206.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏂᏁ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏕ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎨ ᏙᏧᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ,
020206.english: But there were certain of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
020206.phonetic: A-se-no na-hna a-ni-ne gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di di-no-we-li-s-gi, hi-a nu-s-de a-na-da-nv-te-s-ge do-tsu-na-da-nv-dv-i,

020207.cherokee: ᎦᏙᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᎭ ᎢᎠᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᎭ?  ᎦᎪ ᏰᎵ ᎠᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎬᏩᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ, ᏌᏉᏉ ᎤᏩᏒ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ?
020207.english: Why doth this man thus speak blasphemies? who can forgive sins but God only?
020207.phonetic: Ga-do-no hi-a a-s-ga-ya hi-a ni-ga-we-ha i-a-so-tlv-i-s-di-ha?  Ga-go ye-li a-s-ga-nv-tsv gv-wa-da-do-li-s-di, sa-quo-quo u-wa-sv, na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi?

020208.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏥᏌ ᏧᏓᏅᏛ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬ ᏙᏧᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎤᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏖᎭ ᏙᏗᏣᏓᏅᏛᎢ?
020208.english: And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?
020208.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv Tsi-sa tsu-da-nv-dv u-do-le-ho-sv nu-s-dv a-na-da-nv-te-s-gv do-tsu-na-da-nv-dv-i, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; Ga-do-no na-s-gi hi-a ni-u-s-di i-tsa-da-nv-te-ha do-di-tsa-da-nv-dv-i?

020209.cherokee: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎤᏟ ᎠᎯᏗᏳ?  ᏥᎪ ᎯᎠ ᏱᎾᎦᏪᏎ ᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎩ, ᏣᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎡᏣᏙᎵᎩ; ᎯᎠᎨ ᏱᎾᎦᏪᎠ, ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎾᎩ ᏣᏤᏍᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᎮᏓ?
020209.english: Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?
020209.phonetic: Ga-do u-s-di u-tli a-hi-di-yu?  Tsi-go hi-a yi-na-ga-we-se u-lv-da-lv-v-s-gi, tsa-s-ga-nv-tsv e-tsa-do-li-gi; hi-a-ge yi-na-ga-we-a, Ta-le-hv-ga, a-le hi-na-gi tsa-tse-s-do, a-le he-da?

020210.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᏣᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᎲᎢ ᎬᏩᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ (ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎩ,)
020210.english: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (he saith to the sick of the palsy,)
020210.phonetic: A-se-no i-tsa-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi yv-wi U-we-tsi u-hv-i gv-wa-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv a-s-ga-nv-tsv-i a-ni e-lo-hi (hi-a nu-we-se-le u-lv-da-lv-v-s-gi,)

020211.cherokee: ᎬᏲᏎᎭ, ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎾᎩ ᏣᏤᏍᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏤᏅᏒ ᏫᎶᎯ.
020211.english: I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.
020211.phonetic: Gv-yo-se-ha, Ta-le-hv-ga, a-le hi-na-gi tsa-tse-s-do, a-le di-tse-nv-sv wi-lo-hi.

020212.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᎴᏁᎢ, ᎤᏁᏒᏃ ᎠᏤᏍᏙ, ᎤᏄᎪᏤ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᎸᏉᏔᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎥᏝ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏲᎩᎪᎰᎢ.
020212.english: And immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went forth before them all; insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, We never saw it on this fashion.
020212.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv du-le-ne-i, u-ne-sv-no a-tse-s-do, u-nu-go-tse ni-ga-dv a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di ni-ga-dv u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi u-ni-lv-quo-ta-ne hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; v-tla i-lv-hi-yu na-s-gi i-yu-s-di yo-gi-go-ho-i.

020213.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏁᏃ ᎤᏄᎪᏤ ᎥᏓᎵ ᎤᎶᏗ ᎤᏪᏙᎴᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏫᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏪᏲᏁᎢ.
020213.english: And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the multitude resorted unto him, and he taught them.
020213.phonetic: Ta-li-ne-no u-nu-go-tse v-da-li u-lo-di u-we-do-le-i; a-le ni-ga-dv u-ni-tsa-ti wi-gv-wa-lu-tse-le-i, a-le na-s-gi du-we-yo-ne-i.

020214.cherokee: ᎠᎢᏒᏃ, ᎵᏫ ᎡᎵᏈ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᎪᎮ ᎤᏬᎴ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᎩᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏍᎩᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦ.  ᏚᎴᏅᏃ ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ.
020214.english: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the receipt of custom, and said unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.
020214.phonetic: A-i-sv-no, Li-wi E-li-qui u-we-tsi u-go-he u-wo-le a-ye-li a-de-lv a-gi-s-di-yi, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, S-gi-s-da-wa-du-ga.  Du-le-nv-no u-s-da-wa-dv-se-i.

020215.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ [ᏥᏌ] ᎦᏅᎬ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ, ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏓᏂᏅᎨ ᏥᏌ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ; ᎤᏂᏣᏖᏰᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎮᎢ.
020215.english: And it came to pass, that, as Jesus sat at meat in his house, many publicans and sinners sat also together with Jesus and his disciples: for there were many, and they followed him.
020215.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, na-s-gi [Tsi-sa] ga-nv-gv a-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv na-s-gi ga-ne-lv-i, u-ni-tsa-te a-de-lv a-ni-gi-si-do-hi a-le a-ni-s-ga-na i-tsu-la-ha da-ni-nv-ge Tsi-sa a-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi; u-ni-tsa-te-ye-no, a-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-he-i.

020216.cherokee: ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎬᏩᎪᎲ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏂᏪᏎᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏣᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏣᎾᏗᏔᏍᎦ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾᎢ.
020216.english: And when the scribes and Pharisees saw him eat with publicans and sinners, they said unto his disciples, How is it that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners?
020216.phonetic: Di-no-we-li-s-gi-no a-le A-ni-qua-li-si gv-wa-go-hv i-tsu-la-ha a-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv a-de-lv a-ni-gi-si-do-hi a-le a-ni-s-ga-na, hi-a ni-du-ni-we-se-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, Ga-do-no i-tsu-la-ha tsa-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-ga a-le i-tsu-la-ha tsa-na-di-ta-s-ga a-de-lv a-ni-gi-si-do-hi a-le a-ni-s-ga-na-i.

020217.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᏚᏂᏢᎬᎾ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏚᎸᏗ ᏱᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎰ ᏗᏓᏅᏫᏍᎩ, ᏧᏂᏢᎩᏍᎩᏂ; ᎥᏝ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᏱᏗᏥᏯᏅᎯᎸ, ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾᏍᎩᏂ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᏧᏂᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗᏱ.
020217.english: When Jesus heard it, he saith unto them, They that are whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
020217.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no na-s-gi u-dv-ga-nv hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Na-s-gi ni-du-ni-tlv-gv-na v-tla u-du-lv-di yi-nu-na-li-s-da-ne-ho di-da-nv-wi-s-gi, tsu-ni-tlv-gi-s-gi-ni; v-tla u-na-da-nv-ti yi-di-tsi-ya-nv-hi-lv, a-ni-s-ga-na-s-gi-ni du-na-da-nv-dv tsu-ni-ne-tli-yv-s-di-yi.

020218.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎬᏩᏂᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎠᏅᏍᎩ ᎨᏎᎢ; ᎤᏂᎷᏤᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᏣᏂ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎬᏩᏂᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᎹᏟ ᏣᏅᏍᎪᎢ, ᏂᎯᏃ ᎨᏣᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎾᏅᏍᎬᎾ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ?
020218.english: And the disciples of John and of the Pharisees used to fast: and they come and say unto him, Why do the disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?
020218.phonetic: Tsa-ni-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi a-le A-ni-qua-li-si gv-wa-ni-s-da-wa-di-do-hi a-ma-tli a-nv-s-gi ge-se-i; u-ni-lu-tse-no a-le hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i, Ga-do-no Tsa-ni gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, a-le A-ni-qua-li-si gv-wa-ni-s-da-wa-di-do-hi a-ma-tli tsa-nv-s-go-i, ni-hi-no ge-tsa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi a-ma-tli na-nv-s-gv-na tsi-ge-so-i?

020219.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏥᎪ ᏕᎨᎦᏨᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎯ ᏰᎵ ᎠᎹᏟ ᏯᏅᎾ ᎠᏕᏒᎲᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏗᏙᎲ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ?  ᎢᎪᎯᏛ ᎠᏕᏒᎲᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏗᏙᎲ, ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᎠᎹᏟ ᏴᎬᏅᎾ.
020219.english: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
020219.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Tsi-go de-ge-ga-tsv-s-di-s-gv a-ni-ga-ta-hi ye-li a-ma-tli ya-nv-na a-de-sv-hv-s-gi u-ne-la-di-do-hv i-go-hi-dv?  I-go-hi-dv a-de-sv-hv-s-gi u-ne-la-di-do-hv, v-tla ye-li a-ma-tli yv-gv-nv-na.

020220.cherokee: ᏛᏍᏆᎸᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎨᏥᏯᏅᎡᏗᏱ ᎠᏕᏒᎲᏍᎩ, ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᎹᏟ ᏛᏅᏂ.
020220.english: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
020220.phonetic: Dv-s-qua-lv-hi-s-gi-ni na-hi-yu ge-tsi-ya-nv-e-di-yi a-de-sv-hv-s-gi, na-hi-yu-no hna-quo a-ma-tli dv-nv-ni.

020221.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏪᏘ ᎠᏄᏬ ᎢᏤ ᎠᎬᎭᎸᏛ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏢᏗᏍᎪᎢ, ᎢᏤᏰᏃ ᎠᎬᎭᎸᏛ ᎦᎳᏍᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᏴᎦᏣᎦᎸᏙᏓ ᎤᏪᏘ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏓᏣᏕᎸᎲ ᎤᏟ ᎡᏉᎯᏳ ᏱᏅᎦᎵᏍᏓ.
020221.english: No man also seweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old, and the rent is made worse.
020221.phonetic: V-tla a-le gi-lo u-we-ti a-nu-wo i-tse a-gv-ha-lv-dv yi-ga-li-s-tlv-di-s-go-i, i-tse-ye-no a-gv-ha-lv-dv ga-la-s-tlv-ta-nv-hi yv-ga-tsa-ga-lv-do-da u-we-ti, na-s-gi-no u-da-tsa-de-lv-hv u-tli e-quo-hi-yu yi-nv-ga-li-s-da.

020222.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏤ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᏧᏪᏘ ᏗᏑᏢᏛ ᏱᏓᏟᏍᏗᏍᎪᎢ; ᎢᏤᏰᏃ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏕᏔᏍᏗ ᏱᏙᎦᏣᎦᎸ ᏗᏑᏢᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᏯᏤᏬᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏑᏢᏛ ᏱᏓᏲᎩ; ᎢᏤᏍᎩᏂ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ, ᏗᏤ ᏗᏑᏢᏛ ᏗᏟᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏐᎢ.
020222.english: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine doth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will be marred: but new wine must be put into new bottles.
020222.phonetic: V-tla a-le gi-lo i-tse gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di tsu-we-ti di-su-tlv-dv yi-da-tli-s-di-s-go-i; i-tse-ye-no gi-ga-ge--a-de-ta-s-di yi-do-ga-tsa-ga-lv di-su-tlv-dv, a-le gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di ya-tse-wo-gi, a-le di-su-tlv-dv yi-da-yo-gi; i-tse-s-gi-ni gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di, di-tse di-su-tlv-dv di-tli-s-do-di ge-so-i.

020223.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏠᎨᏏ ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ ᏓᏫᏒ ᎤᎶᏎ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ; ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎤᎾᎴᏅᎮ ᎠᎾᎢᏒ ᎤᏂᏍᎫᏕᏍᏔᏅᏎ ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ.
020223.english: And it came to pass, that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day; and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of corn.
020223.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, na-s-gi tlo-ge-si u-tsa-le-s-di da-wi-sv u-lo-se u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga; gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no u-na-le-nv-he a-na-i-sv u-ni-s-gu-de-s-ta-nv-se u-tsa-le-s-di.

020224.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ ᏂᏚᏳᎪᏛᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎭ.
020224.english: And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?
020224.phonetic: A-ni-qua-li-si-no hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i, Gv-ni-yu-quo, ga-do-no u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga ni-du-yu-go-dv-na ge-sv na-na-dv-ne-ha.

020225.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏝᏍᎪ ᏱᏥᎪᎵᏰᎣ ᏕᏫ ᏄᏛᏁᎸ ᏧᏂᎬᏎᎮ ᎢᏳ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎪᏄ ᏧᏲᏏᏍᎨᎢ, ᎤᏩᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏁᎮᎢ?
020225.english: And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
020225.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, tla-s-go yi-tsi-go-li-ye-o De-wi nu-dv-ne-lv tsu-ni-gv-se-he i-yu, a-le a-go-nu tsu-yo-si-s-ge-i, u-wa-sv, a-le na-s-gi tsa-ne-he-i?

020226.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᏧᏴᎴ ᎾᎯᏳ ᏤᎮ ᎡᏆᏱᏓ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎨ ᎠᎦᏙᏗ ᎦᏚ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᎩᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏥᎨᏎ ᎠᏥᎸᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎤᏅᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏥᏚᏁᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏁᎯ.
020226.english: How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?
020226.phonetic: Na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li a-da-ne-lv tsu-yv-le na-hi-yu tse-he E-qua-yi-da nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi, a-le tsu-ge a-ga-do-di ga-du, na-s-gi gv-gi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na tsi-ge-se a-tsi-lv-a-ne-lo-hi u-nv-sv, a-le na-s-quo tsi-du-ne-le na-s-gi a-ne-hi.

020227.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎤᏙᏢᏁ ᏴᏫ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᎢ; ᎥᏝᏃ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᏱᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᏴᏫ ᏱᎨᎪᏢᏁᎢ.
020227.english: And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:
020227.phonetic: A-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv u-do-tlv-ne yv-wi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-i; v-tla-no u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv yi-nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge yv-wi yi-ge-go-tlv-ne-i.

020228.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ.
020228.english: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.
020228.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di na-s-quo yv-wi U-we-tsi u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv U-gv-wi-yu-hi.

020301.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏁᏃ ᏭᏴᎴ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ; ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎡᏙᎮ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ ᎤᏩᎢᏎᎸᎯ.
020301.english: And he entered again into the synagogue; and there was a man there which had a withered hand.
020301.phonetic: Ta-li-ne-no wu-yv-le di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi; na-hna-no e-do-he a-s-ga-ya u-wo-ye-ni u-wa-i-se-lv-hi.

020302.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏯᏫᏎᏃ ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎨ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎬᏩᏓᏅᏬᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏰᎢᎵᏓᏍᏗᏱ.
020302.english: And they watched him, whether he would heal him on the sabbath day; that they might accuse him.
020302.phonetic: Gv-wa-ya-wi-se-no u-na-du-li-s-ge u-na-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi gv-wa-da-nv-wo-di ge-sv u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga, na-s-gi gv-wa-ye-i-li-da-s-di-yi.

020303.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ ᎤᏩᎢᏎᎸᎯ; ᎠᏰᎵ ᎭᎴᎲᎦ.
020303.english: And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.
020303.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-le a-s-ga-ya u-wo-ye-ni u-wa-i-se-lv-hi; A-ye-li ha-le-hv-ga.

020304.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏚᏳᎪᏗᏍᎪ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ, ᎤᏲᎨ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ?  ᎬᏅ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏍᏕᎸᎡᏗᏱ, ᎠᎯᏍᏗᏱᎨ?  ᎡᎳᏪᏉᏃ ᎤᏅᏎᎢ.
020304.english: And he saith unto them, Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? But they held their peace.
020304.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, Du-yu-go-di-s-go o-s-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga, u-yo-ge di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi?  Gv-nv gi-lo a-s-de-lv-e-di-yi, a-hi-s-di-yi-ge?  E-la-we-quo-no u-nv-se-i.

020305.cherokee: ᎤᏐᏅᏤᎯᏃ ᏕᎤᎧᎿᎭᏅ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏚᏫᏛ ᏄᎾᏛᏅ, ᎤᏕᏯᏔᏁᎲ ᏗᏍᏓᏱᏳ ᎨᏒ ᏧᏂᎾᏫ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ; ᎭᏙᏯᏅᎯᏛ.  ᎤᏙᏯᏅᎯᏕᏃ, ᎤᏬᏰᏂᏃ ᎬᏩᏃᏍᏛ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏐᎢ ᏄᏍᏛᎢ.
020305.english: And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
020305.phonetic: U-so-nv-tse-hi-no de-u-ka-hna-nv na-s-gi gv-wa-du-wi-dv nu-na-dv-nv, u-de-ya-ta-ne-hv di-s-da-yi-yu ge-sv tsu-ni-na-wi, hi-a nu-we-se-le a-s-ga-ya; Ha-do-ya-nv-hi-dv.  U-do-ya-nv-hi-de-no, u-wo-ye-ni-no gv-wa-no-s-dv nu-li-s-ta-ne na-s-gi-ya so-i nu-s-dv-i.

020306.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏᏃ ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏨ, ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏭᎾᎵᏃᎮᎴ ᎡᎶᏛ ᎬᏩᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏡᏗᏍᎬ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏅᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
020306.english: And the Pharisees went forth, and straightway took counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
020306.phonetic: A-ni-qua-li-si-no u-ni-nu-go-tsv, gi-la-quo i-yv-dv wu-na-li-no-he-le E-lo-dv gv-wa-s-de-li-s-gi gv-wa-tlu-di-s-gv, na-s-gi i-yu-nv-ne-di-yi u-ni-hi-s-di-yi.

020307.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏓᏅᏎ ᏚᏘᏅᏎ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎥᏓᎵ ᏭᏂᎶᏎᎢ; ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ, ᏅᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ ᎨᎵᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏗᏱ,
020307.english: But Jesus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judaea,
020307.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-da-nv-se du-ti-nv-se gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi v-da-li wu-ni-lo-se-i; u-ni-tsa-ti-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-se-i, nv-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi Ge-li-li, a-le Tsu-di-yi,

020308.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏙᎻᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏦᏓᏂ ᎢᏍᎪᏂᏗᏢ; ᎠᎴ ᏓᏯ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏙᏂ ᎾᎥ ᏗᏁᎯ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᎠᏂᎪᏗᏳ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏕᎤᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸ, ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴᎢ.
020308.english: And from Jerusalem, and from Idumaea, and from beyond Jordan; and they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they had heard what great things he did, came unto him.
020308.phonetic: A-le Tsi-lu-si-li-mi, a-le I-do-mi-yi, a-le Tso-da-ni i-s-go-ni-di-tlv; a-le Da-ya a-le Sa-do-ni na-v di-ne-hi, u-tsa-ti a-ni-go-di-yu, na-s-gi u-na-dv-ga-nv ni-ga-v u-s-qua-ni-go-di de-u-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv, gv-wa-lu-tse-le-i.

020309.cherokee: ᏚᏁᏤᎴᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏳ ᎤᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗᏱ, ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏁᏄᎳᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
020309.english: And he spake to his disciples, that a small ship should wait on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.
020309.phonetic: Du-ne-tse-le-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, na-s-gi tsi-yu u-ga-ti-di-s-di-yi, u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv i-yu-s-di, na-s-gi gv-wa-ne-nu-la-s-do-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na.

020310.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏖᏰᏃ ᏚᏅᏩᏁᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎬᏩᏁᏄᎳᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᎬᏩᏃᏟᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎨᎢ, ᏂᎦᏛ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏗᎾᎵᏍᏗᏍᎩ.
020310.english: For he had healed many; insomuch that they pressed upon him for to touch him, as many as had plagues.
020310.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-te-ye-no du-nv-wa-ne-i; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di gv-wa-ne-nu-la-s-di-s-ge gv-wa-no-tli-s-do-di-yi u-na-du-li-s-ge-i, ni-ga-dv go-hu-s-di di-na-li-s-di-s-gi.

020311.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᏓᎭ ᏗᏓᏅᏙ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎬᏩᎪᏩᏛ, ᎡᎳᏗ ᎬᏩᏓᏅᏁᎮ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏁᎷᎲᏍᎨ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏂᎯ.
020311.english: And unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.
020311.phonetic: A-le di-ga-da-ha di-da-nv-do, i-yu-no gv-wa-go-wa-dv, e-la-di gv-wa-da-nv-ne-he, a-le a-ne-lu-hv-s-ge, hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i, U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi ni-hi.

020312.cherokee: ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛᏃ ᏚᏁᏤᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᎬᏩᏁᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
020312.english: And he straitly charged them that they should not make him known.
020312.phonetic: U-li-ni-gi-dv-no du-ne-tse-le na-s-gi gv-ni-ge-sv i-gv-wa-ne-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na.

020313.cherokee: ᎤᎿᎭᎷᏎᏃ ᎣᏓᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏯᏅᎮ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᏧᏯᏅᏗᏱ; ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎮᎢ.
020313.english: And he goeth up into a mountain, and calleth unto him whom he would: and they came unto him.
020313.phonetic: U-hna-lu-se-no o-da-lv-i, a-le du-ya-nv-he na-s-gi u-du-li-s-gv tsu-ya-nv-di-yi; a-le gv-wa-lu-tse-he-i.

020314.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏚᏑᏰᏎ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏓᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᎵᏥᏙᏂᏓᏍᏗᏱ,
020314.english: And he ordained twelve, that they should be with him, and that he might send them forth to preach,
020314.phonetic: A-le du-su-ye-se ta-la-du i-ya-ni-dv, na-s-gi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-da-s-di-yi, a-le na-s-gi tsu-nv-s-di-yi u-na-li-tsi-do-ni-da-s-di-yi,

020315.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏧᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᏚᏂᏢᎬ ᏧᏂᏅᏬᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏧᏂᏄᎪᏫᏍᏗᏱ;
020315.english: And to have power to heal sicknesses, and to cast out devils:
020315.phonetic: A-le tsu-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-di-yi du-ni-tlv-gv tsu-ni-nv-wo-di-yi, a-le a-ni-s-gi-na tsu-ni-nu-go-wi-s-di-yi;

020317.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏥᎻ ᏤᏈᏗ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏂ ᏥᎻ ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ; ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏉᎠᎾᏥ ᏚᏬᎡᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴᏓᏆᎶᏍᎩ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎦᏛᎦ;
020317.english: And James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he surnamed them Boanerges, which is, The sons of thunder:
020317.phonetic: A-le Tsi-mi Tse-qui-di u-we-tsi, a-le Tsa-ni Tsi-mi di-na-da-nv-tli; a-le na-s-gi Quo-a-na-tsi du-wo-e-i, na-s-gi a-yv-da-qua-lo-s-gi tsu-we-tsi ga-dv-ga;

020318.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎡᏂᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏈᎵᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏆᏙᎳᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᎹᏚ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᎻ ᎡᎵᏈ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏗᏯ, ᎠᎴ ᏌᏩᏂ ᎠᎨᎾᏂᏗ,
020318.english: And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Canaanite,
020318.phonetic: A-le E-ni-di, a-le Qui-li-gi, a-le Qua-do-la-mi, a-le Ma-du, a-le Da-mi, a-le Tsi-mi E-li-qui u-we-tsi, a-le Da-di-ya, a-le Sa-wa-ni A-ge-na-ni-di,

020319.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓᏏ ᎢᏍᎦᎳᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏍᏉ ᏧᎶᏄᎮᎴᎢ; ᎠᏓᏁᎸᏃ ᏭᏂᎶᏎᎢ.
020319.english: And Judas Iscariot, which also betrayed him: and they went into an house.
020319.phonetic: A-le Tsu-da-si I-s-ga-la-di, na-s-gi s-quo tsu-lo-nu-he-le-i; a-da-ne-lv-no wu-ni-lo-se-i.

020320.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏚᏂᎳᏫᏦᎴᎢ, ᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏂᎤᏟᏍᏕ ᎦᏚ ᎤᏂᎩᏍᏗᏱ.
020320.english: And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
020320.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no ta-li-ne du-ni-la-wi-tso-le-i, s-gi i-yu-s-di na-s-quo ni-u-tli-s-de ga-du u-ni-gi-s-di-yi.

020321.cherokee: ᎪᎱᏍᏗᏃ ᏧᏩᏅ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ, ᎤᏁᏅᏎ ᎬᏩᏂᏴᎲᏎᎢ; ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ, ᎤᎸᏃᏘᎭ.
020321.english: And when his friends heard of it, they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is beside himself.
020321.phonetic: Go-hu-s-di-no tsu-wa-nv u-na-dv-ga-nv, u-ne-nv-se gv-wa-ni-yv-hv-se-i; hi-a-ye-no na-ni-we-s-ge-i, u-lv-no-ti-ha.

020322.cherokee: ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩᏃ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏅᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ, ᏇᎵᏥᏆ ᎤᏪᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᎬᏗᎭ ᏕᎦᏄᎪᏫᏍᎦ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ.
020322.english: And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils.
020322.phonetic: Di-no-we-li-s-gi-no Tsi-lu-si-li-mi nv-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi, hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i, Que-li-tsi-qua u-we-ha, a-le u-gv-wi-yu-hi a-ni-s-gi-na u-na-tse-li-ga gv-di-ha de-ga-nu-go-wi-s-ga a-ni-s-gi-na.

020323.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎲ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴ ᏚᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᏎᏓᏂ ᏱᎦᏄᎪᏩ ᏎᏓᏂ?
020323.english: And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?
020323.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no wi-du-ya-nv-hv, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le du-tli-lo-s-da-ne-le-i, Ga-do yi-ga-li-s-do-da Se-da-ni yi-ga-nu-go-wa Se-da-ni?

020324.cherokee: ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎪᏢᏒ ᏔᎵ ᏱᏄᏓᎠ, ᎤᏩᏒᏉ ᏯᏓᏡᏗᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᏱᏂᎬᏩᏍᏗᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎪᏢᏒᎢ.
020324.english: And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
020324.phonetic: I-yu a-le a-ye-li go-tlv-sv ta-li yi-nu-da-a, u-wa-sv-quo ya-da-tlu-di-ha, v-tla ye-li yi-ni-gv-wa-s-di-quo na-s-gi a-ye-li go-tlv-sv-i.

020325.cherokee: ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᏏᏓᏁᎸ ᎨᏒ ᏔᎵ ᏱᏄᏓᎠ, ᎤᏩᏒᏉ ᏯᏓᏡᏗᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏏᏓᏁᎸ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᏱᏂᎬᏩᏍᏗᏉ.
020325.english: And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
020325.phonetic: I-yu a-le si-da-ne-lv ge-sv ta-li yi-nu-da-a, u-wa-sv-quo ya-da-tlu-di-ha, na-s-gi si-da-ne-lv ge-sv v-tla ye-li yi-ni-gv-wa-s-di-quo.

020326.cherokee: ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᏎᏓᏂ ᏱᏚᎴᏅ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏱᏚᏓᎦᏘᎴᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵ ᏱᏄᏓᎠ, ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᏱᏂᎬᏩᏍᏗᏉ, ᏱᏩᏍᏔᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎡᎲᎢ.
020326.english: And if Satan rise up against himself, and be divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.
020326.phonetic: I-yu a-le Se-da-ni yi-du-le-nv u-wa-sv yi-du-da-ga-ti-le-nv, a-le ta-li yi-nu-da-a, v-tla ye-li yi-ni-gv-wa-s-di-quo, yi-wa-s-ta-quo-s-gi-ni e-hv-i.

020327.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎦᏁᎸ ᏴᎬᏴᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏴᎬᏬᏅ ᎤᎿᎭᎥᎢ, ᎬᏂ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏳᎸᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ; ᎩᎳ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏯᏬᏅ ᎦᏁᎸ ᎤᎿᎭᎥᎢ.
020327.english: No man can enter into a strong man's house, and spoil his goods, except he will first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.
020327.phonetic: V-tla gi-lo u-li-ni-gi-dv a-s-ga-ya ga-ne-lv yv-gv-yv-ha, a-le yv-gv-wo-nv u-hna-v-i, gv-ni i-gv-yi yu-lv-lv na-s-gi u-li-ni-gi-dv a-s-ga-ya; gi-la hna-quo ya-wo-nv ga-ne-lv u-hna-v-i.

020328.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎢᏨᏲᏎᎭ, ᏂᎦᎥ ᎠᏍᎦᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ, ᎦᎨᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗᏉ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏴᏫ ᏧᏁᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏐᏢᎢᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ, ᏂᎦᎥ ᎬᏩᏂᏐᏢᎢᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ;
020328.english: Verily I say unto you, All sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:
020328.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya i-tsv-yo-se-ha, Ni-ga-v a-s-ga-nv-i-s-di ge-sv, ga-ge-tsi-do-li-s-di-quo ge-se-s-di yv-wi tsu-ne-tsi, a-le a-so-tlv-i-s-do-di ge-sv, ni-ga-v gv-wa-ni-so-tlv-i-s-do-di ge-sv-i;

020329.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎠᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎦᏰᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏗᎦᏰᎫᎪᏓᏁᏗᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏫᎾᏍᏛᎾ ᎤᏍᏛᏗᏍᏗᏱ;
020329.english: But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost hath never forgiveness, but is in danger of eternal damnation:
020329.phonetic: Gi-lo-s-gi-ni Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do a-so-tlv-i-s-di-s-ge-s-di, v-tla i-lv-hi-yu ga-ye-tsi-do-li-s-di yi-ge-se-s-di, di-ga-ye-gu-go-da-ne-di-quo-s-gi-ni wi-na-s-dv-na u-s-dv-di-s-di-yi;

020330.english: Because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.

020331.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᏱᏗᏢ ᏓᏂᏙᎨ, ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏎ ᏫᎬᏩᏯᏂᏍᎨᎢ.
020331.english: There came then his brethren and his mother, and, standing without, sent unto him, calling him.
020331.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gv-wa-lu-tse-le a-na-li-nv-tli, a-le u-tsi, a-le do-yi-di-tlv da-ni-do-ge, u-na-da-nv-se wi-gv-wa-ya-ni-s-ge-i.

020332.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎠᏂᏁ ᎬᏩᏓᏡᏫᏍᏕᎢ; ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ, ᏣᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᎵᏅᏟ ᏙᏱᏗᏢ ᏗᎨᏣᏲᎭ.
020332.english: And the multitude sat about him, and they said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee.
020332.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no a-ni-ne gv-wa-da-tlu-wi-s-de-i; hi-a-no ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i, Gv-ni-yu-quo, tsa-tsi a-le i-tsa-li-nv-tli do-yi-di-tlv di-ge-tsa-yo-ha.

020333.cherokee: ᏚᏁᏤᎴᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎦᎪ ᎡᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏣᎵᏅᏟ?
020333.english: And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?
020333.phonetic: Du-ne-tse-le-no hi-a nu-we-se-i, Ga-go e-tsi, a-le o-tsa-li-nv-tli?

020334.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏚᏫᏛᏃ ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏂᏙᎸ ᎤᏬᎸ ᎾᎥ ᎠᏂᏂ ᏚᎧᎾᏅ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎡᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏣᎵᏅᏟ?
020334.english: And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
020334.phonetic: Gv-wa-du-wi-dv-no du-ka-hna-ni-do-lv u-wo-lv na-v a-ni-ni du-ka-na-nv hi-a nu-we-se-i; Gv-ni-yu-quo e-tsi, a-le o-tsa-li-nv-tli?

020335.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᎾᏛᏁᎮᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏦᏍᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᎩᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᏥ.
020335.english: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.
020335.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no U-ne-la-nv-hi u-du-li-s-gv na-dv-ne-he-s-di, na-s-gi tso-s-da-da-nv-tli, a-le v-gi-do, a-le e-tsi.

020401.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏁᏃ ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᏚᏕᏲᏁ ᎥᏓᎷᎶᏗ; ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎬᏩᏓᏡᏫᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᏣᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏓᎵ ᎠᏔᎸ ᎤᏪᏁᎢ; ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏓᎷᎶᏗ ᏙᏱ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎡᎢ.
020401.english: And he began again to teach by the sea side: and there was gathered unto him a great multitude, so that he entered into a ship, and sat in the sea; and the whole multitude was by the sea on the land.
020401.phonetic: Ta-li-ne-no u-le-nv-he du-de-yo-ne v-da-lu-lo-di; u-ni-tsa-ti-no gv-wa-da-tlu-wi-s-ta-ne-i, na-s-gi i-yu-s-di tsi-yu-hi u-tsa-ne-i, a-le v-da-li a-ta-lv u-we-ne-i; ni-ga-dv-no u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv v-da-lu-lo-di do-yi ge-sv a-ni-do-na-e-i.

020402.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏖ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏚᏪᏲᏁ ᏚᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴ ᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬᎢ,
020402.english: And he taught them many things by parables, and said unto them in his doctrine,
020402.phonetic: A-le u-tsa-te tsu-da-le-nv-dv du-we-yo-ne du-tli-lo-s-da-ne-le-i, a-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-le da-de-yo-hv-s-gv-i,

020403.english: Hearken; Behold, there went out a sower to sow:

020404.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎠᏫᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᏛ ᏅᏃᎱᎶᏗ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ, ᏥᏍᏆᏃ ᎦᎸᎶᎢ ᎠᏂᏃᎯᎢᏙᎯ ᎤᏂᎷᏤ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎪᏁᎢ.
020404.english: And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.
020404.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne a-wi-s-gv-i, na-s-gi i-ga-dv nv-no-hu-lo-di u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i, tsi-s-qua-no ga-lv-lo-i a-ni-no-hi-i-do-hi u-ni-lu-tse a-le u-ni-go-ne-i.

020405.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏅᏲᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏲᎵᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎦᏓ; ᎠᎴ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏗᎤᎵᏰᏁᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᏌᎨᎢᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎦᏓ;
020405.english: And some fell on stony ground, where it had not much earth; and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of earth:
020405.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no nv-yo-hi ge-sv u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i, na-hna ga-yo-li-yu ge-sv ga-da; a-le gi-la-quo i-yv-dv di-u-li-ye-ne-i, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge sa-ge-i-yu ge-sv ga-da;

020406.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏅᏙ ᎢᎦ-ᎡᎯ ᏧᎧᎸᏨ ᎤᎴᏴᏎᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏂᏚᎿᎭᏍᏕᏢᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎤᎿᎭᏍᎬᏤᏉ.
020406.english: But when the sun was up, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
020406.phonetic: A-se-no nv-do i-ga--e-hi tsu-ka-lv-tsv u-le-yv-se-i; a-le ni-du-hna-s-de-tlv-na ge-sv i-yu-s-di, u-hna-s-gv-tse-quo.

020407.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎠᏄᎦᎸᎯ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ; ᎠᏄᎦᎸᏃ ᏧᏛᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏄᎳᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᎾᏄᎪᏫᏒᎾ ᎨᏎ ᎤᎦᏔ.
020407.english: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
020407.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no a-nu-ga-lv-hi u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i; a-nu-ga-lv-no tsu-dv-se-i, a-le u-ne-nu-la-s-ta-ne-i, a-le nu-na-nu-go-wi-sv-na ge-se u-ga-ta.

020408.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎣᏒ ᎦᏙᎯ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏰᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏛᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎧᏁᏉᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎦᏔᏔᏁᎢ, ᎢᎦᏛ ᏦᎠᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᏳᏩᎫᏗ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏑᏓᎵᏍᎪᎯ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ.
020408.english: And other fell on good ground, and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased; and brought forth, some thirty, and some sixty, and some an hundred.
020408.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no o-sv ga-do-hi u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i, a-le u-li-ye-ne-i, a-le a-dv-s-ge-i, a-le ka-ne-quo-ge-i, a-le u-ga-ta-ta-ne-i, i-ga-dv tso-a-s-go-hi i-yu-wa-gu-di, i-ga-dv-no su-da-li-s-go-hi, i-ga-dv-no a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui.

020409.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎩᎶ ᏕᎦᎵᎷᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏛᎪᏗᏱ, ᏩᏛᎬᎦ.
020409.english: And he said unto them, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
020409.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, gi-lo de-ga-li-lu-ge-s-di u-dv-go-di-yi, wa-dv-gv-ga.

020410.cherokee: ᎤᏩᏒᏃ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏕᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎬᏩᏛᏛᏁ ᎦᏛᎬ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛᎢ.
020410.english: And when he was alone, they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parable.
020410.phonetic: U-wa-sv-no ge-se-i, na-s-gi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-de-gi a-le ta-la-du i-ya-ni-dv gv-wa-dv-dv-ne ga-dv-gv na-s-gi da-tli-lo-s-dv-i.

020411.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ.  ᏂᎯ ᎡᏥᏁᎸ ᎢᏦᎵᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ; Ꮎ-ᏍᎩᏂ ᏙᏱᏗᏢ ᎠᏁᏙᎯ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏕᎨᎦᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎰᎢ;
020411.english: And he said unto them, Unto you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all these things are done in parables:
020411.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i.  Ni-hi e-tsi-ne-lv i-tso-li-s-di-yi u-de-li-dv ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i; na--s-gi-ni do-yi-di-tlv a-ne-do-hi hi-a na-s-gi ni-ga-dv de-ge-ga-tli-lo-s-da-ne-ho-i;

020412.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᎪᏩᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏂᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎾᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ; ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᏍᎬ ᎤᎾᏛᎪᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏃᎵᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎾᏓᏁᏟᏴᎡᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎦᎨᏥᏁᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
020412.english: That seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest at any time they should be converted, and their sins should be forgiven them.
020412.phonetic: Na-s-gi a-ni-go-wa-di-s-gv u-ni-go-wa-dv-di-yi, a-le gv-wa-na-do-le-ho-hi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi; a-le a-na-dv-gi-s-gv u-na-dv-go-di-yi, a-le gv-wa-no-li-s-di ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi; na-s-gi tsu-na-da-ne-tli-yv-e-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na du-na-da-nv-dv, a-le u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv ga-ge-tsi-ne-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na.

020413.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏝᏍᎪ ᏱᏦᎵᎦ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛᎢ?  ᎦᏙᏃ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᏱᏕᏦᎵᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛᎢ?
020413.english: And he said unto them, Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?
020413.phonetic: A-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Tla-s-go yi-tso-li-ga hi-a na-s-gi da-tli-lo-s-dv-i?  Ga-do-no yi-ga-li-s-do-da yi-de-tso-li-gi ni-ga-dv da-tli-lo-s-dv-i?

020415.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏃᎱᎶᏗ ᎾᎿᎭᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏥᎨᏥᏫᏎᎴᎢ; ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᎾᏛᎬᎦ, ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎦᎷᎪ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎩᏍᎪ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏧᏂᎾᏫᏱ ᎠᏫᏒᎯ.
020415.english: And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, Satan cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.
020415.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-s-gi nv-no-hu-lo-di na-hna ka-no-he-dv tsi-ge-tsi-wi-se-le-i; hna-quo-no a-na-dv-gv-ga, A-s-gi-na ga-lu-go gi-la-quo i-yv-dv, a-le a-gi-s-go ka-no-he-dv tsu-ni-na-wi-yi a-wi-sv-hi.

020416.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏅᏲᎯ ᏥᎨᏥᏫᏎᎴᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎠᎾᏛᎬᎦ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᎮᎵᏨᎯ ᏓᎾᏓᏂᎸᎪᎢ;
020416.english: And these are they likewise which are sown on stony ground; who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with gladness;
020416.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-s-gi na-s-quo nv-yo-hi tsi-ge-tsi-wi-se-le-i; na-s-gi hna-quo ka-no-he-dv a-na-dv-gv-ga gi-la-quo i-yv-dv u-na-li-he-li-tsv-hi da-na-da-ni-lv-go-i;

020417.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏚᏂᎿᎭᏍᏕᏠᎢ, ᏞᎦᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏓᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏕᎪᎢ; ᎿᎭᏉᏃ, ᎠᎩᎵᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏕᏯᏙᏗ ᎠᎵᏰᎢᎶᎦ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏛᎵᏰᎢᎸᏍᏓ, ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᏃᏕᎯᎰᎢ.
020417.english: And have no root in themselves, and so endure but for a time: afterward, when affliction or persecution ariseth for the word's sake, immediately they are offended.
020417.phonetic: A-le v-tla yi-du-ni-hna-s-de-tlo-i, tle-ga-quo-s-gi-ni da-ni-ka-hna-wa-de-go-i; hna-quo-no, a-gi-li-ya a-le u-de-ya-do-di a-li-ye-i-lo-ga ka-no-he-dv dv-li-ye-i-lv-s-da, gi-la-quo i-yv-dv du-no-de-hi-ho-i.

020418.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏄᎦᎸᎯ ᏥᎨᏥᏫᏎᎴᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏣᎾᏛᎩᏍᎪᎢ,
020418.english: And these are they which are sown among thorns; such as hear the word,
020418.phonetic: A-le hi-a a-nu-ga-lv-hi tsi-ge-tsi-wi-se-le-i; na-s-gi hi-a ka-no-he-dv tsa-na-dv-gi-s-go-i,

020419.cherokee: ᎤᏪᎵᎯᏍᏗᏃ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎡᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎨᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏓᎵᏓᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎠᏚᎸᏗ ᎨᏒ, ᎠᏂᏴᎯᎲ ᎠᏂᏁᏄᎳᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎦᏔᏛᏍᎬᎾᏉ ᎨᏐᎢ.
020419.english: And the cares of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in, choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.
020419.phonetic: U-we-li-hi-s-di-no e-lo-hi e-hi, a-le ge-nv-i-s-di ge-sv u-da-li-da-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le tsu-da-le-nv-dv go-hu-s-di a-du-lv-di ge-sv, a-ni-yv-hi-hv a-ni-ne-nu-la-s-di-s-go ka-no-he-dv, a-le na-s-gi na-ga-ta-dv-s-gv-na-quo ge-so-i.

020420.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏒ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏥᎨᏥᏫᏎᎴᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᏍᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎾᏓᏂᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᏛᏍᎩ, ᎢᎦᏛ ᏦᎠᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᏳᏩᎫᏗ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏑᏓᎳᏍᎪᎯ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ.
020420.english: And these are they which are sown on good ground; such as hear the word, and receive it, and bring forth fruit, some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some an hundred.
020420.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-s-gi o-sv ga-do-hi tsi-ge-tsi-wi-se-le-i; na-s-gi a-na-dv-gi-s-gi ka-no-he-dv, a-le di-na-da-ni-lv-gi, a-le a-ni-ga-ta-dv-s-gi, i-ga-dv tso-a-s-go-hi i-yu-wa-gu-di, i-ga-dv-no su-da-la-s-go-hi, i-ga-dv-no a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui.

020421.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎠᏨᏍᏙᏗᏍᎪ ᎠᏂᏃᎯᎰ ᎠᏟᎶᏍᏗ ᎦᎧᎲ ᎭᏫᏂᏗᏢ ᎤᏂᎪᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏂᏢᏫᏉ?  ᎥᏝᏃ ᎤᏂᎪᏗᏱ ᎠᏨᏍᏙᏗ ᎦᎪᏗᏱ?
020421.english: And he said unto them, Is a candle brought to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? and not to be set on a candlestick?
020421.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, A-tsv-s-do-di-s-go a-ni-no-hi-ho a-tli-lo-s-di ga-ka-hv ha-wi-ni-di-tlv u-ni-go-di-yi, a-le ga-ni-tlv-wi-quo?  V-tla-no u-ni-go-di-yi a-tsv-s-do-di ga-go-di-yi?

020422.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᎬᏍᎦᎳ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᎬᏁᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏱᏂᎬᏃ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏎ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
020422.english: For there is nothing hid, which shall not be manifested; neither was any thing kept secret, but that it should come abroad.
020422.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no go-hu-s-di yi-gv-s-ga-la na-s-gi gv-ni-ge-sv i-gv-ne-di ni-ge-sv-na; v-tla a-le go-hu-s-di u-de-li-dv yi-ni-gv-no, na-s-gi a-se gv-ni-ge-sv i-yu-li-s-do-di ni-ge-sv-na.

020423.english: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.

020424.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᏤᏯᏔᎮᏍᏗ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎢᏣᏛᎩᏍᎬᎢ; ᏄᏍᏛ ᎢᏣᏓᏟᎶᏒᎲ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎡᏣᎵᎾᎶᎡᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᏂᎯᏃ ᎢᏣᏛᎩᏍᎩ ᎡᏥᏁᏉᎡᏗ ᎡᏥᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
020424.english: And he said unto them, Take heed what ye hear: with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you: and unto you that hear shall more be given.
020424.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, I-tse-ya-ta-he-s-di nu-s-dv i-tsa-dv-gi-s-gv-i; nu-s-dv i-tsa-da-tli-lo-sv-hv, na-s-gi-ya e-tsa-li-na-lo-e-di ge-se-s-di; ni-hi-no i-tsa-dv-gi-s-gi e-tsi-ne-quo-e-di e-tsi-ne-di ge-se-s-di.

020425.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎤᎮᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᏄᎲᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᎩᏒᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᎲᎢ.
020425.english: For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he hath.
020425.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no u-he-s-di, na-s-gi a-tsi-ne-di ge-se-s-di; gi-lo-no nu-hv-na ge-se-s-di, na-s-gi a-tsi-gi-sv-di ge-se-s-di na-s-quo u-hv-i.

020426.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏉ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᎦᏔ ᏳᏫᏒ ᎦᏙᎯ;
020426.english: And he said, So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed into the ground;
020426.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-i, U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv na-s-gi-ya-quo gi-lo a-s-ga-ya u-ga-ta yu-wi-sv ga-do-hi;

020427.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏳᎸᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᏯᏗᏗᎭ ᏗᎬᏩᎩᏨᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎦᏔ ᏳᎵᏰᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᏳᏛᏒ, ᏄᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅ ᏄᏙᎴᎰᏒᎾ.
020427.english: And should sleep, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring and grow up, he knoweth not how.
020427.phonetic: A-le yu-lv-nv, a-le ya-di-di-ha di-gv-wa-gi-tsv-di, a-le u-ga-ta yu-li-ye-nv, a-le yu-dv-sv, nu-li-s-do-ta-nv nu-do-le-ho-sv-na.

020428.cherokee: ᎦᏙᎯᏰᏃ ᎤᏩᏒᏉ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪ ᎤᎦᏔᏔᏅᎯ; ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎦᎶᎬ, ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏍᎫᏓᏛᎢ, ᎣᏂᏃ ᎤᎧᎵᏨᎯ ᎤᎦᏔᏛ ᎤᏍᎫᏓᏛᎢ.
020428.english: For the earth bringeth forth fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, after that the full corn in the ear.
020428.phonetic: Ga-do-hi-ye-no u-wa-sv-quo ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-go u-ga-ta-ta-nv-hi; i-gv-yi-yi ge-sv u-ga-lo-gv, hna-quo-no u-s-gu-da-dv-i, o-ni-no u-ka-li-tsv-hi u-ga-ta-dv u-s-gu-da-dv-i.

020429.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᎦᏛᎾᎩ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᏍᎫᏕᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᏍᎫᏕᏍᏗᏱᏰᏃ ᎤᎵᏰᎢᎶᎶᎢ.
020429.english: But when the fruit is brought forth, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because the harvest is come.
020429.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-ga-dv-na-gi gi-la-quo i-yv-dv a-s-gu-de-s-go-i, a-s-gu-de-s-di-yi-ye-no u-li-ye-i-lo-lo-i.

020430.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎦᏙ ᏙᏓᏓᏤᎳᏍᏔᏂ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ?  ᎦᏙᎨ ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛ ᏙᏓᏓᏟᎶᏍᏔᏂ?
020430.english: And he said, Whereunto shall we liken the kingdom of God? or with what comparison shall we compare it?
020430.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-i, Ga-do do-da-da-tse-la-s-ta-ni U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i?  Ga-do-ge da-tli-lo-s-dv do-da-da-tli-lo-s-ta-ni?

020431.cherokee: ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎤᎦᏔ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏣᏫᏐᎢ, ᏥᏭᏓᎪᎾᏛᏗ ᎤᏍᏗᎩᏳ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᎦᏔ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏓᎭᏛᎢ.
020431.english: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, is less than all the seeds that be in the earth:
020431.phonetic: A-tsi-lv--u-ga-ta na-s-gi-ya-i, na-s-gi ga-do-hi tsa-wi-so-i, tsi-wu-da-go-na-dv-di u-s-di-gi-yu e-s-ga-quo ni-ga-v u-ga-ta ga-do-hi da-ha-dv-i.

020432.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏫᏒᎯ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᏗᏛᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏟ ᎡᏉᎯᏳ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏰᎿᎭᎥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏤᏉᎯᏳ ᏓᏱᏢᏍᎪᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏥᏍᏆ ᎤᏜᏓᏅᏛ ᎠᏂᏃᎯᎵᏙᎯ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎤᏓᏩᏗᏍᏛ ᎭᏫᏂᏗᏢ ᎠᎾᎭᏗᏍᎪᎢ.
020432.english: But when it is sown, it groweth up, and becometh greater than all herbs, and shooteth out great branches; so that the fowls of the air may lodge under the shadow of it.
020432.phonetic: A-se-no a-wi-sv-hi tsi-ge-so-i, di-dv-s-go-i, a-le u-tli e-quo-hi-yu ni-ga-li-s-di-s-go e-s-ga-quo ni-ga-v u-ye-hna-v-i, a-le tse-quo-hi-yu da-yi-tlv-s-go-i; a-le tsi-s-qua u-dla-nv-dv a-ni-no-hi-li-do-hi ye-li-quo u-da-wa-di-s-dv ha-wi-ni-di-tlv a-na-ha-di-s-go-i.

020433.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏪᏎ ᎤᏣᏖ ᏚᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ, ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏚᎵᏥᏙᏁᎴᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᎾᏛᎪᏗ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
020433.english: And with many such parables spake he the word unto them, as they were able to hear it.
020433.phonetic: Na-s-gi nu-we-se u-tsa-te du-tli-lo-s-da-ne-le-i, ka-no-he-dv du-li-tsi-do-ne-le-i, na-s-gi ye-li gv-wa-na-dv-go-di ni-ga-li-s-di-s-gv-i.

020434.cherokee: ᏂᏓᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎲᎾᏍᎩᏂ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏚᏬᏁᏔᏁᎢ; ᎤᏅᏒᏃ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᏂᎦᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏬᏏᏌᏁᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ.
020434.english: But without a parable spake he not unto them: and when they were alone, he expounded all things to his disciples.
020434.phonetic: Ni-da-tli-lo-s-da-ne-hv-na-s-gi-ni v-tla yi-du-wo-ne-ta-ne-i; u-nv-sv-no ge-se-i, ni-ga-dv na-s-gi du-wo-si-sa-ne-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi.

020435.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏉᏃ ᎢᎦ, ᎤᏒ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏗᏗᏐᎩ ᏍᎪᎾ ᏫᏗᎶᎯ.
020435.english: And the same day, when the even was come, he saith unto them, Let us pass over unto the other side.
020435.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-quo-no i-ga, u-sv nu-li-s-ta-nv, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Di-di-so-gi s-go-na wi-di-lo-hi.

020436.cherokee: ᏚᏂᏰᎵᎯᏍᏔᏅᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏅᏩᏛᏁᏉ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᏣᎥ ᎬᏩᏘᎾᏫᏛᎮᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏂᏗᎬᏩᏓᎴ ᏧᏍᏗ ᏥᏳ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᏩᏗᏎᎢ.
020436.english: And when they had sent away the multitude, they took him even as he was in the ship. And there were also with him other little ships.
020436.phonetic: Du-ni-ye-li-hi-s-ta-nv-no u-ni-tsa-ti nv-wa-dv-ne-quo tsi-yu-hi u-tsa-v gv-wa-ti-na-wi-dv-he-i; a-le na-s-quo ni-di-gv-wa-da-le tsu-s-di tsi-yu u-na-li-go-wa-di-se-i.

020437.cherokee: ᎤᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᏱᎶᎴ ᎤᏃᎴ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎵᏍᏗᎳᏁᎬ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎠᏟᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᎧᎵᏬᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ.
020437.english: And there arose a great storm of wind, and the waves beat into the ship, so that it was now full.
020437.phonetic: U-tsa-ti-no u-yi-lo-le u-no-le, a-le da-li-s-di-la-ne-gv tsi-yu-hi a-tli-ge-i, a-le hna-quo a-ka-li-wo-hi ge-se-i.

020438.cherokee: ᏥᏳᎯᏃ ᎣᏂᏗᏢ ᎤᏣᎡ ᎦᎵᎮᎢ, ᎠᎫᏍᏙ ᎤᎫᏍᏓᎡᎢ; ᎬᏩᏰᏍᏔᏁᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᏝᏍᎪ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏰᎵᎭ ᏨᏓᏲᏣᏗᏒᏂ?
020438.english: And he was in the hinder part of the ship, asleep on a pillow: and they awake him, and say unto him, Master, carest thou not that we perish?
020438.phonetic: Tsi-yu-hi-no o-ni-di-tlv u-tsa-e ga-li-he-i, a-gu-s-do u-gu-s-da-e-i; gv-wa-ye-s-ta-ne-no, a-le hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i, Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, tla-s-go go-hu-s-di ye-li-ha tsv-da-yo-tsa-di-sv-ni?

020439.cherokee: ᏚᎴᏁᏃ, ᎤᏃᎴ ᎤᏍᎦᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎥᏓᎵ, ᏙᏉ; ᎤᏓᏥᎾᏍᏛᎯ ᎿᎭᎵᏍᏓ.  ᎦᏃᎸᎥᏍᎬᏃ ᎤᏑᎵᎪᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏓᏥᎾᏍᏛᎯ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
020439.english: And he arose, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, Peace, be still. And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
020439.phonetic: Du-le-ne-no, u-no-le u-s-ga-tse-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-le v-da-li, Do-quo; u-da-tsi-na-s-dv-hi hna-li-s-da.  Ga-no-lv-v-s-gv-no u-su-li-go-tse-i, a-le u-tsa-ti u-da-tsi-na-s-dv-hi nu-li-s-ta-ne-i.

020440.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᏂᏥᏍᎦᏒᎾ?  ᎦᏙ ᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᏦᎯᏳᏒᎾ ᎢᎩ?
020440.english: And he said unto them, Why are ye so fearful? how is it that ye have no faith?
020440.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, Ga-do-no ni-tsi-s-ga-sv-na?  Ga-do di-ga-li-s-do-di ni-tso-hi-yu-sv-na i-gi?

020441.cherokee: ᎤᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᎾᏓᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎾᏍᏉᏰᏃ ᎤᏃᎴ ᎠᎴ ᎥᏓᎵ ᎢᎬᏬᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ.
020441.english: And they feared exceedingly, and said one to another, What manner of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?
020441.phonetic: U-tsa-ti-no u-ni-s-ga-le-i, a-le hi-a ni-du-na-da-we-se-le-i, Ga-do u-s-di hi-a a-s-ga-ya, na-s-quo-ye-no u-no-le a-le v-da-li i-gv-wo-hi-yu-hv-s-ga.

020501.english: And they came over unto the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gadarenes.

020502.cherokee: ᏥᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᏣᎢᏒ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᏠᏎ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏓᏓᏂᏌᎲ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏯᎢ,
020502.english: And when he was come out of the ship, immediately there met him out of the tombs a man with an unclean spirit,
020502.phonetic: Tsi-yu-hi-no u-tsa-i-sv gi-la-quo i-yv-dv du-tlo-se a-s-ga-ya da-da-ni-sa-hv nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi ga-da-ha a-da-nv-do u-ya-i,

020503.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏓᏂᏌᎲᏉ ᎡᎮᎢ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏧᏓᏕᏒᏛ ᎬᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎬᏩᎸᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ.
020503.english: Who had his dwelling among the tombs; and no man could bind him, no, not with chains:
020503.phonetic: Na-s-gi da-da-ni-sa-hv-quo e-he-i; v-tla a-le na-s-quo tsu-da-de-sv-dv gv-di gi-lo gv-wa-lv-s-di yi-ge-se-i.

020504.cherokee: ᏯᏃᎩᏳᏰᏃ ᏣᏥᏅᏠᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓᏕᏒᏛ ᏣᎦᎸᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᏧᏓᏕᏒᏛᏃ ᏓᎦᎵᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᏅᏠᏍᏗ ᏓᏍᏆᎵᏍᎨᎢ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎬᏩᏓᏅᏘᏐᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ.
020504.english: Because that he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been plucked asunder by him, and the fetters broken in pieces: neither could any man tame him.
020504.phonetic: Ya-no-gi-yu-ye-no tsa-tsi-nv-tlo-lv-hi ge-se-i, a-le tsu-da-de-sv-dv tsa-ga-lv-s-ta-nv-hi ge-se-i, tsu-da-de-sv-dv-no da-ga-li-s-ge-i, a-le di-ga-nv-tlo-s-di da-s-qua-li-s-ge-i; v-tla a-le gi-lo gv-wa-da-nv-ti-so-di yi-ge-se-i.

020505.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ, ᏒᏃᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎦ, ᎦᏚᏏ ᎠᎴ ᏓᏓᏂᏌᎲᎢ, ᎤᏪᎷᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏅᏯ ᎬᏗ ᎠᏓᏰᎶᎮᎢ.
020505.english: And always, night and day, he was in the mountains, and in the tombs, crying, and cutting himself with stones.
020505.phonetic: A-le ni-go-hi-lv, sv-no-yi a-le i-ga, ga-du-si a-le da-da-ni-sa-hv-i, u-we-lu-ge-i, a-le nv-ya gv-di a-da-ye-lo-he-i.

020506.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᏅᎯᏳ ᏭᎪᎲ ᏥᏌ, ᏚᏍᏆᎸᏔᏁ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ,
020506.english: But when he saw Jesus afar off, he ran and worshipped him,
020506.phonetic: A-se-no i-nv-hi-yu wu-go-hv Tsi-sa, du-s-qua-lv-ta-ne a-le u-da-do-li-s-da-ne-le-i,

020507.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎤᏪᎷᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᏙ ᏗᎩᎾᏓᏛᏙᏗ ᏂᎯ ᏥᏌ, ᏩᏍᏛ ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏳ ᎡᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ?  ᎬᏁᏤᎭ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎬᏁᎢᏍᏓᏁᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏍᎩᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
020507.english: And cried with a loud voice, and said, What have I to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of the most high God? I adjure thee by God, that thou torment me not.
020507.phonetic: A-le a-s-da-ya u-we-lu-ne hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ga-do di-gi-na-da-dv-do-di ni-hi Tsi-sa, wa-s-dv ga-lv-la-di-yu e-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi?  Gv-ne-tse-ha, U-ne-la-nv-hi gv-ne-i-s-da-ne-ha, na-s-gi s-gi-gi-li-yo-i-s-do-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na.

020508.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎡᎯᏄᎪᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᏂᎯ ᎯᎦᏓᎭ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ.
020508.english: For he said unto him, Come out of the man, thou unclean spirit.
020508.phonetic: Hi-a-ye-no nu-we-se-le-i, E-hi-nu-go-i na-s-gi na a-s-ga-ya, ni-hi hi-ga-da-ha a-da-nv-do.

020509.cherokee: ᎤᏛᏛᏁᏃ, ᎦᏙ ᏕᏣᏙᎥ?  ᎤᏬᏎᎴᎢ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᏓᏆᏙᎥ; ᎣᎩᏣᏔᏰᏃ.
020509.english: And he asked him, What is thy name? And he answered, saying, My name is Legion: for we are many.
020509.phonetic: U-dv-dv-ne-no, Ga-do de-tsa-do-v?  U-wo-se-le-i.  Hi-a-no nu-we-se-le-i, Su-na-da-tlu-gi da-qua-do-v; o-gi-tsa-ta-ye-no.

020510.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏍᏗᏰᏔᏁ ᎾᎿᎭᎨᏒ ᏧᏄᎪᏫᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020510.english: And he besought him much that he would not send them away out of the country.
020510.phonetic: A-le u-tsa-ti u-s-di-ye-ta-ne na-hna ge-sv tsu-nu-go-wi-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i.

020511.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎣᏓᎷᎶᏗ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᏏᏆ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏂᏙᎮᎢ.
020511.english: Now there was there nigh unto the mountains a great herd of swine feeding.
020511.phonetic: Na-hna-no o-da-lu-lo-di u-ni-tsa-ti su-na-da-tlu-gi si-qua a-na-li-s-da-yv-ni-do-he-i.

020512.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎬᏩᏍᏗᏰᏔᏁ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏏᏆ ᏗᏁᏙᎲ ᏬᏥᎶᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏫᏙᏥᏴᏏ.
020512.english: And all the devils besought him, saying, Send us into the swine, that we may enter into them.
020512.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no a-ni-s-gi-na gv-wa-s-di-ye-ta-ne hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i, U-ne-la-gi si-qua di-ne-do-hv wo-tsi-lo-hi, na-s-gi wi-do-tsi-yv-si.

020513.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏥᏌ ᏚᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎴᎢ.  ᏗᎦᏓᎭᏃ ᏗᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏨ, ᏏᏆ ᏫᏚᏂᏴᏎᎴᎢ; ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩᏃ ᎤᎾᏁᎷᎩᏎ ᎤᏂᎦᏐᎠᏎ ᎦᏁᏡᎩ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏓᎵ ᏭᎾᎵᏔᏗᏅᏎᎢ, (ᏔᎵ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎾᏂᎡᎢ,) ᎠᎴ ᎥᏓᎵ ᏚᏂᎬᏤᎢ.
020513.english: And forthwith Jesus gave them leave. And the unclean spirits went out, and entered into the swine: and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the sea, (they were about two thousand;) and were choked in the sea.
020513.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv Tsi-sa du-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-le-i.  Di-ga-da-ha-no di-da-nv-do u-ni-nu-go-tsv, si-qua wi-du-ni-yv-se-le-i; su-na-da-tlu-gi-no u-na-ne-lu-gi-se u-ni-ga-so-a-se ga-ne-tlu-gi ge-sv v-da-li wu-na-li-ta-di-nv-se-i, (ta-li i-ya-ga-yv-li i-yv-dv na-ni-e-i,) a-le v-da-li du-ni-gv-tse-i.

020514.cherokee: ᏗᏂᎦᏘᏯᏃ ᎤᎾᎵᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏚᎲ ᏭᏂᏃᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏓᏁᏩᏗᏒᎢ.  ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏤᏃ ᎤᎾᎦᏔᏅᏎ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎢ.
020514.english: And they that fed the swine fled, and told it in the city, and in the country. And they went out to see what it was that was done.
020514.phonetic: Di-ni-ga-ti-ya-no u-na-li-se-i, a-le ga-du-hv wu-ni-no-ne-i, a-le i-yv-dv da-ne-wa-di-sv-i.  U-ni-nu-go-tse-no u-na-ga-ta-nv-se na-s-gi nu-li-s-ta-nv-i.

020515.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎪᎮ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎬᏩᏴᎸᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᎬᏩᏴᎸᎯ, ᎤᏬᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏄᏪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᎯ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎮᎢ.
020515.english: And they come to Jesus, and see him that was possessed with the devil, and had the legion, sitting, and clothed, and in his right mind: and they were afraid.
020515.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no gv-wa-lu-tse-le-i, a-le gv-wa-go-he na-s-gi a-ni-s-gi-na gv-wa-yv-lv-hi, na-s-gi su-na-da-tlu-gi gv-wa-yv-lv-hi, u-wo-le-i, a-le du-nu-we-i, a-le do-hi u-da-nv-te-i; a-le a-ni-s-ga-i-he-i.

020516.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏂᎪᎲᎯ, ᎬᏩᏂᏃᏁᎴ ᏄᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎬᏩᏴᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏏᏆ ᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ.
020516.english: And they that saw it told them how it befell to him that was possessed with the devil, and also concerning the swine.
020516.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-ni-go-hv-hi, gv-wa-ni-no-ne-le nu-li-s-da-ne-lv a-ni-s-gi-na gv-wa-yv-lv-hi, a-le na-s-quo si-qua nu-na-li-s-da-ne-lv-i.

020517.english: And they began to pray him to depart out of their coasts.

020518.cherokee: ᏥᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᏣᏅ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎬᏩᏴᎸᎯ ᎤᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᎤᏪᎵᏎᏗᏱ ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏗᏓᏍᏗᏱ.
020518.english: And when he was come into the ship, he that had been possessed with the devil prayed him that he might be with him.
020518.phonetic: Tsi-yu-hi-no u-tsa-nv na-s-gi a-ni-s-gi-na gv-wa-yv-lv-hi u-ta-yo-se-le u-ne-la-gi u-we-li-se-di-yi u-s-da-wa-di-da-s-di-yi.

020519.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏳᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎴᎢ ᏥᏌ, ᎯᎠᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏥᎮᎾ ᏗᏤᏅᏒ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏗᏨᎾ ᏗᏁᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᏗᏃᎲᏏ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏙᎵᏨᎢ.
020519.english: Howbeit Jesus suffered him not, but saith unto him, Go home to thy friends, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had compassion on thee.
020519.phonetic: A-se-no v-tla yu-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-le-i Tsi-sa, hi-a-quo-s-gi-ni nu-we-se-le-i, Tsi-he-na di-tse-nv-sv go-hu-s-di di-tsv-na di-ne-hv-i, a-le wi-di-no-hv-si ni-ga-v u-s-qua-ni-go-di U-gv-wi-yu-hi ni-tsa-dv-ne-lv-i, a-le tsa-do-li-tsv-i.

020520.cherokee: ᎤᏂᎩᏎᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᏚᏃᏣᎳᏁ ᎠᏍᎪᎯ-ᎢᎦᏚᎩᏱ, ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏥᏌ ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ.
020520.english: And he departed, and began to publish in Decapolis how great things Jesus had done for him: and all men did marvel.
020520.phonetic: U-ni-gi-se-no, a-le u-le-nv-he du-no-tsa-la-ne A-s-go-hi--i-ga-du-gi-yi, ni-ga-v u-s-qua-ni-go-di Tsi-sa nu-dv-ne-lv-i; a-le ni-ga-dv yv-wi u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i.

020521.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏥᏳᎯ ᏙᎤᏪᏐᏨ ᎢᏍᎪᎾ ᏫᎤᎷᏨ, ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ ᎬᏩᏓᏡᏫᏍᏔᏁᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎥᏓᎵ ᎤᎶᏗ ᎡᏙᎮᎢ.
020521.english: And when Jesus was passed over again by ship unto the other side, much people gathered unto him: and he was nigh unto the sea.
020521.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no ta-li-ne tsi-yu-hi do-u-we-so-tsv i-s-go-na wi-u-lu-tsv, u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi gv-wa-da-tlu-wi-s-ta-ne-i; a-le v-da-li u-lo-di e-do-he-i.

020522.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᎷᏤ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᏤᎳ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ; ᎤᎪᎲᏃ [ᏥᏌ] ᏚᎳᏍᎬ ᎤᏓᏅᏁᎢ,
020522.english: And, behold, there cometh one of the rulers of the synagogue, Jairus by name; and when he saw him, he fell at his feet,
020522.phonetic: A-le gv-ni-yu-quo u-lu-tse gi-lo i-yu-s-di nu-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, Tse-la tsu-do-i-dv; u-go-hv-no [Tsi-sa] du-la-s-gv u-da-nv-ne-i,

020523.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏍᏗᏰᏔᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎠᏇᏥ ᎠᎨᏳᏣ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏲᎱᏍᎦ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᏁᎾ, ᎯᏯᏏᏔᏛᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏗᏫᏍᏗᏱ, ᎬᏁᏍᏗᏃ.
020523.english: And besought him greatly, saying, My little daughter lieth at the point of death: I pray thee, come and lay thy hands on her, that she may be healed; and she shall live.
020523.phonetic: A-le u-tsa-ti u-s-di-ye-ta-ne-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; A-que-tsi a-ge-yu-tsa hna-quo a-yo-hu-s-ga; a-se-no i-ne-na, hi-ya-si-ta-dv-ha, na-s-gi u-di-wi-s-di-yi, gv-ne-s-di-no.

020524.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏅᏎᏃ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᏴᏫ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏁᏄᎳᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
020524.english: And Jesus went with him; and much people followed him, and thronged him.
020524.phonetic: U-ne-nv-se-no; a-le u-ni-tsa-te yv-wi gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-se-i, a-le gv-wa-ne-nu-la-s-di-s-ge-i.

020525.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎨᏴ ᏔᎳᏚ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎩᎬ ᎤᏪᏅᎡᎮᎢ,
020525.english: And a certain woman, which had an issue of blood twelve years,
020525.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di a-ge-yv ta-la-du tsu-de-ti-yv-dv gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv gi-gv u-we-nv-e-he-i,

020526.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎪᏗᏳ ᎠᏂᎦᎾᎦᏘ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎬᏩᎩᎸᏅᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᎲ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏒᏅᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎠᎫᏱᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᏄᎵᏍᏓᏁᎮᎢ, ᎧᏁᏉᎨᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏢᎬᎢ;
020526.english: And had suffered many things of many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was nothing bettered, but rather grew worse,
020526.phonetic: A-le a-ni-go-di-yu a-ni-ga-na-ga-ti u-tsa-ti gv-wa-gi-lv-nv-hi ge-se-i, a-le go-hu-s-di u-hv ni-ga-dv u-sv-nv-hi ge-se a-gu-yi-s-gv-i, a-se-no v-tla o-si-yu yi-nu-li-s-da-ne-he-i, ka-ne-quo-ge-quo-s-gi-ni u-tlv-gv-i;

020527.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ ᏥᏌ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ, ᎣᏂᏗᏢ ᎤᏅᏘᏛ ᏄᎾᏛᏅ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏔᏁ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏒᏂᎴ ᎤᏄᏮᎢ.
020527.english: When she had heard of Jesus, came in the press behind, and touched his garment.
020527.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-dv-ga-nv Tsi-sa a-tsi-no-he-s-gv-i, o-ni-di-tlv u-nv-ti-dv nu-na-dv-nv u-lu-hi-s-ta-ne, a-le u-sv-ni-le u-nu-wv-i.

020528.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᏧᏄᏬᏉ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏱᏓᏆᏒᏂᎸ, ᏯᏆᏗᏫ.
020528.english: For she said, If I may touch but his clothes, I shall be whole.
020528.phonetic: Hi-a-ye-no nu-we-se-i, Tsu-nu-wo-quo na-s-quo yi-da-qua-sv-ni-lv, ya-qua-di-wi.

020529.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎩᎬ ᎤᏪᏅᎡᎲ ᎤᏲᎯᏍᏔᏁᎢ; ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏎᏃ ᎠᏰᎸ ᎤᏗᏩᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏢᎬᎢ.
020529.english: And straightway the fountain of her blood was dried up; and she felt in her body that she was healed of that plague.
020529.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv gi-gv u-we-nv-e-hv u-yo-hi-s-ta-ne-i; u-do-le-ho-se-no a-ye-lv u-di-wa-sv na-s-gi u-tlv-gv-i.

020530.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏓᏅᏬᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏄᎪᏨᎢ, ᎤᎦᏔᎲᏎ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏅᏘᏛ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎦᎪ ᏓᏒᏂᎦ ᏗᏆᏄᏬ?
020530.english: And Jesus, immediately knowing in himself that virtue had gone out of him, turned him about in the press, and said, Who touched my clothes?
020530.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no gi-la-quo i-yv-dv u-do-le-ho-sv u-wa-sv ge-sv u-da-nv-wo-di ge-sv u-nu-go-tsv-i, u-ga-ta-hv-se na-hna u-nv-ti-dv a-ni-do-na-v-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-i, Ga-go da-sv-ni-ga di-qua-nu-wo?

020531.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏕᎩᎪᏩᏗᎭ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏣᏁᏄᎳᏍᏗᏍᎬ, ᏥᎪᏃ ᎦᎪ ᎠᏆᏒᏂᎦ ᎢᎭᏗᎭ?
020531.english: And his disciples said unto him, Thou seest the multitude thronging thee, and sayest thou, Who touched me?
020531.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i, De-gi-go-wa-di-ha u-ni-tsa-ti ge-tsa-ne-nu-la-s-di-s-gv, tsi-go-no Ga-go a-qua-sv-ni-ga i-ha-di-ha?

020532.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏚᏫᏛᏃ ᎤᎦᏖᏃᎴ ᎤᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏳᏛᏁᎸᎯ.
020532.english: And he looked round about to see her that had done this thing.
020532.phonetic: Gv-wa-du-wi-dv-no u-ga-te-no-le u-go-wa-dv-di-yi na-s-gi hi-a i-yu-dv-ne-lv-hi.

020533.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎠᏍᎦᎢᎮ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᎾᏫᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᎦᏔᎮ ᏄᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ, ᎤᎷᏤ ᎠᎴ ᎡᎳᏗ ᎤᏓᏅᏁᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎤᏃᏁᎴᎢ.
020533.english: But the woman fearing and trembling, knowing what was done in her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.
020533.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no a-ge-yv a-s-ga-i-he a-le u-we-na-wi-s-ge-i, a-ga-ta-he nu-li-s-da-ne-lv-i, u-lu-tse a-le e-la-di u-da-nv-ne-le-i, a-le ni-ga-dv du-yu-go-dv u-no-ne-le-i.

020534.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎠᏇᏥ, ᏦᎯᏳᏒ ᏣᏗᏫᏍᏓ; ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᏥᎮᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏗᏩᏒᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏣᏢᎬᎢ.
020534.english: And he said unto her, Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace, and be whole of thy plague.
020534.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-le-i, A-que-tsi, tso-hi-yu-sv tsa-di-wi-s-da; nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv tsi-he-na, a-le tsa-di-wa-sv-hi ge-se-s-di tsa-tlv-gv-i.

020535.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉ ᎧᏁᎨᎢ, ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎦᏁᎸ ᏅᏓᏳᎾᏓᎴᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᎷᏤ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏤᏥ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎠᏏᏉ ᎢᎯᏯᏕᏯᏙᏗᎭ ᏗᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ?
020535.english: While he yet spake, there came from the ruler of the synagogue's house certain which said, Thy daughter is dead: why troublest thou the Master any further?
020535.phonetic: A-si-quo ka-ne-ge-i, nu-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi ga-ne-lv nv-da-yu-na-da-le-nv-hi u-ni-lu-tse hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i, U-yo-hu-sv tse-tsi; ga-do-no a-si-quo i-hi-ya-de-ya-do-di-ha di-de-yo-hv-s-gi?

020536.cherokee: ᎤᏛᎦᏅᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏂᏬᏂᏒᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ, ᏦᎯᏳᏎᏍᏗᏉ.
020536.english: As soon as Jesus heard the word that was spoken, he saith unto the ruler of the synagogue, Be not afraid, only believe.
020536.phonetic: U-dv-ga-nv-quo-no Tsi-sa nu-s-dv u-ni-wo-ni-sv-i, hi-a nu-we-se-le nu-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, Tle-s-di yi-s-ga-i-he-s-di, tso-hi-yu-se-s-di-quo.

020537.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᏳᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏍᏗᏱ, ᏈᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏂ ᏥᎻ ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᎤᏅᏒ.
020537.english: And he suffered no man to follow him, save Peter, and James, and John the brother of James.
020537.phonetic: V-tla a-le gi-lo yu-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-s-di-yi, Qui-da, a-le Tsi-mi, a-le Tsa-ni Tsi-mi di-na-da-nv-tli u-nv-sv.

020538.cherokee: ᎤᎷᏤᏃ ᎦᏁᎸ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎦᎮ ᎤᎾᏓᏑᏰᏛ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏓᎾᏠᏱᎲ ᎠᎴ ᏓᏂᏴᎬᎢ.
020538.english: And he cometh to the house of the ruler of the synagogue, and seeth the tumult, and them that wept and wailed greatly.
020538.phonetic: U-lu-tse-no ga-ne-lv nu-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, a-le du-ga-he u-na-da-su-ye-dv yv-wi, a-le u-tsa-ti da-na-tlo-yi-hv a-le da-ni-yv-gv-i.

020539.cherokee: ᎤᏰᎸᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᏣᏓᏃᏴᎵᏍᏗᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᏣᏠᏱᎭ?  ᎥᏝ ᏳᏲᎱᏒ ᎠᎨᏳᏣ, ᎦᎵᎭᏉᏍᎩᏂ.
020539.english: And when he was come in, he saith unto them, Why make ye this ado, and weep? the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth.
020539.phonetic: U-ye-lv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Ga-do-no i-tsa-da-no-yv-li-s-di-ha, a-le do-tsa-tlo-yi-ha?  V-tla yu-yo-hu-sv a-ge-yu-tsa, ga-li-ha-quo-s-gi-ni.

020540.cherokee: ᎢᎬᏩᏙᏢᏔᏁᏉᏃ.  ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᏚᏄᎪᏫᏐᏅ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎨᏳᏣ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏚᏘᏅᏎ, ᎠᎴ ᏭᏴᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᎨᏳᏣ ᏗᏐᏅᎢ.
020540.english: And they laughed him to scorn. But when he had put them all out, he taketh the father and the mother of the damsel, and them that were with him, and entereth in where the damsel was lying.
020540.phonetic: I-gv-wa-do-tlv-ta-ne-quo-no.  Ni-ga-dv-no du-nu-go-wi-so-nv, hi-a na-s-gi a-ge-yu-tsa u-do-da a-le u-tsi a-le na-s-gi a-ne-hi du-ti-nv-se, a-le wu-yv-le na-hna a-ge-yu-tsa di-so-nv-i.

020541.cherokee: ᎤᏬᏯᏁᏒᏃ ᎠᎨᏳᏣ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏔᎵᏓ ᎫᎻ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏁᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏗ ᎦᏛᎦ; ᎯᎨᏳᏣ, ᎬᏲᏎᎭ, ᏔᎴᎲᎦ.
020541.english: And he took the damsel by the hand, and said unto her, Talitha cumi; which is, being interpreted, Damsel, I say unto thee, arise.
020541.phonetic: U-wo-ya-ne-sv-no a-ge-yu-tsa, hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, Ta-li-da Gu-mi; na-s-gi a-ne-tlv-ta-nv-hi hi-a nu-s-di ga-dv-ga; Hi-ge-yu-tsa, gv-yo-se-ha, Ta-le-hv-ga.

020542.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᎨᏳᏣ ᏚᎴᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᏙᎴᎢ; ᏔᎳᏚᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎨᏎᎢ.  ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯᏃ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ.
020542.english: And straightway the damsel arose, and walked; for she was of the age of twelve years. And they were astonished with a great astonishment.
020542.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv a-ge-yu-tsa du-le-ne-i, a-le u-we-do-le-i; ta-la-du-ye-no i-yu-de-ti-yv-dv ge-se-i.  U-tsa-ta-nv-hi-no u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i.

020543.cherokee: ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛᏃ ᏚᏁᏤᎴ ᏚᏅᏍᏓᏕᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏤᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᎤᏂᏁᏗᏱ.
020543.english: And he charged them straitly that no man should know it; and commanded that something should be given her to eat.
020543.phonetic: U-li-ni-gi-dv-no du-ne-tse-le du-nv-s-da-de-le gi-lo u-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi na-s-gi; a-le u-ne-tse-go-hu-s-di u-li-s-da-yv-di u-ni-ne-di-yi.

020601.cherokee: ᎤᏄᎪᏤᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏭᎷᏤᎢ; ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ.
020601.english: And he went out from thence, and came into his own country; and his disciples follow him.
020601.phonetic: U-nu-go-tse-no na-hna-ni, a-le u-wa-sv u-tse-li-go-hi wu-lu-tse-i; gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-se-i.

020602.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬᏃ ᎤᎵᏰᎶᎸ, ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᏚᏕᏲᏁ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ; ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎬᏩᏛᎦᏁᎸ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ, ᎭᏢ ᎠᎩᎭ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏩᏛᏁᏗ ᏥᎩ?  ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᏁᎸᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏥᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ?
020602.english: And when the sabbath day was come, he began to teach in the synagogue: and many hearing him were astonished, saying, From whence hath this man these things? and what wisdom is this which is given unto him, that even such mighty works are wrought by his hands?
020602.phonetic: U-na-do-da-qua-s-gv-no u-li-ye-lo-lv, u-le-nv-he du-de-yo-ne di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi; u-ni-tsa-ti-no gv-wa-dv-ga-ne-lv u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i, hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i, Ha-tlv a-gi-ha hi-a a-s-ga-ya hi-a na-s-gi i-gv-wa-dv-ne-di tsi-gi?  A-le ga-do u-s-di a-ga-do-v-hi-s-di na-s-gi a-tsi-ne-lv-hi tsi-gi, na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-s-qua-ni-go-di tsi-du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ha?

020603.cherokee: ᏝᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏗᏁᏍᎨᏍᎩ ᏱᎩ, ᎺᎵ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᏥᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᏦᏏ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᏌᏩᏂ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ?  ᎠᎴ ᏧᏙ ᏝᏍᎪ ᎠᏂ ᏱᎨᎦᏓᏑᏯ?  ᎥᎬᏩᏐᏅᏤᎸᎩᏃ.
020603.english: Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary, the brother of James, and Joses, and of Juda, and Simon? and are not his sisters here with us? And they were offended at him.
020603.phonetic: Tla-s-go na-s-gi hi-a di-ne-s-ge-s-gi yi-gi, Me-li u-we-tsi, Tsi-mi, a-le Tso-si, a-le Tsu-da, a-le Sa-wa-ni a-na-li-nv-tli?  A-le tsu-do tla-s-go a-ni yi-ge-ga-da-su-ya?  V-gv-wa-so-nv-tse-lv-gi-no.

020604.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎾᏥᎸᏉᏛᎾ ᏱᎨᏐᎢ, ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎤᏩᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏴᏫ ᎠᏁᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ.
020604.english: But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house.
020604.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; a-do-le-ho-s-gi v-tla na-tsi-lv-quo-dv-na yi-ge-so-i, u-tse-li-go-hi-s-gi-ni-no-nv u-wa-sv, a-le tsu-yv-wi a-ne-hv-i, a-le u-wa-sv ga-ne-lv-i.

020605.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏗᎬᏩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ, ᎤᏩᏒᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᏚᏏᏔᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏅᏩᏅᎢ.
020605.english: And he could there do no mighty work, save that he laid his hands upon a few sick folk, and healed them.
020605.phonetic: A-le na-hna v-tla go-hu-s-di u-s-qua-ni-go-di di-gv-wa-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di yi-ge-se-i, u-wa-sv-s-gi-ni-no-nv i-lv-s-gi i-ya-ni-dv tsu-ni-tlv-gi du-si-ta-dv a-le du-nv-wa-nv-i.

020606.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎ ᎾᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎬᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᏙᎴ ᎤᏚᏫᏛ ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒᎢ ᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎨᎢ.
020606.english: And he marvelled because of their unbelief. And he went round about the villages, teaching.
020606.phonetic: A-le u-s-qua-ni-go-se na-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gv-na ge-sv-i.  A-le u-we-do-le u-du-wi-dv de-ga-du-wa-di-sv-i da-de-yo-hv-s-ge-i.

020607.cherokee: ᏔᎳᏚᏃ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏅᏏᏙᎴ ᏔᎵᎭ ᏧᎵᎪᏅᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎴ ᏧᏂᏄᎪᏫᏍᏗᏱ ᏗᎦᏓᎭ ᏗᏓᏅᏙ;
020607.english: And he called unto him the twelve, and began to send them forth by two and two; and gave them power over unclean spirits;
020607.phonetic: Ta-la-du-no i-ya-ni-dv wi-du-ya-nv-he-i, a-le du-nv-si-do-le ta-li-ha tsu-li-go-nv-dv, a-le du-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-le tsu-ni-nu-go-wi-s-di-yi di-ga-da-ha di-da-nv-do;

020608.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏚᏁᏤᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏴᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎠᏁᏙᎲᎢ, ᎠᏙᎳᏅᏍᏗ ᎤᏩᏒ; ᎥᏝ ᏕᎦᎶᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏚ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏣᏱ ᏚᎾᏓᏠᎲᎢ;
020608.english: And commanded them that they should take nothing for their journey, save a staff only; no scrip, no bread, no money in their purse:
020608.phonetic: A-le du-ne-tse-le na-s-gi go-hu-s-di u-ni-yv-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na u-na-li-s-de-lv-do-di a-ne-do-hv-i, a-do-la-nv-s-di u-wa-sv; v-tla de-ga-lo-di, a-le ga-du, a-le v-tsa-yi du-na-da-tlo-hv-i;

020609.english: But be shod with sandals; and not put on two coats.

020610.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᎢᎸᎯᏢ ᎢᏥᏴᎯᎮᏍᏗ, ᎾᎿᎭᏉ ᎢᏣᏅᎥᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎬᏂ ᎥᏣᏂᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᏂ.
020610.english: And he said unto them, In what place soever ye enter into an house, there abide till ye depart from that place.
020610.phonetic: A-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, a-da-ne-lv i-lv-hi-tlv i-tsi-yv-hi-he-s-di, na-hna-quo i-tsa-nv-v-s-ge-s-di gv-ni v-tsa-ni-gi na-hna-ni.

020611.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᏂᏗᏣᏓᏂᎸᎬᎾ ᎠᎴ ᏂᏣᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎲᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎾᎿᎭᎢᏣᏂᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᏗᏣᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᎪᏍᏚ ᏕᏥᏅᎪᎥᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎯᏳᏗᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏡᏗᏍᎩ.  ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎤᏂᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏐᏓᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎹᎵ, ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎤᏟᎯᏳ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏚᎲᎢ.
020611.english: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear you, when ye depart thence, shake off the dust under your feet for a testimony against them. Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.
020611.phonetic: A-le gi-lo ni-di-tsa-da-ni-lv-gv-na a-le ni-tsa-dv-da-s-da-ne-hv-na i-ge-se-s-di, hna-quo na-hna i-tsa-ni-gi-s-ge-s-di, di-tsa-la-si-de-ni go-s-du de-tsi-nv-go-v-di-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi go-hi-yu-di-s-gi u-na-tlu-di-s-gi.  U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, e-s-ga-quo i-ga-i u-ni-gi-li-yo-i-s-di ge-se-s-di So-da-mi, a-le Go-ma-li, di-gu-go-do-di-yi i-ga ge-se-s-di, u-tli-hi-yu na-hna ga-du-hv-i.

020612.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏤᏃ, ᏭᎾᎵᏥᏙᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏎ ᏧᏂᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ.
020612.english: And they went out, and preached that men should repent.
020612.phonetic: U-ni-nu-go-tse-no, wu-na-li-tsi-do-ne na-s-gi yv-wi a-se tsu-ni-ne-tli-yv-s-di ge-sv du-na-da-nv-dv-i.

020613.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᏚᏂᏄᎪᏫᏎ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎢ ᏚᏂᎶᏁᏔᏁ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᏅᏩᏁᎢ.
020613.english: And they cast out many devils, and anointed with oil many that were sick, and healed them.
020613.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no du-ni-nu-go-wi-se a-ni-s-gi-na, a-le go-i du-ni-lo-ne-ta-ne u-ni-tsa-ti tsu-ni-tlv-gi, a-le du-ni-nv-wa-ne-i.

020614.cherokee: ᎡᎶᏛᏃ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏛᎦᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ; ᏚᏃᏣᎶᏤᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏙᎥᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎ ᎡᎶᏛ, ᏣᏂ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ ᏓᎴᎯᏌ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏥᏕᎤᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ.
020614.english: And king Herod heard of him; (for his name was spread abroad:) and he said, That John the Baptist was risen from the dead, and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him.
020614.phonetic: E-lo-dv-no u-gv-wi-yu-hi u-dv-ga-ne na-s-gi a-tsi-no-he-s-gv-i; du-no-tsa-lo-tse-ye-no na-s-gi du-do-v-i; a-le hi-a nu-we-se E-lo-dv, Tsa-ni Di-da-wo-s-gi da-le-hi-sa u-yo-hu-sv-i, a-le na-s-gi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha u-s-qua-ni-go-di tsi-de-u-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ha.

020615.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ, ᎢᎳᏯ ᎯᎠ.  ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ.
020615.english: Others said, That it is Elias. And others said, That it is a prophet, or as one of the prophets.
020615.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i, I-la-ya hi-a.  I-ga-dv-no hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i, a-do-le-ho-s-gi hi-a, a-le gi-lo a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi ge-sv na-s-gi-ya-i.

020616.cherokee: ᎡᎶᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏣᏂ ᎯᎠ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᏥᏥᏍᎫᏕᏒᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᎴᎯᏌᏅ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎢ.
020616.english: But when Herod heard thereof, he said, It is John, whom I beheaded: he is risen from the dead.
020616.phonetic: E-lo-dv-s-gi-ni u-dv-ga-nv, hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tsa-ni hi-a, na-s-gi a-yv tsi-tsi-s-gu-de-sv-gi; na-s-gi du-le-hi-sa-nv u-yo-hu-sv-i.

020617.cherokee: ᎡᎶᏛᏰᏃ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏓᏅᏒᎯ ᎨᏎ ᏭᏂᏴᎲᎯ ᎨᏎ ᏣᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ ᎤᎸᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᏍᏛᏗᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᎡᎶᏗᏏ, ᎤᏅᏟ ᏈᎵᎩ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎠᏓᏰᎮᎢ.
020617.english: For Herod himself had sent forth and laid hold upon John, and bound him in prison for Herodias' sake, his brother Philip's wife: for he had married her.
020617.phonetic: E-lo-dv-ye-no u-wa-sv u-da-nv-sv-hi ge-se wu-ni-yv-hv-hi ge-se Tsa-ni, a-le di-da-s-du-di-yi u-lv-lv-hi ge-se-i, a-s-dv-di-s-di-s-ge E-lo-di-si, u-nv-tli Qui-li-gi u-da-li-i; na-s-gi-ye-no a-da-ye-he-i.

020618.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏳᏪᏎᎸᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎡᎶᏛ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᏚᏳᎪᏗ ᎡᏣᏅᏟ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ ᏣᏓᏰᎭ.
