020618.english: For John had said unto Herod, It is not lawful for thee to have thy brother's wife.
020618.phonetic: Tsa-ni-ye-no hi-a i-yu-we-se-lv-hi ge-se E-lo-dv, v-tla yi-du-yu-go-di e-tsa-nv-tli u-da-li-i tsa-da-ye-ha.

020619.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎡᎶᏗᏏ ᎠᏍᎦᎨ [ᏣᏂ] ᎠᎴ ᏳᎴᎢ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏩᏛᏁᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ.
020619.english: Therefore Herodias had a quarrel against him, and would have killed him; but she could not:
020619.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di E-lo-di-si a-s-ga-ge [Tsa-ni] a-le yu-le-i; a-se-no v-tla na-s-gi i-gv-wa-dv-ne-di yi-ge-se-i.

020620.cherokee: ᎡᎶᏛᏰᏃ ᎤᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏎ ᏣᏂ, ᎠᎦᏔᎮᏰᏃ ᎤᏓᏅᏘᏳ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎦᏅᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎦᏌᏯᏍᏗᏳ ᎨᏎᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ [ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎬ,] ᎤᏣᏖ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏨᎯ ᎠᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎮᎢ.
020620.english: For Herod feared John, knowing that he was a just man and an holy, and observed him; and when he heard him, he did many things, and heard him gladly.
020620.phonetic: E-lo-dv-ye-no u-lv-quo-di-yu ge-se Tsa-ni, a-ga-ta-he-ye-no u-da-nv-ti-yu a-le na-s-ga-nv-na ge-sv a-s-ga-ya, a-le u-ga-sa-ya-s-di-yu ge-se-i; na-s-gi-no u-dv-ga-nv [a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gv,] u-tsa-te du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-le-i, a-le u-li-he-li-tsv-hi a-dv-da-s-da-ne-he-i.

020621.cherokee: ᎠᎯᏛᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏍᏆᎸᎲ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎶᏛ ᎤᏕᏅ ᎢᏳ ᎢᎦ ᎤᏛᏅᎢᏍᏔᏁ ᎬᏩᎵᏍᏓᏴᎾᏁᏗᏱ ᏧᏤᎵ ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏰᎬᏍᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎠᏁᎯ,
020621.english: And when a convenient day was come, that Herod on his birthday made a supper to his lords, high captains, and chief estates of Galilee;
020621.phonetic: A-hi-dv-no na-s-gi i-gv-dv-ne-di ge-sv u-s-qua-lv-hv, na-s-gi E-lo-dv u-de-nv i-yu i-ga u-dv-nv-i-s-ta-ne gv-wa-li-s-da-yv-na-ne-di-yi tsu-tse-li u-ni-gv-wi-yu-hi, a-le a-ni-s-ga-ye-gv-s-da, a-le di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di Ge-li-li a-ne-hi,

020622.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎡᎶᏗᏏ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏛ ᎤᏴᎵᎸ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏍᎩᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎠᏂᏂᏜᏓᎢ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏅ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎠᏛ, ᏍᎩᏔᏲᏏ ᎢᏳᏍᏗᏉ ᎮᎵᏍᎬᎢ, ᏓᎬᏁᎵᏃ.
020622.english: And when the daughter of the said Herodias came in, and danced, and pleased Herod and them that sat with him, the king said unto the damsel, Ask of me whatsoever thou wilt, and I will give it thee.
020622.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no E-lo-di-si u-we-tsi a-dv u-yv-li-lv, a-le u-li-s-gi-sv, a-le E-lo-dv a-le na-s-gi i-tsu-la-ha a-ni-ni-dla-i o-si-yu u-ni-ye-lv-nv, u-gv-wi-yu-hi hi-a nu-we-se-le a-dv, S-gi-ta-yo-si i-yu-s-di-quo he-li-s-gv-i, da-gv-ne-li-no.

020623.cherokee: ᎤᏎᎵᏓᏁᎴᏃ [ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ,] ᎢᏳᏍᏗᏉ ᎮᎵᏍᎬ ᏍᎩᏔᏲᏏ, ᏓᎬᏁᎵᏃ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏱᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᎩᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020623.english: And he sware unto her, Whatsoever thou shalt ask of me, I will give it thee, unto the half of my kingdom.
020623.phonetic: U-se-li-da-ne-le-no [hi-a nu-we-se-i,] I-yu-s-di-quo he-li-s-gv s-gi-ta-yo-si, da-gv-ne-li-no, na-s-quo a-ye-li i-yv-dv yi-gi na-hna a-gi-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i.

020624.cherokee: ᎤᏄᎪᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏫᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎤᏥ, ᎦᏙ ᏓᏥᏔᏲᎵ?  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏣᏂ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎪᎵ.
020624.english: And she went forth, and said unto her mother, What shall I ask? And she said, The head of John the Baptist.
020624.phonetic: U-nu-go-tsv-no hi-a wi-nu-we-se-le u-tsi, ga-do da-tsi-ta-yo-li?  Hi-a-no nu-we-se-le-i, Tsa-ni Di-da-wo-s-gi a-s-go-li.

020625.cherokee: ᎢᎬᏪᏅᏛᏉᏃ ᎤᎵᏍᏗ ᎤᏴᎵᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏯᎥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏔᏲᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎠᏆᏚᎵ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏍᎩᏁᏗᏱ, ᎠᏖᎵᏙᎩᎯ, ᏣᏂ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ ᎤᏍᎪ.
020625.english: And she came in straightway with haste unto the king, and asked, saying, I will that thou give me by and by in a charger the head of John the Baptist.
020625.phonetic: I-gv-we-nv-dv-quo-no u-li-s-di u-yv-li-le u-gv-wi-yu-hi a-ya-v-i, a-le u-ta-yo-le hi-a nu-we-se-i, A-qua-du-li gi-la-quo i-yv-dv s-gi-ne-di-yi, a-te-li-do-gi-hi, Tsa-ni di-da-wo-s-gi u-s-go.

020626.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏝᏉ ᎤᏰᎸᏁᎢ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏎᎵᏔᏅᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎤᎾᏂᏜᏓᏅᎯ ᏅᏧᎵᏍᏙᏔᏁ, ᎥᏝ ᎬᏩᎨᏳᏙᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ.
020626.english: And the king was exceeding sorry; yet for his oath's sake, and for their sakes which sat with him, he would not reject her.
020626.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no u-tsa-ti tla-quo u-ye-lv-ne-i; a-se-no u-se-li-ta-nv-i, a-le na-s-gi i-tsu-la-ha u-na-ni-dla-nv-hi nv-tsu-li-s-do-ta-ne, v-tla gv-wa-ge-yu-do-di yi-ge-se-i.

020627.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏅᏎ ᎠᏯᏫᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏤ ᎤᏍᎪ ᎤᏂᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ; ᎤᏪᏅᏎᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᏭᏍᎫᏕᏎ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ ᎠᏯᎥᎢ,
020627.english: And immediately the king sent an executioner, and commanded his head to be brought: and he went and beheaded him in the prison,
020627.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv u-gv-wi-yu-hi u-nv-se a-ya-wi-s-gi, a-le u-ne-tse u-s-go u-ni-yo-hi-s-di-yi; u-we-nv-se-no, a-le wu-s-gu-de-se di-da-s-du-di-yi a-ya-v-i,

020628.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲᎴ ᎤᏍᎪ ᎠᏖᎵᏙᎩᎯ ᎦᎶᏕᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏛ ᎤᏁᎴᎢ; ᎠᏛᏃ ᎤᏥ ᎤᏁᎴᎢ.
020628.english: And brought his head in a charger, and gave it to the damsel: and the damsel gave it to her mother.
020628.phonetic: A-le u-yo-le u-s-go a-te-li-do-gi-hi ga-lo-de-i, a-le a-dv u-ne-le-i; a-dv-no u-tsi u-ne-le-i.

020629.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ, ᎤᏂᎷᏤ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏁᏎ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᎤᏂᏅᏁᎢ.
020629.english: And when his disciples heard of it, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.
020629.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no u-na-dv-ga-nv, u-ni-lu-tse a-le u-ni-ne-se a-ye-lv-i, a-le a-tse-li-s-dv u-ni-nv-ne-i.

020630.cherokee: ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏟᏌᏁ ᏥᏌ ᎡᏙᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏃᏁᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏄᎾᏛᏁᎵᏙᎸ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏚᎾᏕᏲᏅᎢ.
020630.english: And the apostles gathered themselves together unto Jesus, and told him all things, both what they had done, and what they had taught.
020630.phonetic: Ge-tsi-nv-si-dv-no u-na-da-tli-sa-ne Tsi-sa e-do-hv-i, a-le gv-wa-no-ne-le ni-ga-dv nu-na-dv-ne-li-do-lv, a-le na-s-quo nu-s-dv du-na-de-yo-nv-i.

020631.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᏕᎾ ᎢᏨᏒᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏫᏗᎶᎯ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏗᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏞᎦ ᎢᏣᏣᏪᏐᎸᏍᏓ; ᎤᏂᏣᏖᏰᏃ ᎠᏂᎷᎨ ᎠᎴ ᏓᏁᎨᎢ, ᎥᏞᏃ ᏯᏜᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᎨ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ.
020631.english: And he said unto them, Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while: for there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.
020631.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, I-de-na i-tsv-sv-quo i-yv-dv wi-di-lo-hi i-na-ge di-ge-sv-i, a-le tle-ga i-tsa-tsa-we-so-lv-s-da; u-ni-tsa-te-ye-no a-ni-lu-ge a-le da-ne-ge-i, v-tle-no ya-dla-nv-da-di-s-ge na-s-quo u-na-li-s-da-yv-di-yi i-go-hi-dv.

020632.cherokee: ᏥᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᎾᏣᎡᎢ, ᎤᎾᏂᎩᏎ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏭᏂᎶᏎ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ.
020632.english: And they departed into a desert place by ship privately.
020632.phonetic: Tsi-yu-hi-no u-na-tsa-e-i, u-na-ni-gi-se i-na-ge wu-ni-lo-se u-de-li-dv.

020633.cherokee: ᏴᏫᏃ ᎬᏩᏂᎪᎮ ᎠᎾᏂᎩᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᎬᏬᎵᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᎳᏗᏉ ᏚᏂᏍᏆᎸᏔᏁ ᎾᎿᎭᎢᏗᏢ ᏭᏂᎶᏎ ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒ ᏂᏙᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ, ᏕᎬᏩᏂᎩᏰᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᎬᏩᏓᏟᏌᏁᎴᎢ.
020633.english: And the people saw them departing, and many knew him, and ran afoot thither out of all cities, and outwent them, and came together unto him.
020633.phonetic: Yv-wi-no gv-wa-ni-go-he a-na-ni-gi-s-gv-i, a-le u-ni-tsa-te gv-wo-li-ge-i, a-le e-la-di-quo du-ni-s-qua-lv-ta-ne na-hna i-di-tlv wu-ni-lo-se de-ga-du-wa-di-sv ni-do-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi, de-gv-wa-ni-gi-ye-no, a-le wi-gv-wa-da-tli-sa-ne-le-i.

020634.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏣᎢᏒ, ᏚᎪᎮ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏪᏙᎵᏤᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᎠᏫ ᎤᏂᏃᏕᎾ ᏓᎾᏤᎳᎬ ᏁᎲᎾ ᏥᎨᏐ ᎤᏂᎦᏘᏯ; ᎤᎴᏅᎮᏃ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏚᏪᏲᏁᎢ.
020634.english: And Jesus, when he came out, saw much people, and was moved with compassion toward them, because they were as sheep not having a shepherd: and he began to teach them many things.
020634.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-tsa-i-sv, du-go-he u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi, a-le du-we-do-li-tse-i, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge a-wi u-ni-no-de-na da-na-tse-la-gv ne-hv-na tsi-ge-so u-ni-ga-ti-ya; u-le-nv-he-no u-tsa-ti tsu-da-le-nv-dv du-we-yo-ne-i.

020635.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᎶᏐᏂᏕᎾ ᎨᏎ ᎢᎦ, ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᎠᏂ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᎶᏐᏂᏗ ᎢᎦ.
020635.english: And when the day was now far spent, his disciples came unto him, and said, This is a desert place, and now the time is far passed:
020635.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-lo-so-ni-de-na ge-se i-ga, gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi gv-wa-lu-tse-le, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i, A-ni i-na-ge-i, a-le hna-quo u-lo-so-ni-di i-ga.

020636.cherokee: ᏘᏰᎵᎯᏍᏓ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏓᏁᎳᏗᏒ ᎬᏩᏚᏫᏛ ᏩᏂᎶᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏭᏂᏩᎯ ᎦᏚ, ᎥᏞᏰᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏯᏂᏰ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ.
020636.english: Send them away, that they may go into the country round about, and into the villages, and buy themselves bread: for they have nothing to eat.
020636.phonetic: Ti-ye-li-hi-s-da, na-s-gi da-da-ne-la-di-sv gv-wa-du-wi-dv wa-ni-lo-hi, a-le de-ga-du-wa-di-sv-i, a-le wu-ni-wa-hi ga-du, v-tle-ye-no go-hu-s-di ya-ni-ye u-na-li-s-da-yv-di.

020637.cherokee: ᏚᏁᏤᎴᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏂᎯ ᏗᏤᎳᏍᏓ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏥᎪ ᏓᏲᏤᏏ ᏔᎵᏧᏈ ᎠᏂᎩᏏ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎦᏚ ᏮᏓᏲᎩᏩᏏ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᏓᏲᏤᎳᏍᏔᏂ?
020637.english: He answered and said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they say unto him, Shall we go and buy two hundred pennyworth of bread, and give them to eat?
020637.phonetic: Du-ne-tse-le-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Ni-hi di-tse-la-s-da.  Hi-a-no ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i, Tsi-go da-yo-tse-si ta-li-tsu-qui a-ni-gi-si tsu-gv-wa-lo-di ga-du wv-da-yo-gi-wa-si, a-le do-da-yo-tse-la-s-ta-ni?

020638.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᎳᎪ ᎦᏚ ᏕᏥᏰᎭ?  ᏫᏣᎦᏔ.  ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒᏃ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᎯᏪᏎᎢ, ᎯᏍᎩ, ᏔᎵᏃ ᎠᏣᏗ.
020638.english: He saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? go and see. And when they knew, they say, Five, and two fishes.
020638.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, I-la-go ga-du de-tsi-ye-ha?  Wi-tsa-ga-ta.  U-na-do-le-ho-sv-no, hi-a nu-hi-we-se-i, Hi-s-gi, ta-li-no a-tsa-di.

020639.cherokee: ᏚᏁᏤᎴᏃ ᎤᎾᏂᏢᏗᏱ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎢᏤ ᎧᏁᏍᎪᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏧᎾᏓᏡᏅᏛ.
020639.english: And he commanded them to make all sit down by companies upon the green grass.
020639.phonetic: Du-ne-tse-le-no u-na-ni-tlv-di-yi ni-ga-dv i-tse ka-ne-s-go-hi ge-sv tsu-na-da-tlu-nv-dv.

020640.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏂᏢᏁᏃ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᎿᎭᏁᎢ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᏍᎦᏍᎪᎯ ᏧᎾᏓᏡᏅᏛ.
020640.english: And they sat down in ranks, by hundreds, and by fifties.
020640.phonetic: U-na-ni-tlv-ne-no o-s-dv du-na-da-nv-hna-ne-i a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui, a-le hi-s-ga-s-go-hi tsu-na-da-tlu-nv-dv.

020641.cherokee: ᎯᏍᎩᏃ ᎦᏚ ᏚᎩᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵ ᎠᏣᏗ ᏚᏁᏒ, ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏫᏚᎧᎿᎭᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎬᎭᎷᏰ ᎦᏚ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏚᏁᎴ ᏧᏂᏁᏗᏱ; ᏔᎵᏃ ᎠᏣᏗ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏚᏯᏙᎮᎴᎢ.
020641.english: And when he had taken the five loaves and the two fishes, he looked up to heaven, and blessed, and brake the loaves, and gave them to his disciples to set before them; and the two fishes divided he among them all.
020641.phonetic: Hi-s-gi-no ga-du du-gi-sv a-le ta-li a-tsa-di du-ne-sv, ga-lv-la-di wi-du-ka-hna-ne-i, a-le u-li-he-li-tse-i, a-le du-gv-ha-lu-ye ga-du, a-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi du-ne-le tsu-ni-ne-di-yi; ta-li-no a-tsa-di ni-ga-dv du-ya-do-he-le-i.

020642.english: And they did all eat, and were filled.

020643.cherokee: ᎤᏄᏖᏎᏃ ᎤᎵᎬᎭᎷᏴᎯ ᏔᎳᏚ ᏗᎧᎵᎢ ᏔᎷᏣ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏣᏗ.
020643.english: And they took up twelve baskets full of the fragments, and of the fishes.
020643.phonetic: U-nu-te-se-no u-li-gv-ha-lu-yv-hi ta-la-du di-ka-li-i ta-lu-tsa, a-le na-s-quo a-tsa-di.

020644.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ Ꮎ ᎦᏚ ᏧᏂᎬᎯ ᎯᏍᎩ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎾᏂᎡ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ.
020644.english: And they that did eat of the loaves were about five thousand men.
020644.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no na ga-du tsu-ni-gv-hi hi-s-gi i-ya-ga-yv-li i-yv-dv na-ni-e a-ni-s-ga-ya.

020645.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᏅᏫᏍᏔᏁ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᎾᏦᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏧᏂᏐᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᏇᏣᏱᏗ ᏭᏂᎷᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᏏᏉ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏓᏰᎵᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ.
020645.english: And straightway he constrained his disciples to get into the ship, and to go to the other side before unto Bethsaida, while he sent away the people.
020645.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv du-nv-wi-s-ta-ne gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi tsi-yu-hi u-na-tso-di-yi, a-le i-gv-yi tsu-ni-so-hi-s-di-yi, Que-tsa-yi-di wu-ni-lu-hi-s-di-yi, a-si-quo u-wa-sv da-ye-li-hi-s-di-s-gv u-ni-tsa-ti.

020646.english: And when he had sent them away, he departed into a mountain to pray.

020647.cherokee: ᎤᏒᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᏥᏳ ᎥᏓᎵ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎠᏂᏂᏎᎢ, ᎤᏩᏒᏃ ᏙᏱ ᎡᏙᎮᎢ.
020647.english: And when even was come, the ship was in the midst of the sea, and he alone on the land.
020647.phonetic: U-sv-no nu-li-s-ta-nv tsi-yu v-da-li a-ye-li a-ni-ni-se-i, u-wa-sv-no do-yi e-do-he-i.

020648.cherokee: ᏚᎪᎮᏃ ᎠᏂᎩᎵᏲᎬ ᎠᏂᎦᏪᎯᎲᎢ; ᏩᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎢᏗᏏ ᏗᎦᏃᎸᏗᏍᎨᎢ.  ᏅᎩᏁᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎬ ᏒᏃᏱ ᏚᎷᏤᎴᎢ, ᎥᏓᎵ ᎦᏚᎢ ᎠᎢᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏱᏚᎶᎯᏎᎴᎢ.
020648.english: And he saw them toiling in rowing; for the wind was contrary unto them: and about the fourth watch of the night he cometh unto them, walking upon the sea, and would have passed by them.
020648.phonetic: Du-go-he-no a-ni-gi-li-yo-gv a-ni-ga-we-hi-hv-i; wa-ni-ga-dv-no i-di-si di-ga-no-lv-di-s-ge-i.  Nv-gi-ne-no i-yv-dv a-ni-ya-wi-s-gv sv-no-yi du-lu-tse-le-i, v-da-li ga-du-i a-i-se-i, a-le yi-du-lo-hi-se-le-i.

020649.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏫᎬᏩᎪᎲ ᎥᏓᎵ ᎦᏚᎢ ᎠᎢᏒᎢ, ᎤᏤᎸᏅᎯ ᎤᏁᎵᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎷᏁᎢ.
020649.english: But when they saw him walking upon the sea, they supposed it had been a spirit, and cried out:
020649.phonetic: A-se-no wi-gv-wa-go-hv v-da-li ga-du-i a-i-sv-i, u-tse-lv-nv-hi u-ne-li-se-i, a-le u-ne-lu-ne-i.

020650.cherokee: (ᏂᎦᏛᏰᏃ ᎬᏩᎪᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏕᏯᏔᏁᎴᎢ.)  ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᎵᏃᎮᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎤᎦᎵᏍᏗᏉ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏓᏓ; ᎠᏴᏉ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏥᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ.
020650.english: For they all saw him, and were troubled. And immediately he talked with them, and saith unto them, Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid.
020650.phonetic: (Ni-ga-dv-ye-no gv-wa-go-he-i, a-le u-na-de-ya-ta-ne-le-i.)  Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv du-li-no-he-ta-ne-i, a-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, U-ga-li-s-di-quo i-tsa-da-nv-da-da; a-yv-quo; tle-s-di yi-tsi-s-ga-i-he-s-di.

020651.cherokee: ᎤᎩᎳᏫᏎᏃ ᏫᏚᏣᏁᎴ ᏥᏳᎯ; ᎦᏃᎸᎥᏍᎬᏃ ᎤᏎᎵᎪᏤᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᎶᏒᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏖᏔᏁ ᏙᏧᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ.
020651.english: And he went up unto them into the ship; and the wind ceased: and they were sore amazed in themselves beyond measure, and wondered.
020651.phonetic: U-gi-la-wi-se-no wi-du-tsa-ne-le tsi-yu-hi; ga-no-lv-v-s-gv-no u-se-li-go-tse-i; a-le u-lo-sv-s-ta-nv-hi u-na-da-nv-te-ta-ne do-tsu-na-da-nv-dv-i, a-le u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i.

020652.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᏱᏚᎾᏓᏅᏖᎴ ᎦᏚ; ᏧᏂᎾᏫᏰᏃ ᏗᏍᏓᏱᏳ ᎨᏎᎢ.
020652.english: For they considered not the miracle of the loaves: for their heart was hardened.
020652.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no yi-du-na-da-nv-te-le ga-du; tsu-ni-na-wi-ye-no di-s-da-yi-yu ge-se-i.

020653.english: And when they had passed over, they came into the land of Gennesaret, and drew to the shore.

020654.english: And when they were come out of the ship, straightway they knew him,

020655.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎬᏩᏚᏫᏛ ᎤᏁᏙᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᏚᏂᏂᏙᎴ ᏗᏤᏍᏙᎩᎯ ᏚᏂᏢᏕ, ᏫᏓᏂᏃᎯᎮ ᎠᎾᏛᎬᎦ ᎾᎿᎭᎡᏙᎲᎢ.
020655.english: And ran through that whole region round about, and began to carry about in beds those that were sick, where they heard he was.
020655.phonetic: A-le na-hna gv-wa-du-wi-dv u-ne-do-le-i, a-le tsu-ni-tlv-gi du-ni-ni-do-le di-tse-s-do-gi-hi du-ni-tlv-de, wi-da-ni-no-hi-he a-na-dv-gv-ga na-hna e-do-hv-i.

020656.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎢᎸᎯᏢ ᏫᎦᎷᎩ, ᏧᏍᏗ ᏕᎦᏚᎲ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏛᎾ ᏕᎦᏚᎲ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏗᏓᏁᎶᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᏕᎦᎳᏅᏛ ᏓᏂᏅᎥᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎮ ᎤᎾᏒᏂᏍᏗᏱ, ᎦᏓᎷᏯᏛᏉ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏄᏮᎢ; ᎾᏂᎥᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏒᏂᎸᎯ ᏚᎾᏗᏩᏍᎨᎢ.
020656.english: And whithersoever he entered, into villages, or cities, or country, they laid the sick in the streets, and besought him that they might touch if it were but the border of his garment: and as many as touched him were made whole.
020656.phonetic: A-le i-lv-hi-tlv wi-ga-lu-gi, tsu-s-di de-ga-du-hv, a-le tsu-dv-na de-ga-du-hv, a-le i-yv-dv di-da-ne-lo-hi ge-sv-i, tsu-ni-tlv-gi de-ga-la-nv-dv da-ni-nv-v-s-ge-i, a-le gv-wa-ta-yo-se-he u-na-sv-ni-s-di-yi, ga-da-lu-ya-dv-quo na-s-quo u-nu-wv-i; na-ni-v-no na-s-gi gv-wa-sv-ni-lv-hi du-na-di-wa-s-ge-i.

020701.cherokee: ᏕᎬᏩᎳᏫᏦᎴᏃ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ, ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏅᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ.
020701.english: Then came together unto him the Pharisees, and certain of the scribes, which came from Jerusalem.
020701.phonetic: De-gv-wa-la-wi-tso-le-no A-ni-qua-li-si, a-le gi-lo i-yu-na-s-gi di-no-we-li-s-gi, Tsi-lu-si-li-mi nv-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi.

020702.cherokee: ᏚᏂᎪᎲᏃ ᎢᎦᏛ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎦᏚ ᎠᏂᎩᏍᎬ ᏗᎦᏓᎭ ᏧᏃᏰᏂ ᎬᏗ, (ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᏚᎾᏑᎴᎲᎾ ᎦᏛᎦ,) ᎤᏂᏐᏅᏤᎴᎢ.
020702.english: And when they saw some of his disciples eat bread with defiled, that is to say, with unwashen, hands, they found fault.
020702.phonetic: Du-ni-go-hv-no i-ga-dv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi ga-du a-ni-gi-s-gv di-ga-da-ha tsu-no-ye-ni gv-di, (na-s-gi ni-du-na-su-le-hv-na ga-dv-ga,) u-ni-so-nv-tse-le-i.

020703.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏᏰᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏂᎥᏉ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏂᏚᎾᏑᎴᎲᎾ ᏱᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎪᎢ, ᏓᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏕᎪ ᏄᏂᏪᏒ ᎡᏘ ᎤᎾᏕᏅᎯ.
020703.english: For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, except they wash their hands oft, eat not, holding the tradition of the elders.
020703.phonetic: A-ni-qua-li-si-ye-no, a-le na-ni-v-quo A-ni-tsu-si, i-yu-no o-s-dv ni-du-na-su-le-hv-na yi-gi, v-tla yi-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-go-i, da-ni-ka-hna-wa-de-go nu-ni-we-sv e-ti u-na-de-nv-hi.

020704.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎦᏃᏙᏗᏱ ᏛᏂᎶᎯ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᏚᎾᏑᎴᎲᎾ ᏱᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᏴᎬᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᎦ. ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏔ ᏄᏓᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏧᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏄᏅᏁᎸ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎵᏍᏈᏗ ᏗᎫᎯᎶᎥᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏖᎵᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏣᏱ ᏗᎪᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᏗᏖᎵᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᏢᏗᏱ.
020704.english: And when they come from the market, except they wash, they eat not. And many other things there be, which they have received to hold, as the washing of cups, and pots, brasen vessels, and of tables.
020704.phonetic: A-le ga-no-do-di-yi dv-ni-lo-hi, i-yu-no ni-du-na-su-le-hv-na yi-gi, v-tla yv-gv-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-ga.  A-le u-tsa-ta nu-da-le go-hu-s-di tsu-ni-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di nu-nv-ne-lv, na-s-gi tsu-li-s-qui-di di-gu-hi-lo-v-i a-le di-te-li-do, a-le v-tsa-yi-di-go-tlv-ta-nv-hi di-te-li-do, a-le di-ni-tlv-di-yi.

020705.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏩᏛᏛᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎨᏣᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏓᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏕᎦ ᎡᏘ ᎤᎾᏕᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᏁᏨᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᏚᎾᏑᎴᎲᎾᏉ ᏣᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎦ?
020705.english: Then the Pharisees and scribes asked him, Why walk not thy disciples according to the tradition of the elders, but eat bread with unwashen hands?
020705.phonetic: Hna-quo-no A-ni-qua-li-si a-le di-no-we-li-s-gi i-gv-wa-dv-dv-ne hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i; Ga-do-no ge-tsa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi v-tla yi-da-ni-ka-hna-wa-de-ga e-ti u-na-de-nv-hi u-ni-ne-tsv-i, na-s-gi ni-du-na-su-le-hv-na-quo tsa-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-ga?

020706.cherokee: ᏚᏁᏤᎸᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᏌᏯ ᏰᎵᏉ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏣᏠᎾᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏁᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎬᏅ ᏥᎪᏪᎳ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏴᏫ ᎬᎩᎸᏉᏗᎭ ᏚᏂᎭᏁᎦᎸ ᏓᏅᏗᎭ, ᏧᏂᎾᏫᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᏅᎯᏳ ᏂᏚᏅᎿᎭᏕᎦ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏆᏛᏅᎢ.
020706.english: He answered and said unto them, Well hath Esaias prophesied of you hypocrites, as it is written, This people honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.
020706.phonetic: Du-ne-tse-lv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, I-sa-ya ye-li-quo nu-we-sv u-do-le-ho-sv ni-hi i-tsa-tlo-na-s-di i-tsi-ne-i-s-di-s-gv-i, hi-a tsi-ni-gv-nv tsi-go-we-la, Hi-a na-s-gi yv-wi gv-gi-lv-quo-di-ha du-ni-ha-ne-ga-lv da-nv-di-ha, tsu-ni-na-wi-s-gi-ni i-nv-hi-yu ni-du-nv-hna-de-ga a-yv na-qua-dv-nv-i.

020707.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ, ᎠᏎᏉᏉ ᎬᎩᎸᏉᏗᎭ ᏥᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎦ ᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬ ᏴᏫᏉ ᎤᏂᏁᏨᎯ.
020707.english: Howbeit in vain do they worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.
020707.phonetic: A-se-no, a-se-quo-quo gv-gi-lv-quo-di-ha tsi-da-na-de-yo-hv-s-ga da-na-de-yo-hv-s-gv yv-wi-quo u-ni-ne-tsv-hi.

020708.cherokee: ᎢᏴᏛᏰᏃ ᏂᏨᏁ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏁᏨᎯ, ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᏁᏨ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏂᏨᏁᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏖᎵᏙ ᎠᎴ ᏧᎵᏍᏈᏗ ᏗᎫᎯᎶᎥᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏔ ᏄᏓᎴᎭ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎤᏠᏱ ᏕᏥᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎰᎢ.
020708.english: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.
020708.phonetic: I-yv-dv-ye-no ni-tsv-ne U-ne-la-nv-hi u-ne-tsv-hi, yv-wi u-ni-ne-tsv di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di ni-tsv-ne-ha, na-s-gi di-te-li-do a-le tsu-li-s-qui-di di-gu-hi-lo-v-i; a-le u-tsa-ta nu-da-le-ha na-s-gi-ya u-tlo-yi de-tsi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ho-i.

020709.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎣᏏᏉ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎭ ᏥᏥᏲᎢᏎᎭ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏁᏨ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏣᏤᎵ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗᏱ.
020709.english: And he said unto them, Full well ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own tradition.
020709.phonetic: A-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, o-si-quo ni-tsa-dv-ne-ha tsi-tsi-yo-i-se-ha U-ne-la-nv-hi u-ne-tsv, na-s-gi i-tsa-tse-li di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di i-tsi-s-qua-ni-go-do-di-yi.

020710.cherokee: ᎼᏏᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎯᎸᏉᏕᏍᏗ ᏣᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᏣᏥ; ᎠᎴ, ᎩᎶ ᎠᏍᎩ ᏅᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ, ᎠᏎ ᎤᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
020710.english: For Moses said, Honour thy father and thy mother; and, Whoso curseth father or mother, let him die the death:
020710.phonetic: Mo-si-ye-no hi-a nu-we-se-i, Hi-lv-quo-de-s-di tsa-do-da a-le tsa-tsi; a-le, Gi-lo a-s-gi nv-di-s-ge-s-di u-do-da a-le u-tsi, a-se u-yo-hu-hi-s-di ge-se-s-di.

020711.cherokee: ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏥᏪᎭ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏎᎮᏍᏗ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ, ᎪᏆᏂ, (ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏛᎬ, ᎠᏆᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅᎯ,) ᏂᎦᎥ ᎨᏣᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏴ ᎬᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬᎢ; [ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏚᏓᎴᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.]
020711.english: But ye say, If a man shall say to his father or mother, It is Corban, that is to say, a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me; he shall be free.
020711.phonetic: Ni-hi-s-gi-ni hi-a ni-tsi-we-ha, I-yu-no gi-lo hi-a ni-ga-we-se-he-s-di u-do-da a-le u-tsi, Go-qua-ni, (na-s-gi ga-dv-gv, a-qua-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv-hi,) ni-ga-v ge-tsa-li-s-de-lv-do-di ge-sv a-yv gv-s-de-li-s-gv-i; [na-s-gi u-du-da-le-dv ge-se-s-di.]

020712.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏫᏓᏛᏂᏏ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ ᏰᏤᎵᏎᎰᎢ;
020712.english: And ye suffer him no more to do ought for his father or his mother;
020712.phonetic: A-le v-tla hna-quo u-ne-la-gi go-hu-s-di wi-da-dv-ni-si u-do-da a-le u-tsi ye-tse-li-se-ho-i;

020713.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎠᏎᏉ ᏂᏨᏁᎰ ᎢᏨᏗᏍᎪ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏥᏁᏨᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎢᏳᏓᎴᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗᏓᏂ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎰᎢ.
020713.english: Making the word of God of none effect through your tradition, which ye have delivered: and many such like things do ye.
020713.phonetic: Na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li ka-no-he-dv a-se-quo ni-tsv-ne-ho i-tsv-di-s-go ni-hi i-tsi-ne-tsv-i; a-le u-tsa-ti i-yu-da-le-gi na-s-gi i-yu-s-di-da-ni ni-tsa-dv-ne-ho-i.

020714.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᏴᏫ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎲ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏍᎩᏯᏛᏓᏍᏓᏏ ᏂᏥᎥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏦᎵᎩ;
020714.english: And when he had called all the people unto him, he said unto them, Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand:
020714.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no yv-wi wi-du-ya-nv-hv, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, S-gi-ya-dv-da-s-da-si ni-tsi-v-i, a-le i-tso-li-gi;

020715.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏴᏫᎯ ᏙᏱᏗᏢ ᎡᎯ, ᎤᏴᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏱᏄᏩᏁᎰᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏂ ᏅᏓᏳᏄᎪᏨᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏄᏩᏁᎰ ᏴᏫ.
020715.english: There is nothing from without a man, that entering into him can defile him: but the things which come out of him, those are they that defile the man.
020715.phonetic: V-tla-no go-hu-s-di yv-wi-hi do-yi-di-tlv e-hi, u-yv-ha, na-s-gi ga-da-ha yi-nu-wa-ne-ho-i; na-s-gi-ni nv-da-yu-nu-go-tsv-hi, na-s-gi ga-da-ha nu-wa-ne-ho yv-wi.

020716.english: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.

020717.cherokee: ᎦᎵᏦᏕᏃ ᏭᏴᎸ ᏚᏓᏅᎡᎸ ᏴᏫ, ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎬᏩᏛᏛᏁ ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
020717.english: And when he was entered into the house from the people, his disciples asked him concerning the parable.
020717.phonetic: Ga-li-tso-de-no wu-yv-lv du-da-nv-e-lv yv-wi, gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi gv-wa-dv-dv-ne da-tli-lo-s-dv u-gv-wa-li.

020718.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎪ ᏂᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᎢ ᏂᏦᎵᎬᎾ ᎢᎩ?  ᏝᏍᎪ ᏱᏥᎪᏩᏘᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏙᏱᏗᏢ ᎡᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏳᏴᎯᎭ ᏴᏫ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎢᎬᏩᏁᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ;
020718.english: And he saith unto them, Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, it cannot defile him;
020718.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, Na-s-quo-s-go ni-hi na-s-gi i-ga-i ni-tso-li-gv-na i-gi?  Tla-s-go yi-tsi-go-wa-ti-ha, na-s-gi go-hu-s-di do-yi-di-tlv e-hi na-s-gi yu-yv-hi-ha yv-wi ga-da-ha i-gv-wa-ne-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i;

020719.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏫᏱ ᏫᎾᏴᎯᎲᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎤᏍᏉᎵᏱᏉᏍᎩᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᏉ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎦᏅᎦᎵᏍᎬ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ?
020719.english: Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats?
020719.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-na-wi-yi wi-na-yv-hi-hv-na ge-sv i-yu-s-di, u-s-quo-li-yi-quo-s-gi-ni, a-le wi-ga-lo-hi-s-di-s-gv-quo, na-s-gi-no ga-nv-ga-li-s-gv ni-ga-v a-li-s-da-yv-di?

020720.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏴᏫᎯ ᎤᏄᎪᏨᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏄᏩᏁᎰ ᏴᏫ.
020720.english: And he said, That which cometh out of the man, that defileth the man.
020720.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-i, Na-s-gi na yv-wi-hi u-nu-go-tsv-hi na-s-gi ga-da-ha nu-wa-ne-ho yv-wi.

020721.cherokee: ᎭᏫᏂᏰᏃ ᏅᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᎯ, ᏴᏫ ᎤᎾᏫᏱ, ᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎦ ᎤᏲ ᎠᏓᏅᏖᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᏓᏲᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏗᏂᏏᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᏓᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ,
020721.english: For from within, out of the heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications, murders,
020721.phonetic: Ha-wi-ni-ye-no nv-da-yu-da-le-nv-hi, yv-wi u-na-wi-yi, di-da-le-hv-s-ga u-yo a-da-nv-te-di ge-sv-i, a-da-yo-ne-di ge-sv-i, u-de-li-dv di-ni-si-di ge-sv-i, a-da-hi-s-di ge-sv-i,

020722.cherokee: ᎦᏃᏍᎩᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎠᎬᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏲ ᎢᏯᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎦᎶᏄᎮᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎵᏐᏢᎢᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᏛᏳᎨᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᏓᏐᏢᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᏢᏉᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᎵᏍᎦᎿᎭᏫᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020722.english: Thefts, covetousness, wickedness, deceit, lasciviousness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness:
020722.phonetic: Ga-no-s-gi-dv ge-sv-i, tsu-gv-wa-lo-di a-gv-v-hi-s-di ge-sv-i, u-yo i-ya-dv-ne-di ge-sv-i, ga-lo-nu-he-dv ge-sv-i, a-li-so-tlv-i-s-do-di ge-sv-i, a-dv-yu-ge-di ge-sv-i, a-da-so-tlv-i-s-do-di-yi, a-tlv-quo-do-di-yi, a-li-s-ga-hna-wi-s-di ge-sv-i.

020723.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏲᎢ ᎭᏫᏂ ᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏄᏩᏁᎰ ᏴᏫ.
020723.english: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.
020723.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi ni-ga-dv u-yo-i ha-wi-ni di-da-le-hv-s-go-i, a-le ga-da-ha nu-wa-ne-ho yv-wi.

020724.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏚᎴᏅ, ᏔᏯ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏙᏂ ᎠᏍᏛ ᏭᎷᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᏭᏴᎸ, ᎥᏝ ᏳᏚᎵᏍᎨ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎬᏩᏗᏍᎦᎶᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ.
020724.english: And from thence he arose, and went into the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and entered into an house, and would have no man know it: but he could not be hid.
020724.phonetic: Na-hna-no du-le-nv, Ta-ya a-le Sa-do-ni a-s-dv wu-lu-tse-i, a-le a-da-ne-lv wu-yv-lv, v-tla yu-du-li-s-ge gi-lo u-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi; a-se-no v-tla gv-wa-di-s-ga-lo-di yi-ge-se-i.

020725.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏛᏄᏣ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏯᎢ, ᎤᏛᎦᏅ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᎷᏤ ᎠᎴ ᏚᎳᏍᎬ ᎤᏓᏅᏁᎢ:
020725.english: For a certain woman, whose young daughter had an unclean spirit, heard of him, and came and fell at his feet:
020725.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no i-yu-s-di a-ge-yv na-s-gi u-we-tsi a-dv-nu-tsa ga-da-ha a-da-nv-do u-ya-i, u-dv-ga-nv a-tsi-no-he-s-gv-i, u-lu-tse a-le du-la-s-gv u-da-nv-ne-i:

020726.cherokee: (ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎠᎪᎢ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᏌᎶᏈᏂᏏ ᎨᏎᎢ;) ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏍᏗᏰᏔᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏛᏄᏣ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏄᎪᏫᏎᏗᏱ.
020726.english: The woman was a Greek, a Syrophenician by nation; and she besought him that he would cast forth the devil out of her daughter.
020726.phonetic: (Na-s-gi na a-ge-yv A-go-i ge-se-i, A-sa-lo-qui-ni-si ge-se-i;) a-le na-s-gi u-s-di-ye-ta-ne na-s-gi u-we-tsi a-dv-nu-tsa a-s-gi-na u-nu-go-wi-se-di-yi.

020727.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎤᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᎬᏱ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᏫᏓᏃᎸᎯ; ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᎩ, ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᏧᎾᏤᎵ ᎦᏚ ᏗᎩᎡᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎵ ᏫᏓᏗᏁᏗᏱ.
020727.english: But Jesus said unto her, Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children's bread, and to cast it unto the dogs.
020727.phonetic: A-se-no Tsi-sa hi-a ni-u-we-se-le-i, I-gv-yi di-ni-yo-li wi-da-no-lv-hi; v-tla-ye-no o-si-yu yi-gi, di-ni-yo-li tsu-na-tse-li ga-du di-gi-e-di-yi, a-le gi-li wi-da-di-ne-di-yi.

020728.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏁᏤ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎩᎵ ᎦᏍᎩᎸ ᎭᏫᏂᏗᏢ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᎤᏂᏅᎪᎠᎯᏎᎸᎯ ᎦᏚ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏍᎪᎢ.
020728.english: And she answered and said unto him, Yes, Lord: yet the dogs under the table eat of the children's crumbs.
020728.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-ne-tse hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, U-do-hi-yu-hi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi; a-se-no gi-li ga-s-gi-lv ha-wi-ni-di-tlv di-ni-yo-li u-ni-nv-go-a-hi-se-lv-hi ga-du a-na-li-s-da-yv-di-s-go-i.

020729.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᏫ ᏫᏂᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ, ᏥᎮᎾ; ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᏤᏥᏱ ᎠᏯᎥ ᎤᏄᎪᎩ.
020729.english: And he said unto her, For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter.
020729.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-le-i, Na-s-gi hi-a tsi-ni-wi wi-ni-ga-li-s-do-da, tsi-he-na; a-s-gi-na tse-tsi-yi a-ya-v u-nu-go-gi.

020730.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏧᏪᏅᏒ ᏫᎤᎷᏨ, ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏎ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏄᎪᏨᎢ, ᎤᏪᏥᏃ ᎠᏤᏍᏙᎩᎯ ᎦᏅᎬᎢ.
020730.english: And when she was come to her house, she found the devil gone out, and her daughter laid upon the bed.
020730.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no tsu-we-nv-sv wi-u-lu-tsv, u-do-le-ho-se a-s-gi-na u-nu-go-tsv-i, u-we-tsi-no a-tse-s-do-gi-hi ga-nv-gv-i.

020731.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏔᏯ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏙᏂ ᎠᏍᏛ ᎤᏂᎩᏒ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎥᏓᎸ ᏭᎷᏤᎢ, ᎠᏰᎵ ᎤᎶᏎ ᎠᏍᎪᎯ-ᏗᎦᏚᎩᏱ ᏍᎦᏚᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020731.english: And again, departing from the coasts of Tyre and Sidon, he came unto the sea of Galilee, through the midst of the coasts of Decapolis.
020731.phonetic: A-le ta-li-ne Ta-ya a-le Sa-do-ni a-s-dv u-ni-gi-sv Ge-li-li v-da-lv wu-lu-tse-i, a-ye-li u-lo-se A-s-go-hi--di-ga-du-gi-yi s-ga-du-gi ge-sv-i.

020732.cherokee: ᎢᎬᏩᏘᏃᎮᎴᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏧᎵᎡᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏂᎳ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎢᎬᏩᏍᏗᏰᏔᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏏᏔᏗᏍᏗᏱ.
020732.english: And they bring unto him one that was deaf, and had an impediment in his speech; and they beseech him to put his hand upon him.
020732.phonetic: I-gv-wa-ti-no-he-le-no gi-lo tsu-li-e-na, a-le ga-ni-la ga-wo-ni-s-gi; a-le i-gv-wa-s-di-ye-ta-ne na-s-gi u-si-ta-di-s-di-yi.

020733.cherokee: ᎢᏴᏛᏃ ᏭᏘᏅᏍᏔᏅ ᎤᏓᏰᎵᎸ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏁᏙᎲᎢ, ᏕᎦᏰᏌᏛ ᏗᎦᎴᏂ ᏚᏐᎾᏕᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎵᏥᏍᏇᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏃᎪ ᎤᏒᏂᎴᎢ;
020733.english: And he took him aside from the multitude, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spit, and touched his tongue;
020733.phonetic: I-yv-dv-no wu-ti-nv-s-ta-nv u-da-ye-li-lv u-ni-tsa-ti a-ne-do-hv-i, de-ga-ye-sa-dv di-ga-le-ni du-so-na-de-i, a-le du-li-tsi-s-que-i, a-le ga-no-go u-sv-ni-le-i;

020734.cherokee: ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏃ ᏫᏚᎧᎿᎭᏅ ᎤᎵᏰᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎡᏇᏓ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏛᎬ ᎭᎵᏍᏚᎢ.
020734.english: And looking up to heaven, he sighed, and saith unto him, Ephphatha, that is, Be opened.
020734.phonetic: Ga-lv-la-di-no wi-du-ka-hna-nv u-li-ye-ta-ne-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, E-que-da, na-s-gi ga-dv-gv ha-li-s-du-i.

020735.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏗᎦᎴᏂ ᏚᎵᏍᏚᎢᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏃᎪ ᎤᎸᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᏚᎵᎧᏁᏴᎮ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏬᏂᏎᎢ.
020735.english: And straightway his ears were opened, and the string of his tongue was loosed, and he spake plain.
020735.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv di-ga-le-ni du-li-s-du-i-se-i, a-le ga-no-go u-lv-s-da-ne-lv-hi du-li-ka-ne-yv-he, a-le o-s-dv u-wo-ni-se-i.

020736.cherokee: ᏚᏅᏍᏓᏕᎴᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏂᏃᏁᏗᏱ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏕᎦᏅᏍᏓᏗᏏ ᎤᏟᏉ ᎢᎦᎢ ᏓᏂᏰᎵᎯᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ;
020736.english: And he charged them that they should tell no man: but the more he charged them, so much the more a great deal they published it;
020736.phonetic: Du-nv-s-da-de-le-no gi-lo u-ni-no-ne-di-yi; a-se-no de-ga-nv-s-da-di-si u-tli-quo i-ga-i da-ni-ye-li-hi-s-di-s-ge-i;

020737.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᎶᏒᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ, ᏂᎦᏛ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏕᎤᎸᏫᏍᏓᏏ; ᎤᎾᏛᎪᏗᏱ ᏂᏕᎬᏁ ᏧᏂᎵᎡᎾ, ᏧᏅᎨᏫᏃ ᎤᏂᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
020737.english: And were beyond measure astonished, saying, He hath done all things well: he maketh both the deaf to hear, and the dumb to speak.
020737.phonetic: A-le u-lo-sv-s-ta-nv-hi u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i, hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i, Ni-ga-dv o-si-yu de-u-lv-wi-s-da-si; u-na-dv-go-di-yi ni-de-gv-ne tsu-ni-li-e-na, tsu-nv-ge-wi-no u-ni-wo-ni-hi-s-di-yi.

020801.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒ ᏴᏫ ᏧᏈᏯ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏂᎲᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ, ᏥᏌ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎲ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ;
020801.english: In those days the multitude being very great, and having nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples unto him, and saith unto them,
020801.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-no u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv yv-wi tsu-qui-ya ge-sv i-yu-s-di, a-le nu-ni-hv-na ge-sv u-na-li-s-da-yv-di, Tsi-sa wi-du-ya-nv-hv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i;

020802.cherokee: ᎦᏥᏯᏙᎵᎦ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏦᎢ ᎢᎦ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎣᎨᏙᎸᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏄᏂᎲᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ;
020802.english: I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat:
020802.phonetic: Ga-tsi-ya-do-li-ga u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv-i, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di hna-quo tso-i i-ga i-tsu-la-ha o-ge-do-lv-i a-le nu-ni-hv-na ge-sv u-na-li-s-da-yv-di;

020803.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᏱᎦᏥᏯᏂᎩᏍᏗᎭ ᏙᏧᏁᏅᏒ ᏯᏁᎦ ᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅᎾ, ᏩᎾᎢᏒᏉ ᏱᏓᏂᏩᎾᎦᎶᎩ; ᎢᎦᏛᏰᏃ ᎢᏅᎯᏳ ᏙᏧᏁᏅ.
020803.english: And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.
020803.phonetic: I-yu-no yi-ga-tsi-ya-ni-gi-s-di-ha do-tsu-ne-nv-sv ya-ne-ga nu-na-li-s-da-yv-nv-na, wa-na-i-sv-quo yi-da-ni-wa-na-ga-lo-gi; i-ga-dv-ye-no i-nv-hi-yu do-tsu-ne-nv.

020804.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎭᏢ ᏱᏓᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎦᏚ ᎠᏂ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᏂᏛᏓᏁᏗ.
020804.english: And his disciples answered him, From whence can a man satisfy these men with bread here in the wilderness?
020804.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i, Ha-tlv yi-da-gi gi-lo na-s-gi i-ga-i ga-du a-ni i-na-ge-i, hi-a na-s-gi ye-li gv-wa-ni-dv-da-ne-di.

020805.cherokee: ᏚᏛᏛᏁᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎢᎳᎪ ᎦᏚ ᏕᏥᏰᎭ?  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᎦᎵᏉᎩ.
020805.english: And he asked them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven.
020805.phonetic: Du-dv-dv-ne-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; I-la-go ga-du de-tsi-ye-ha?  Hi-a-no nu-ni-we-se-i, Ga-li-quo-gi.

020806.cherokee: ᏚᏁᏤᎴᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎦᏙᎯ ᎤᎾᏂᏢᏗᏱ; ᎦᎵᏉᎩᏃ ᎦᏚ ᏚᎩᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏨ, ᏚᎬᎭᎷᏰᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏚᏁᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ ᏧᏂᏁᏗᏱ; ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢᏃ ᏚᏂᏁᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ.
020806.english: And he commanded the people to sit down on the ground: and he took the seven loaves, and gave thanks, and brake, and gave to his disciples to set before them; and they did set them before the people.
020806.phonetic: Du-ne-tse-le-no u-ni-tsa-ti ga-do-hi u-na-ni-tlv-di-yi; ga-li-quo-gi-no ga-du du-gi-sv, a-le u-li-he-li-tsv, du-gv-ha-lu-ye-i, a-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi du-ne-le na-s-gi i-gv-yi-di-tlv tsu-ni-ne-di-yi; i-gv-yi-di-tlv-no du-ni-ne-le u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi.

020807.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᏧᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏣᏗ ᏓᏂᏁᎮᎢ, ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏨᏃ ᎤᏁᏤ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ ᏧᏂᏅᏗᏱ.
020807.english: And they had a few small fishes: and he blessed, and commanded to set them also before them.
020807.phonetic: A-le i-lv-s-gi tsu-na-s-di a-tsa-di da-ni-ne-he-i, u-li-he-li-tsv-no u-ne-tse na-s-gi na-s-quo i-gv-yi-di-tlv tsu-ni-nv-di-yi.

020808.cherokee: ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏁᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏃᎸᏎᎢ; ᎤᏄᏖᏎᏃ ᎤᎵᎬᎭᎷᏴᎯ ᎤᏘᏴᎯ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎢᏯᎧᎵᎢ ᏔᎷᏣ.
020808.english: So they did eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets.
020808.phonetic: U-na-li-s-da-yv-ne-no, a-le du-no-lv-se-i; u-nu-te-se-no u-li-gv-ha-lu-yv-hi u-ti-yv-hi ga-li-quo-gi i-ya-ka-li-i ta-lu-tsa.

020809.cherokee: ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅᎯᏃ ᏅᎩ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎾᏂᎡᎢ; ᏚᏰᎵᎯᏍᏔᏁᏃ.
020809.english: And they that had eaten were about four thousand: and he sent them away.
020809.phonetic: U-na-li-s-da-yv-nv-hi-no nv-gi i-ya-ga-yv-li i-yv-dv na-ni-e-i; du-ye-li-hi-s-ta-ne-no.

020810.cherokee: ᎢᎬᏪᏅᏛᏃ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᏣᏁ ᎠᏁᎮ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎵᎻᏄᏓ ᎠᏍᏛ ᏭᎷᏤᎢ.
020810.english: And straightway he entered into a ship with his disciples, and came into the parts of Dalmanutha.
020810.phonetic: I-gv-we-nv-dv-no tsi-yu-hi u-tsa-ne a-ne-he gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, a-le Da-li-mi-nu-da a-s-dv wu-lu-tse-i.

020811.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏᏃ ᎤᏂᎷᏤ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᎴᏅᎮ ᎬᏩᏛᏛᎮᎸᏁᎢ, ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᏧᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᏗᏱ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ, ᎬᏩᎪᎵᏰᏍᎨᎢ.
020811.english: And the Pharisees came forth, and began to question with him, seeking of him a sign from heaven, tempting him.
020811.phonetic: A-ni-qua-li-si-no u-ni-lu-tse a-le u-na-le-nv-he gv-wa-dv-dv-he-lv-ne-i, gv-wa-ta-yo-se-le tsu-na-nu-go-wi-se-di-yi u-ye-lv-dv ga-lv-la-di nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi, gv-wa-go-li-ye-s-ge-i.

020812.cherokee: ᎤᏓᏅᏙᎩᎯᏃ ᎭᏫᏂ ᎤᎵᏰᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎪᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏣᏁᎭ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎤᏂᏲᎭ?  ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᎨᎬᏁᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎯᎠ ᎪᎯ ᏣᏁᎭ.
020812.english: And he sighed deeply in his spirit, and saith, Why doth this generation seek after a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.
020812.phonetic: U-da-nv-do-gi-hi-no ha-wi-ni u-li-ye-ta-ne-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-i, Ga-do-no go-hi hi-a tsa-ne-ha u-ye-lv-dv u-ni-yo-ha?  u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, v-tla gv-ni-ge-sv i-ge-gv-ne-di yi-ge-se-s-di u-ye-lv-dv hi-a go-hi tsa-ne-ha.

020813.cherokee: ᏚᏓᏅᎡᎸᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᏣᏅ, ᎠᏍᎪᎾ ᏭᎶᏎᎢ.
020813.english: And he left them, and entering into the ship again departed to the other side.
020813.phonetic: Du-da-nv-e-lv-no, a-le ta-li-ne tsi-yu-hi u-tsa-nv, a-s-go-na wu-lo-se-i.

020814.cherokee: [ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ] ᎤᏅᎨᏫᏒᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎦᏚ ᏧᎾᏕᏅᏗᏱ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᏳᎶᏒᏍᏕ ᏌᏉ ᎦᏚ ᎤᏂᎸ ᏥᏳᎯ.
020814.english: Now the disciples had forgotten to take bread, neither had they in the ship with them more than one loaf.
020814.phonetic: [Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no] u-nv-ge-wi-sv-hi ge-se ga-du tsu-na-de-nv-di-yi, v-tla a-le yu-lo-sv-s-de sa-quo ga-du u-ni-lv tsi-yu-hi.

020815.cherokee: ᏚᏁᏤᎴᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎢᏥᏯᏫᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᎠᎪᏙᏗ ᎢᏤᏯᏙᏤᎮᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᏗ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎠᎪᏙᏗ.
020815.english: And he charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod.
020815.phonetic: Du-ne-tse-le-no hi-a nu-we-se-i, I-tsi-ya-wi-s-ge-s-di, A-ni-qua-li-si u-na-tse-li a-go-do-di i-tse-ya-do-tse-he-s-di, a-le e-lo-di u-tse-li a-go-do-di.

020816.cherokee: ᎤᏅᏒᏃ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᎵᏃᎮᎴ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᎦᏚ ᏂᏗᏗᏰᎲᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020816.english: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have no bread.
020816.phonetic: U-nv-sv-no ge-sv u-na-li-no-he-le, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i, Nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha ga-du ni-di-di-ye-hv-na ge-sv-i.

020817.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᏣᎵᏃᎮᎭ ᎦᏚ ᏂᏗᏥᏰᎲᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ?  ᎠᏏᏉᏍᎪ ᏂᏣᏙᎴᎰᏍᎬᎾ ᎢᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᏦᎵᎬᎾ ᎢᎩ?  ᎠᏏᏉᏍᎪ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᏂᏕᏨᏅ ᏗᏥᎾᏫ?
020817.english: And when Jesus knew it, he saith unto them, Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? have ye your heart yet hardened?
020817.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-do-le-ho-sv hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Ga-do-no i-tsa-li-no-he-ha ga-du ni-di-tsi-ye-hv-na ge-sv i-yu-s-di?  A-si-quo-s-go ni-tsa-do-le-ho-s-gv-na i-gi, a-le ni-tso-li-gv-na i-gi?  A-si-quo-s-go a-s-da-ya ni-de-tsv-nv di-tsi-na-wi?

020818.cherokee: ᏕᏥᎧᏅᏍᎪ, ᏂᏥᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬᎾᏃ ᎢᎩ?  ᏕᏥᎵᎷᎦᏍᎪ, ᏂᏣᏛᎩᏍᎬᎾᏃ ᎢᎩ?  ᎠᎴ ᏂᏣᏅᏓᏗᏍᎬᎾᏍᎪ ᎢᎩ?
020818.english: Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?
020818.phonetic: De-tsi-ka-nv-s-go, ni-tsi-go-wa-ti-s-gv-na-no i-gi?  De-tsi-li-lu-ga-s-go, ni-tsa-dv-gi-s-gv-na-no i-gi?  A-le ni-tsa-nv-da-di-s-gv-na-s-go i-gi?

020819.cherokee: ᎯᏍᎩ ᎦᏚ ᏓᎩᎬᎭᎷᏴ ᎯᏍᎩ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᏕᏥᏁᎸ, ᎢᎳᎪ ᎢᏯᎧᎵᎢ ᏔᎷᏣ ᎤᎵᎬᎭᎷᏴᎯ ᎢᏧᏖᏎᎢ?  ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᎬᏬᏎᎴᎢ.
020819.english: When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? They say unto him, Twelve.
020819.phonetic: Hi-s-gi ga-du da-gi-gv-ha-lu-yv hi-s-gi i-ya-ga-yv-li de-tsi-ne-lv, i-la-go i-ya-ka-li-i ta-lu-tsa u-li-gv-ha-lu-yv-hi i-tsu-te-se-i?  Ta-la-du i-gv-wo-se-le-i.

020820.cherokee: ᎦᎵᏉᎩᏃ [ᎦᏚ] ᏅᎩ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᏕᏥᏁᎸ, ᎢᎳᎪ ᎢᏯᎧᎵᎢ ᏔᎷᏣ ᎤᎵᎬᎭᎷᏴᎯ ᎢᏧᏖᏎᎢ?  ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎤᎾᏛᏁᎢ.
020820.english: And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? And they said, Seven.
020820.phonetic: Ga-li-quo-gi-no [ga-du] nv-gi i-ya-ga-yv-li de-tsi-ne-lv, i-la-go i-ya-ka-li-i ta-lu-tsa u-li-gv-ha-lu-yv-hi i-tsu-te-se-i?  Ga-li-quo-gi u-na-dv-ne-i.

020821.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙ ᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᏂᏦᎵᎬᎾ ᏥᎩ?
020821.english: And he said unto them, How is it that ye do not understand?
020821.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, Ga-do ga-li-s-do-di-ha ni-tso-li-gv-na tsi-gi?

020822.cherokee: ᏇᏣᏱᏗᏃ ᏭᎷᏤᎢ; ᏗᎨᏫᏃ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎬᏩᏘᏃᎮᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏒᏂᏍᏗᏱ.
020822.english: And he cometh to Bethsaida; and they bring a blind man unto him, and besought him to touch him.
020822.phonetic: Que-tsa-yi-di-no wu-lu-tse-i; di-ge-wi-no a-s-ga-ya gv-wa-ti-no-he-le-i, a-le u-ni-ta-yo-se-le na-s-gi u-sv-ni-s-di-yi.

020823.cherokee: ᏗᎨᏫᏃ ᎤᏬᏯᏁᏒ, ᎦᏚᎲ ᎤᏄᎪᏫᏎᎢ; ᏗᎦᏙᎵᏃ ᏚᎵᏥᏍᏋ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏏᏔᏛ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᎯᎪᏩᏛᏗᎭ?  ᎤᏬᏎᎴᎢ.
020823.english: And he took the blind man by the hand, and led him out of the town; and when he had spit on his eyes, and put his hands upon him, he asked him if he saw ought.
020823.phonetic: Di-ge-wi-no u-wo-ya-ne-sv, ga-du-hv u-nu-go-wi-se-i; di-ga-do-li-no du-li-tsi-s-quv, a-le du-si-ta-dv, go-hu-s-di-s-go hi-go-wa-dv-di-ha?  U-wo-se-le-i.

020824.cherokee: ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏅᏃ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᏴᏫ ᎦᏥᎪᏩᏘᎭ ᎠᏁᎩᎭ ᏕᏡᎬ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ.
020824.english: And he looked up, and said, I see men as trees, walking.
020824.phonetic: Du-ka-hna-nv-no, hi-a nu-we-se-i, Yv-wi ga-tsi-go-wa-ti-ha a-ne-gi-ha de-tlu-gv na-s-gi-ya-i.

020825.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏗᎦᏙᎵ ᏚᏏᏔᏕᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎧᏃᏗᏱ ᏄᏩᏁᎴᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏗᏩᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏓᎪᏩᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
020825.english: After that he put his hands again upon his eyes, and made him look up: and he was restored, and saw every man clearly.
020825.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ta-li-ne di-ga-do-li du-si-ta-de-i, a-le tsu-ka-no-di-yi nu-wa-ne-le-i; a-le u-di-wa-se-i, a-le na-ni-v o-si-yu da-go-wa-di-s-ge-i.

020826.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏤᎴ ᏧᏪᏅᏒ ᏭᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᏫᏣᏴᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎡᎯ ᎯᏃᏁᎸᎩ.
020826.english: And he sent him away to his house, saying, Neither go into the town, nor tell it to any in the town.
020826.phonetic: A-le u-ne-tse-le tsu-we-nv-sv wu-lo-hi-s-di-yi, hi-a nu-we-se-i, Tle-s-di di-ga-du-hv wi-tsa-yv-lv-gi, a-le tle-s-di gi-lo ga-du-hv e-hi hi-no-ne-lv-gi.

020827.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏄᎪᏤ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᏈᎵᎩ ᏕᎦᏚᎲ ᏭᏂᎶᏎᎢ; ᏩᎾᎢᏒᏃ ᏚᏛᏛᏁ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᎪ ᎬᏉᏎᎰ ᏴᏫ?
020827.english: And Jesus went out, and his disciples, into the towns of Caesarea Philippi: and by the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, Whom do men say that I am?
020827.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-nu-go-tse a-le na-s-gi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, Si-sa-li-yi Qui-li-gi de-ga-du-hv wu-ni-lo-se-i; wa-na-i-sv-no du-dv-dv-ne gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Ga-go gv-quo-se-ho yv-wi?

020828.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᏣᏂ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ; ᎢᎦᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᎳᏯ [ᎠᎾᏗᎭ;] ᎢᎦᏛᏃ, ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ.
020828.english: And they answered, John the Baptist: but some say, Elias; and others, One of the prophets.
020828.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-ni-we-se-i, Tsa-ni Di-da-wo-s-gi; i-ga-dv-s-gi-ni I-la-ya [a-na-di-ha;] i-ga-dv-no, Gi-lo i-yu-s-di a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi.

020829.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏂᎯᎾᏃ ᎦᎪ ᏍᎩᏲᏎᎭ?  ᏈᏓᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏂᎯ.
020829.english: And he saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am? And Peter answereth and saith unto him, Thou art the Christ.
020829.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, Ni-hi-na-no ga-go s-gi-yo-se-ha?  Qui-da-no u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, Ga-lo-ne-dv ni-hi.

020830.english: And he charged them that they should tell no man of him.

020831.cherokee: ᎤᎴᏅᎮᏃ ᏚᏪᏲᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎤᎩ ᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗᏱ; ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏲᎢᏎᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏦᎢᏁᏃ ᎢᎦ ᏧᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020831.english: And he began to teach them, that the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders, and of the chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
020831.phonetic: U-le-nv-he-no du-we-yo-ne-i, na-s-gi yv-wi U-we-tsi u-tsa-ti tsu-da-le-nv-dv u-gi li-yo-i-s-di-yi; a-le gv-wa-yo-i-se-di ge-sv di-ni-la-wi-gi, a-le nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi a-le di-no-we-li-s-gi, a-le a-tsi-i-s-di-yi ge-sv-i, tso-i-ne-no i-ga tsu-le-hi-so-di ge-sv-i.

020832.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ.  ᏈᏓᏃ ᎤᏯᏅᎲ ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᎤᎬᏍᎪᎸᏁᎢ.
020832.english: And he spake that saying openly. And Peter took him, and began to rebuke him.
020832.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a gv-ni-ge-sv nu-we-se-i.  Qui-da-no u-ya-nv-hv u-le-nv-he u-gv-s-go-lv-ne-i.

020833.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᎦᏔᎲᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏅ, ᏈᏓ ᎤᎬᏍᎪᎸᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎠᏆᏐᎭᏛ ᎭᎴᎲᎦ ᏎᏓᏂ; ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎨᏒ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᏣᏰᎸᎭ, ᏴᏫᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020833.english: But when he had turned about and looked on his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.
020833.phonetic: A-se-no u-ga-ta-hv-sv, a-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi du-ka-hna-nv, Qui-da u-gv-s-go-lv-ne-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i, A-qua-so-ha-dv ha-le-hv-ga Se-da-ni; v-tla-ye-no U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li ge-sv o-si-yu yi-tsa-ye-lv-ha, yv-wi-s-gi-ni u-na-tse-li ge-sv-i.

020834.cherokee: ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎲᏃ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎩᎶ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏍᏗᏱ, ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᏓᏓᏱᎮᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏧᏓᎾᏩᏛ ᎠᏱᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎩᏍᏓᏩᏕᎨᏍᏗ.
020834.english: And when he had called the people unto him with his disciples also, he said unto them, Whosoever will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
020834.phonetic: Wi-du-ya-nv-hv-no yv-wi, a-le na-s-quo gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Gi-lo u-du-li-s-ge-s-di a-gi-s-da-wa-dv-s-di-yi, u-wa-sv a-da-da-yi-he-s-di, a-le u-wa-sv u-tse-li tsu-da-na-wa-dv a-yi-s-ge-s-di, a-le a-gi-s-da-wa-de-ge-s-di.

020835.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎬᏅ ᎤᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲᎱᏎᎮᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂ ᎬᏅ ᎤᏲᎱᏎᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
020835.english: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospel's, the same shall save it.
020835.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no u-du-li-s-ge-s-di gv-nv u-s-de-lv-di-yi, na-s-gi u-yo-hu-se-he-s-di; gi-lo-s-gi-ni gv-nv u-yo-hu-se-he-s-di a-yv a-le o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi a-tsi-s-de-lv-di ge-se-s-di.

020836.cherokee: ᎦᏙᏰᏃ ᏳᏁᏉᏥ ᏴᏫ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏱᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᎤᏩᏒᏃ ᎤᏓᏅᏙ ᏳᏲᎱᏎᎸ?
020836.english: For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?
020836.phonetic: Ga-do-ye-no yu-ne-quo-tsi yv-wi, i-yu-no ni-gv-na-dv e-lo-hi u-tse-li yi-nu-li-s-ta-nv, u-wa-sv-no u-da-nv-do yu-yo-hu-se-lv?

020837.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙ ᏯᎲᎦ ᏴᏫ ᏱᎦᏁᏟᏴᏍᏓ ᎤᏓᏅᏙ?
020837.english: Or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?
020837.phonetic: A-le ga-do ya-hv-ga yv-wi yi-ga-ne-tli-yv-s-da u-da-nv-do?

020838.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎠᏆᏕᎰᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏴ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ ᏥᏁᎬᎢ, ᎤᏓᏑᏴ ᎠᏂ ᏗᎾᏓᏲᏁᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾ ᎠᏁᎲᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᏕᎰᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎦᎷᏨᎭ ᎤᏄᏬᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ.
020838.english: Whosoever therefore shall be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation; of him also shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he cometh in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
020838.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no a-qua-de-ho-s-ge-s-di a-yv, a-le a-yv tsi-ne-gv-i, u-da-su-yv a-ni di-na-da-yo-ne-hi a-le a-ni-s-ga-na a-ne-hv-i, na-s-gi na-s-quo yv-wi U-we-tsi u-de-ho-hi-s-di ge-se-s-di, ga-lu-tsv-ha u-nu-wo-s-de-s-di ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv U-do-da u-tse-li-ga, a-ne-he-s-di ge-tsi-lv-quo-di di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi.

020901.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏂ ᏥᏓᏂᏙᎦ, ᎥᏝ ᏴᏛᎾᏙᎴᎰᏏ ᎠᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎬᏂ ᎤᏂᎪᎲᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎷᏨᎭ.
020901.english: And he said unto them, Verily I say unto you, That there be some of them that stand here, which shall not taste of death, till they have seen the kingdom of God come with power.
020901.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, na-s-gi gi-lo a-ni tsi-da-ni-do-ga, v-tla yv-dv-na-do-le-ho-si a-yo-hu-hi-s-di ge-sv-i, gv-ni u-ni-go-hv-hi ge-se-s-di U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv u-li-ni-gi-di-yu ge-sv ga-lu-tsv-ha.

020902.cherokee: ᏑᏓᎵᏃ ᏫᏄᏒᎸ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏘᏅᏎ ᏈᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏂ, ᎢᏅᏃ ᎢᎦᏘ ᎣᏓᎸ ᏚᎿᎭᎷᏔᏁ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏁᏟᏴᏎ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ.
020902.english: And after six days Jesus taketh with him Peter, and James, and John, and leadeth them up into an high mountain apart by themselves: and he was transfigured before them.
020902.phonetic: Su-da-li-no wi-nu-sv-lv Tsi-sa du-ti-nv-se Qui-da, a-le Tsi-mi, a-le Tsa-ni, i-nv-no i-ga-ti o-da-lv du-hna-lu-ta-ne i-yv-dv u-nv-sv ge-sv-i; a-le u-da-ne-tli-yv-se a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i.

020903.cherokee: ᏧᏄᏬᏃ ᏚᏔᎷᎩᏍᎨᎢ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᏧᏁᎩᏳ ᎨᏎᎢ ᎥᏃᏥ ᏥᏄᏁᎩᏴ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏗᎬᎩᎶᏍᎩ ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎡᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏧᏁᎬ ᏱᏂᏙᎦᎬᎦ.
020903.english: And his raiment became shining, exceeding white as snow; so as no fuller on earth can white them.
020903.phonetic: Tsu-nu-wo-no du-ta-lu-gi-s-ge-i, u-tsa-ti tsu-ne-gi-yu ge-se-i v-no-tsi tsi-nu-ne-gi-yv na-s-gi-ya-i; na-s-gi v-tla gi-lo di-gv-gi-lo-s-gi a-ni e-lo-hi e-hi na-s-gi i-tsu-ne-gv yi-ni-do-ga-gv-ga.

020904.cherokee: ᎬᏂᎨᏒᏃ ᏂᎬᏩᎾᏛᏁᎴ ᎢᎳᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎼᏏ; ᏥᏌᏃ ᎬᏩᎵᏃᎮᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
020904.english: And there appeared unto them Elias with Moses: and they were talking with Jesus.
020904.phonetic: Gv-ni-ge-sv-no ni-gv-wa-na-dv-ne-le I-la-ya a-le Mo-si; Tsi-sa-no gv-wa-li-no-he-di-s-ge-i.

020905.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᏥᏌ, ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᎣᏏᏳ ᎠᏂ ᏥᏕᏙᎭ; ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢ ᏫᏙᏥᎵᏦᏛᎦ; ᏌᏉ ᏂᎯ ᏣᏤᎵᎦ, ᏌᏉᏃ ᎼᏏ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᏌᏉᏃ ᎢᎳᏯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
020905.english: And Peter answered and said to Jesus, Master, it is good for us to be here: and let us make three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.
020905.phonetic: Qui-da-no u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-le Tsi-sa, Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, o-si-yu a-ni tsi-de-do-ha; a-le tso-i wi-do-tsi-li-tso-dv-ga; sa-quo ni-hi tsa-tse-li-ga, sa-quo-no Mo-si u-tse-li-ga, sa-quo-no I-la-ya u-tse-li-ga.

020906.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᏯᎦᏔᎮ ᎢᏳᏪᏍᏗᏱ; ᎤᏣ ᏘᏰᏃ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎮᎢ.
020906.english: For he wist not what to say; for they were sore afraid.

020907.cherokee: ᎤᎶᎩᎸᏃ ᎤᏄᏢᏔᏁᎢ; ᎧᏁᎬᏃ ᎤᎶᎩᎸ ᏧᎾᏄᎪᏤᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᎨᏳᎢ ᎠᏇᏥ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᏣᏛᏓᏍᏓᏏ.
020907.english: And there was a cloud that overshadowed them: and a voice came out of the cloud, saying, This is my beloved Son: hear him.
020907.phonetic: U-lo-gi-lv-no u-nu-tlv-ta-ne-i; ka-ne-gv-no u-lo-gi-lv tsu-na-nu-go-tse-i, hi-a ni-ga-we-s-ge-i, Hi-a na-s-gi tsi-ge-yu-i A-que-tsi; na-s-gi e-tsa-dv-da-s-da-si.

020908.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᎾᎧᎿᎭᏂᏙᎸ, ᎥᏝ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎩᎶ ᏳᏂᎪᎮᎢ, ᏥᏌ ᎤᏩᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏅᏒ.
020908.english: And suddenly, when they had looked round about, they saw no man any more, save Jesus only with themselves.
020908.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv du-na-ka-hna-ni-do-lv, v-tla hna-quo gi-lo yu-ni-go-he-i, Tsi-sa u-wa-sv, a-le u-nv-sv.

020909.cherokee: ᏧᏂᎦᏐᎠᏏᏗᏒᏃ ᎣᏓᎸᎢ, ᏚᏁᏤᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏂᏃᏁᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏂᎪᎲᎢ, ᎬᏂ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏧᎴᎯᏌᏅᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏚᏬᏎᎴᎢ.
020909.english: And as they came down from the mountain, he charged them that they should tell no man what things they had seen, till the Son of man were risen from the dead.
020909.phonetic: Tsu-ni-ga-so-a-si-di-sv-no o-da-lv-i, du-ne-tse-le na-s-gi gi-lo u-ni-no-ne-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na nu-s-dv u-ni-go-hv-i, gv-ni yv-wi U-we-tsi u-yo-hu-sv tsu-le-hi-sa-nv-hi ge-se-s-di du-wo-se-le-i.

020910.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎤᎾᎦᏌᏯᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᏓᎾᏓᏛᏛᎲᏍᎨ ᎦᏛᎬ ᎠᏲᎱᏒ ᏗᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020910.english: And they kept that saying with themselves, questioning one with another what the rising from the dead should mean.
020910.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv u-na-ga-sa-ya-s-ta-ne-i, da-na-da-dv-dv-hv-s-ge ga-dv-gv a-yo-hu-sv di-le-hi-so-di ge-sv-i.

020911.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏛᏛᏁᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᎢᎳᏯ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏓᎦᎷᏥ ᏣᎾᏗᏍᎪᎢ?
020911.english: And they asked him, saying, Why say the scribes that Elias must first come?
020911.phonetic: Gv-wa-dv-dv-ne-no hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i, ga-do-no di-no-we-li-s-gi I-la-ya i-gv-yi da-ga-lu-tsi tsa-na-di-s-go-i?

020912.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᎳᏯ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏬᏢᎯᏐᏗ; ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏅ ᎪᏪᎳ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎤᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏎᏉ ᏓᏰᏥᏰᎸᏂ.
020912.english: And he answered and told them, Elias verily cometh first, and restoreth all things; and how it is written of the Son of man, that he must suffer many things, and be set at nought.
020912.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, I-la-ya u-do-hi-yu-hi i-gv-yi u-lu-hi-s-di, a-le ni-ga-dv u-wo-tlv-hi-so-di; a-le na-s-quo hi-a ni-gv-nv-go-we-la yv-wi U-we-tsi a-tsi-no-he-s-gv-i, na-s-gi u-tsa-ti tsu-da-le-nv-dv u-gi-li-yo-i-s-di ge-se-s-di, a-le a-se-quo da-ye-tsi-ye-lv-ni.

020913.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎢᎳᏯ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎤᎷᏨ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᏂᎬᏩᏁᎸ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᎬᏅ ᎪᏪᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ.
020913.english: But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come, and they have done unto him whatsoever they listed, as it is written of him.
020913.phonetic: A-se-no hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, I-la-ya u-do-hi-yu-hi u-lu-tsv, a-le nu-s-dv u-na-du-li-s-gv ni-gv-wa-ne-lv, na-s-gi-ya ni-gv-nv go-we-lv na-s-gi a-tsi-no-he-s-gv-i.

020914.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎠᏁᏙᎲ ᎤᎷᏨ, ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏚᎪᎮ ᎬᏩᎾᏓᏡᏫᏍᏕᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᎾᏛᏛᎮᎸᎢᏍᎨᎢ.
020914.english: And when he came to his disciples, he saw a great multitude about them, and the scribes questioning with them.
020914.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no a-ne-do-hv u-lu-tsv, u-ni-tsa-ti du-go-he gv-wa-na-da-tlu-wi-s-de-i, a-le di-no-we-li-s-gi gv-wa-na-dv-dv-he-lv-i-s-ge-i.

020915.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏴᏫ, ᎬᏩᎪᎲ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ; ᎬᏩᏗᏝᎣᎮᎸᏃ ᎬᏩᏲᎵᎴᎢ.
020915.english: And straightway all the people, when they beheld him, were greatly amazed, and running to him saluted him.
020915.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv ni-ga-dv yv-wi, gv-wa-go-hv, u-tsa-ti u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i; gv-wa-di-tla-o-he-lv-no gv-wa-yo-li-le-i.

020916.english: And he asked the scribes, What question ye with them?

020917.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏴᏫᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒ ᎨᎳ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᎬᏯᏘᏃᎯᏏ ᎠᏇᏥ, ᎤᏩᎨᏫ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏪᎢ;
020917.english: And one of the multitude answered and said, Master, I have brought unto thee my son, which hath a dumb spirit;
020917.phonetic: A-si-yv-wi-no na-s-gi u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv ge-la u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-i, Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, gv-ya-ti-no-hi-si a-que-tsi, u-wa-ge-wi a-da-nv-do u-we-i;

020918.cherokee: ᎢᎸᎯᏢᏃ ᎤᏱᎶᎯᏏ ᎤᎩᎸᏍᎪᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏓᎭᏬᎢᎰᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎦᎸᏓᎩᏍᎪ ᏕᎦᏄᏙᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎸᏕᎯᎰᎢ; ᎨᏣᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎦᏥᏔᏲᏎᎸ ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏫᏍᏗᏱ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏂᏄᎸᏅᏉ.
020918.english: And wheresoever he taketh him, he teareth him: and he foameth, and gnasheth with his teeth, and pineth away: and I spake to thy disciples that they should cast him out; and they could not.
020918.phonetic: I-lv-hi-tlv-no u-yi-lo-hi-si u-gi-lv-s-go-i; a-le da-ha-wo-i-ho-i, a-le de-ga-lv-da-gi-s-go de-ga-nu-do-gv-i, a-le u-lv-de-hi-ho-i; ge-tsa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no ga-tsi-ta-yo-se-lv u-ni-nu-go-wi-s-di-yi; a-se-no u-ni-nu-lv-nv-quo.

020919.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏤᎸᏃ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, Ᏺ!  ᏂᏦᎯᏳᏒᎾ ᎪᎯ ᏥᏤᎭ!  ᎢᎳᎪ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ ᎢᏨᏰᎳᏗᏙᎮᏍᏗ?  ᎢᎳᎪ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᎢᏨᏰᎵᏎᎮᏍᏗ?  ᎠᏂ ᎯᏯᏘᏃᎦ.
020919.english: He answereth him, and saith, O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him unto me.
020919.phonetic: U-ne-tse-lv-no, hi-a nu-we-se-i, Yo!  ni-tso-hi-yu-sv-na go-hi tsi-tse-ha!  i-la-go i-go-hi-dv i-tsv-ye-la-di-do-he-s-di?  I-la-go i-go-hi-dv u-ne-la-gi i-tsv-ye-li-se-he-s-di?  A-ni hi-ya-ti-no-ga.

020920.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏘᏃᎮᎴᏃ; ᎤᎪᎲᏃ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᎩᎸᏁᎢ; ᎡᎳᏗᏃ ᎤᏅᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎭᏬᎢᎲ ᎠᎵᏗᏒᏂᎮᎢ.
020920.english: And they brought him unto him: and when he saw him, straightway the spirit tare him; and he fell on the ground, and wallowed foaming.
020920.phonetic: U-na-ti-no-he-le-no; u-go-hv-no gi-la-quo i-yv-dv a-da-nv-do u-gi-lv-ne-i; e-la-di-no u-nv-tse-i, a-le da-ha-wo-i-hv a-li-di-sv-ni-he-i.

020921.cherokee: ᎤᏛᏛᏁᏃ ᎤᏙᏓ, ᎢᎳᎪ ᏂᎪᎯᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎢᎬᏩᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ?  ᎤᏬᏎᎴᎢ.  ᎠᏲᎵᏉ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏛᏁᎢ;
020921.english: And he asked his father, How long is it ago since this came unto him? And he said, Of a child.
020921.phonetic: U-dv-dv-ne-no u-do-da, I-la-go ni-go-hi-gi na-s-gi hi-a i-gv-wa-li-s-da-ne-lv-hi?  U-wo-se-le-i.  A-yo-li-quo ge-sv-i, u-dv-ne-i;

020922.cherokee: ᏯᏃᎩᏳᏃ ᎠᏥᎸᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏑᏱ ᎤᏓᎡᎪᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎯᏍᏗᏱ; ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎨᏣᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏍᎩᏍᏙᎵᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏍᎩᏍᏕᎸ.
020922.english: And ofttimes it hath cast him into the fire, and into the waters, to destroy him: but if thou canst do any thing, have compassion on us, and help us.
020922.phonetic: Ya-no-gi-yu-no a-tsi-lv-yi a-le a-su-yi u-da-e-go-i, na-s-gi u-hi-s-di-yi; i-yu-s-gi-ni-no-nv go-hu-s-di ge-tsa-dv-ne-di ge-se-s-di, s-gi-s-do-li-gi, a-le s-gi-s-de-lv.

020923.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎨᏦᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎢᎦᏰᎦᏛᏁᏗ ᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ.
020923.english: Jesus said unto him, If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.
020923.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, I-yu-no ge-tso-hi-yu-di ge-se-s-di, ni-ga-di-yu go-hu-s-di i-ga-ye-ga-dv-ne-di go-hi-yu-hv-s-gi.

020924.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᏲᎵ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎤᏪᎷᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎬᎦᏌᏬᎢᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎪᎢᏳᎲᏍᎦ; ᏍᎩᏍᏕᎸᏏ ᎾᏉᎯᏳᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020924.english: And straightway the father of the child cried out, and said with tears, Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief.
020924.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv a-yo-li u-do-da u-we-lu-ne-i, a-le di-gv-ga-sa-wo-i-hi hi-a nu-we-se-i, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, go-i-yu-hv-s-ga; s-gi-s-de-lv-si na-quo-hi-yu-sv-na ge-sv-i.

020925.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᎾᏁᎷᎩᏍᎬ ᎠᎾᏓᏟᏏᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᏍᎦᏤ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏨᎨᏩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏣᎵᎡᎾ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ, ᎬᏁᏤᎭ, ᎡᎯᏄᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏥᎯᏴᎵ.
020925.english: When Jesus saw that the people came running together, he rebuked the foul spirit, saying unto him, Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.
020925.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-go-hv na-s-gi u-ni-tsa-ti a-na-ne-lu-gi-s-gv a-na-da-tli-si-s-gv-i, u-s-ga-tse ga-da-ha a-da-nv-do, hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, Tsv-ge-wa a-le di-tsa-li-e-na a-da-nv-do, gv-ne-tse-ha, e-hi-nu-go-i, a-le tle-s-di hna-quo i-lv-hi-yu tsi-hi-yv-li.

020926.cherokee: [ᎠᏓᏅᏙᏃ] ᎤᏪᎷᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᎩᎸᏅ, ᎤᏄᎪᏤᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯᏉ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎨᏎᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ, ᎠᏲᎱᎯ, ᎠᎾᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
020926.english: And the spirit cried, and rent him sore, and came out of him: and he was as one dead; insomuch that many said, He is dead.
020926.phonetic: [A-da-nv-do-no] u-we-lu-nv, a-le u-tsa-ti u-gi-lv-nv, u-nu-go-tse-i; a-le u-yo-hu-sv-hi-quo na-s-gi-ya ge-se-i; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-ni-tsa-te, a-yo-hu-hi, a-na-di-s-ge-i.

020927.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏬᏯᏁᏒ, ᏚᎴᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎴᏁᎢ.
020927.english: But Jesus took him by the hand, and lifted him up; and he arose.
020927.phonetic: A-se-no tsi-sa u-wo-ya-ne-sv, du-le-ta-ne-i, a-le du-le-ne-i.

020928.cherokee: ᎦᎵᏦᏕᏃ ᏫᎤᏴᎸ, ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏩᏛᏛᏁᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎠᏴ ᎥᏝ ᎦᏲᏥᏄᎪᏫᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ?  ᎤᎾᏛᏁᎢ.
020928.english: And when he was come into the house, his disciples asked him privately, Why could not we cast him out?
020928.phonetic: Ga-li-tso-de-no wi-u-yv-lv, gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi u-nv-sv ge-sv gv-wa-dv-dv-ne-i, Ga-do-no a-yv v-tla ga-yo-tsi-nu-go-wi-s-di yi-ge-se-i?  U-na-dv-ne-i.

020929.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎥᏝ ᏴᎦᏰᎦᏄᎪᏩ, ᎬᏂ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎬᏅᎯ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ.
020929.english: And he said unto them, This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting.
020929.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, Hi-a na-s-gi i-yu-s-di v-tla yv-ga-ye-ga-nu-go-wa, gv-ni a-da-do-li-s-ta-nv-hi a-le a-ma-tli gv-nv-hi tsi-ge-so-i.

020930.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏂᎩᏒᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᏂ, ᎨᎵᎵ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎤᏂᎶᏎᎢ; ᎥᏝᏃ ᏳᏚᎵᏍᎨ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
020930.english: And they departed thence, and passed through Galilee; and he would not that any man should know it.
020930.phonetic: U-na-ni-gi-sv-no na-hna-ni, Ge-li-li a-ye-li u-ni-lo-se-i; v-tla-no yu-du-li-s-ge gi-lo u-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi.

020931.cherokee: ᏚᏪᏲᏁᏰᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏓᏥᏲᎯ ᏕᎨᏥᏲᎯᏏ ᏴᏫ. ᎠᎴ ᏓᎬᏩᎵ; ᎠᎴ ᎣᏂ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏥᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏙᏛᎠᎴᎯᏌᏂ ᏦᎢᏁ ᎢᎦ.
020931.english: For he taught his disciples, and said unto them, The Son of man is delivered into the hands of men, and they shall kill him; and after that he is killed, he shall rise the third day.
020931.phonetic: Du-we-yo-ne-ye-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, a-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, yv-wi U-we-tsi da-tsi-yo-hi de-ge-tsi-yo-hi-si yv-wi. a-le da-gv-wa-li; a-le o-ni hna-quo a-tsi-lv-hi ge-se-s-di, do-dv-a-le-hi-sa-ni tso-i-ne i-ga.

020932.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏃᎵᏤ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎮ ᎤᎾᏛᏛᏗᏱ.
020932.english: But they understood not that saying, and were afraid to ask him.
020932.phonetic: A-se-no v-tla yu-no-li-tse na-s-gi nu-we-sv a-le a-ni-s-ga-i-he u-na-dv-dv-di-yi.

020933.cherokee: ᎨᏆᏂᏃ ᎤᎷᏥᎴᎢ; ᎦᎵᏦᎦᏃ ᎠᏯᎥ ᏚᏛᏛᏁ, ᎦᏙ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᎢᏨᏒ ᎨᏒ Ꭰ ᏗᏓᎢᏒᎢ.
020933.english: And he came to Capernaum: and being in the house he asked them, What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way?
020933.phonetic: Ge-qua-ni-no u-lu-tsi-le-i; ga-li-tso-ga-no a-ya-v du-dv-dv-ne, Ga-do na-s-gi tsi-tsi-no-he-s-gv i-tsv-sv ge-sv a di-da-i-sv-i.

020934.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎡᎳᏪᏱᏉ ᎤᏅᏎᎢ; ᏗᎾᎢᏒᏃ ᎠᏂᏃᎮᏍᎨ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏟ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020934.english: But they held their peace: for by the way they had disputed among themselves, who should be the greatest.
020934.phonetic: A-se-no e-la-we-yi-quo u-nv-se-i; di-na-i-sv-no a-ni-no-he-s-ge u-nv-sv ge-sv na-s-gi u-tli a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv-i.

020935.cherokee: ᎤᏪᏅᏃ, ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏂᏱ ᎠᎦᎴᏙᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏂᎥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎬᏩᏅᏏᏙᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
020935.english: And he sat down, and called the twelve, and saith unto them, If any man desire to be first, the same shall be last of all, and servant of all.
020935.phonetic: U-we-nv-no, ta-la-du i-ya-ni-dv wi-du-ya-nv-he-i, a-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, i-yu-no gi-lo i-gv-yi i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi u-du-li-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi o-ni-yi a-ga-le-do-di ge-se-s-di na-ni-v-i, a-le na-ni-v gv-wa-nv-si-do-hi ge-se-s-di.

020936.cherokee: ᎠᏲᎵᏃ ᏭᏯᏅᎲ, ᎠᏂᏅ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎤᏪᎧᏁᎢ; ᎤᏄᎶᎸᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ,
020936.english: And he took a child, and set him in the midst of them: and when he had taken him in his arms, he said unto them,
020936.phonetic: A-yo-li-no wu-ya-nv-hv, a-ni-nv a-ye-li u-we-ka-ne-i; u-nu-lo-lv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i,

020937.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᏓᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏴ ᎠᏴ ᏓᏆᏙᎥ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏴ ᏓᏆᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᎠᏴ ᏓᏆᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᏴ ᏱᏓᏆᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ, ᏅᏛᎩᏅᏏᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᏓᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ.
020937.english: Whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name, receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.
020937.phonetic: Gi-lo a-si-yv-wi hi-a i-yu-na-s-di di-ni-yo-li da-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di a-yv a-yv da-qua-do-v nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di, a-yv da-qua-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di; gi-lo-no a-yv da-qua-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di, v-tla a-yv yi-da-qua-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di, nv-dv-gi-nv-si-dv-s-gi-ni da-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di.

020938.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏃ ᎤᏁᏤᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᎣᏥᎪᎲᎩ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏕᎦᏄᎪᏫᏍᎬᎩ ᏕᏣᏙᎥ ᎬᏗᏍᎬᎩ; ᎥᏝᏃ ᏱᎩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎭ; ᎣᏥᏅᏍᏓᏕᎸᎩᏃ ᏂᎩᏍᏓᏩᏕᎬᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
020938.english: And John answered him, saying, Master, we saw one casting out devils in thy name, and he followeth not us: and we forbad him, because he followeth not us.
020938.phonetic: Tsa-ni-no u-ne-tse-le-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i, Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, o-tsi-go-hv-gi a-si-yv-wi a-ni-s-gi-na de-ga-nu-go-wi-s-gv-gi de-tsa-do-v gv-di-s-gv-gi; v-tla-no yi-gi-s-da-wa-di-do-ha; o-tsi-nv-s-da-de-lv-gi-no ni-gi-s-da-wa-de-gv-na ge-sv i-yu-s-di.

020939.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᎡᏥᏅᏍᏓᏕᎸᎩ; ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᏰᎭ ᎩᎶ ᏓᏆᏙᎥ ᎬᏗᏍᎩ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏪᏅᏛ ᎤᏐᏅ ᎬᎩᏁᎢᏍᏙᏗ.
020939.english: But Jesus said, Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me.
020939.phonetic: A-se-no tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-i, Tle-s-di e-tsi-nv-s-da-de-lv-gi; v-tla-ye-no ye-ha gi-lo da-qua-do-v gv-di-s-gi u-s-qua-ni-go-di tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi, na-s-gi i-gv-we-nv-dv u-so-nv gv-gi-ne-i-s-do-di.

020940.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏡᏗᏍᎬᎾ ᏥᎨᏐ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᎵᎪᏁᎯ ᎨᏐᎢ.
020940.english: For he that is not against us is on our part.
020940.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no na-s-gi ni-ga-tlu-di-s-gv-na tsi-ge-so, na-s-gi i-ga-li-go-ne-hi ge-so-i.

020941.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎠᎹ ᎤᎵᏍᏈᏗ ᎠᎧᎵᎢ ᎢᏣᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏁᏁᎯ ᎠᏴ ᏓᏆᏙᎥ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎡᏣᏤᎵ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎢᏨᏲᏎᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᎤᏲᎱᏎᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᎦᎫᏴᎡᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
020941.english: For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.
020941.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no a-ma u-li-s-qui-di a-ka-li-i i-tsa-di-ta-s-di i-tsi-ne-ne-hi a-yv da-qua-do-v nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di, Ga-lo-ne-dv e-tsa-tse-li ge-sv i-yu-s-di, u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya i-tsv-yo-se-ha, v-tla u-yo-hu-se-di yi-ge-se-s-di a-ga-gu-yv-e-di ge-sv-i.

020942.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎯᎠ ᏧᎾᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᎬᏉᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ ᏙᎪᏕᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎤᏟ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎠᏍᏙᏍᎩ ᏅᏯ ᏯᏥᏯᏝᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᏱᏩᎦᏓᎢᏅ.
020942.english: And whosoever shall offend one of these little ones that believe in me, it is better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he were cast into the sea.
020942.phonetic: A-le gi-lo a-si-yv-wi hi-a tsu-na-s-di ge-sv na-s-gi tsi-gv-quo-hi-yu-hv-s-ga do-go-de-s-di-s-ge-s-di, u-tli o-si-yu i-yu-li-s-da-ne-di a-s-do-s-gi nv-ya ya-tsi-ya-tla-nv, a-le a-me-quo-hi yi-wa-ga-da-i-nv.

020943.cherokee: ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᏦᏰᏂ ᏕᏦᏕᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎯᏍᏆᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ; ᎤᏟ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏣᎪᎳᏂ ᎬᏂᏛ ᏗᎨᏒ ᏱᏫᏣᏴᎸ, ᎠᏃ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏗᎨᎰᏯᏗ ᏨᏍᎩᏃ ᏱᏫᏣᏴᎸ, ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏥᎸᏱ ᎦᎬᏜᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ;
020943.english: And if thy hand offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter into life maimed, than having two hands to go into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
020943.phonetic: I-yu a-le tso-ye-ni de-tso-de-s-di-s-ge-s-di, hi-s-qua-li-s-ge-s-di; u-tli o-si-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di tsa-go-la-ni gv-ni-dv di-ge-sv yi-wi-tsa-yv-lv, a-no i-tsu-la di-ge-ho-ya-di tsv-s-gi-no yi-wi-tsa-yv-lv, na-hna a-tsi-lv-yi ga-gv-dla-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na;

020944.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᏥᏍᎪᏴ ᏂᎦᎵᏬᎬᎾ ᏗᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᎸ ᏂᎬᏠᏍᎬᎾ ᏗᎨᏒᎢ.
020944.english: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
020944.phonetic: Na-hna u-na-tse-li tsi-s-go-yv ni-ga-li-wo-gv-na di-ge-sv-i, a-le a-tsi-lv ni-gv-tlo-s-gv-na di-ge-sv-i.

020945.cherokee: ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᏣᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏙᏦᏕᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎯᏍᏆᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ; ᎤᏟᏰᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎯᏲᎤᎵ ᎬᏂᏛ ᏗᎨᏒ ᏱᏫᏣᏴᎸ, ᎠᏃ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏗᎨᏣᎳᏍᎩ ᏨᏍᎩᏃ ᏱᏪᏣᏓᎢᏅ, ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏥᎸᏱ ᎦᎬ ᏜᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ;
020945.english: And if thy foot offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter halt into life, than having two feet to be cast into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
020945.phonetic: I-yu a-le tsa-la-si-de-ni do-tso-de-s-di-s-ge-s-di, hi-s-qua-li-s-ge-s-di; u-tli-ye-no o-si-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di hi-yo-u-li gv-ni-dv di-ge-sv yi-wi-tsa-yv-lv, a-no i-tsu-la di-ge-tsa-la-s-gi tsv-s-gi-no yi-we-tsa-da-i-nv, na-hna a-tsi-lv-yi ga-gv dla-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na;

020946.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᏥᏍᎪᏴ ᏂᎦᎵᏬᎬᎾ ᏗᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᎸ ᏂᎬᏠᏍᎬᎾ.
020946.english: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
020946.phonetic: Na-hna u-na-tse-li tsi-s-go-yv ni-ga-li-wo-gv-na di-ge-sv-i, a-le a-tsi-lv ni-gv-tlo-s-gv-na.

020947.cherokee: ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᎯᎦᏙᎵ ᏙᏦᏕᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎭᏓᎦᏖᏙᎢᏍᎨᏍᏗ; ᎤᏟᏰᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏌᏉᏉ ᏱᎧᏅ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏱᏫᏣᏴᎸ ᎠᏃ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏱᏘᎧᏅ ᏨᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏥᎸᏱ ᏱᏪᏣᏓᎢᏅ;
020947.english: And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out: it is better for thee to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye, than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire:
020947.phonetic: I-yu a-le hi-ga-do-li do-tso-de-s-di-s-ge-s-di, ha-da-ga-te-do-i-s-ge-s-di; u-tli-ye-no o-si-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di sa-quo-quo yi-ka-nv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-go-hi yi-wi-tsa-yv-lv a-no i-tsu-la yi-ti-ka-nv tsv-s-gi-no a-tsi-lv-yi yi-we-tsa-da-i-nv;

020948.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᏥᏍᎪᏴ ᏂᎦᎵᏬᎬᎾ ᏗᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᎸ ᏂᎬᏠᏍᎬᎾ ᏗᎨᏒᎢ.
020948.english: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
020948.phonetic: Na-hna u-na-tse-li tsi-s-go-yv ni-ga-li-wo-gv-na di-ge-sv-i, a-le a-tsi-lv ni-gv-tlo-s-gv-na di-ge-sv-i.

020949.cherokee: ᎾᏂᎥᏰᏃ ᎠᏥᎸ ᎬᏗ ~ᎠᎹ ᎨᏥᏅᏝᏰᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎳᏍᏗ ~ᎠᎹ ᎦᏅᎵᏰᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
020949.english: For every one shall be salted with fire, and every sacrifice shall be salted with salt.
020949.phonetic: Na-ni-v-ye-no a-tsi-lv gv-di ~a-ma ge-tsi-nv-tla-ye-di ge-se-s-di, a-le ni-ga-v a-tsi-lv--ge-la-s-di ~a-ma ga-nv-li-ye-di ge-se-s-di.

020950.cherokee: ᎠᎹ ᎣᏏᏳ; ᎠᏎᏃ ~ᎠᎹ ᏳᏥᏍᎪᎸ ᎦᏙ ᏱᏨᎦ ᏄᏥᏍᎪᎸᎾ ᏱᏂᏨᎦ?  ~ᎠᎹ ᎢᏥᎶᏨᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᎯᏱ ᏂᏨᏁᏍᏗ ᎢᏤᎲᎢ.
020950.english: Salt is good: but if the salt have lost his saltness, wherewith will ye season it? Have salt in yourselves, and have peace one with another.
020950.phonetic: -A-ma o-si-yu; a-se-no ~a-ma yu-tsi-s-go-lv ga-do yi-tsv-ga nu-tsi-s-go-lv-na yi-ni-tsv-ga?  ~A-ma i-tsi-lo-tsv-hi ge-se-s-di, a-le do-hi-yi ni-tsv-ne-s-di i-tse-hv-i.

021001.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏚᎴᏅ, ᏧᏗᏱ ᎦᏓ ᎠᎲᎢ ᎤᎷᏤᎢ, ᏦᏓᏂ ᎠᏍᎪᏂᏗᏢ ᏧᎶᎯᏍᏔᏁᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎢᎬᏩᏓᏟᏌᏁᎴᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎢᏳᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏄᏛᏁᎴᎢ, ᏔᎵᏁ ᏚᏪᏲᏁᎢ.
021001.english: And he arose from thence, and cometh into the coasts of Judaea by the farther side of Jordan: and the people resort unto him again; and, as he was wont, he taught them again.
021001.phonetic: Na-hna-no du-le-nv, Tsu-di-yi ga-da a-hv-i u-lu-tse-i, Tso-da-ni a-s-go-ni-di-tlv tsu-lo-hi-s-ta-ne-i; a-le u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi ta-li-ne i-gv-wa-da-tli-sa-ne-le-i; a-le i-yu-dv-ne-di ge-sv nu-dv-ne-le-i, ta-li-ne du-we-yo-ne-i.

021002.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏᏃ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸ ᎬᏩᏛᏛᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᏚᏳᎪᏗᏍᎪ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏓᎢᏅᏗᏱ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ?  ᎬᏩᎪᎵᏰᏍᎨᎢ.
021002.english: And the Pharisees came to him, and asked him, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife? tempting him.
021002.phonetic: A-ni-qua-li-si-no gv-wa-lu-tse-lv gv-wa-dv-dv-ne-i, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i, Du-yu-go-di-s-go a-s-ga-ya u-da-i-nv-di-yi u-da-li-i?  Gv-wa-go-li-ye-s-ge-i.

021003.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏑᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏕ ᎢᏥᏁᏤᎴ ᎼᏏ?
021003.english: And he answered and said unto them, What did Moses command you?
021003.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-no hi-a ni-su-we-se-le-i, Ga-do u-s-de i-tsi-ne-tse-le Mo-si?

021004.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᎼᏏ ᎤᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏁ ᎪᏪᎶᏗᏱ ᏙᎦᎴᏅᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᎢᏅᏗᏱ.
021004.english: And they said, Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement, and to put her away.
021004.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-ni-we-se-i, Mo-si u-li-s-go-lv-ta-ne go-we-lo-di-yi do-ga-le-nv-hv-i, a-le a-da-i-nv-di-yi.

021005.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏗᏍᏓᏱᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏗᏥᎾᏫ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎢᏦᏪᎳᏁᎴᎢ;
021005.english: And Jesus answered and said unto them, For the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept.
021005.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-ne-tsv, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Di-s-da-yi-yu ge-sv i-yu-s-di di-tsi-na-wi hi-a na-s-gi di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di i-tso-we-la-ne-le-i;

021006.cherokee: ᏗᏓᎴᏂᏍᎬᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏪᏢᏅᏅᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎨᏴ ᏚᏬᏢᏁᎢ.
021006.english: But from the beginning of the creation God made them male and female.
021006.phonetic: Di-da-le-ni-s-gv-s-gi-ni u-we-tlv-nv-nv-i, U-ne-la-nv-hi a-s-ga-ya a-le a-ge-yv du-wo-tlv-ne-i.

021007.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏕᎬᏕᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ ᎠᏜᏓᏩᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ;
021007.english: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and cleave to his wife;
021007.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-s-ga-ya de-gv-de-ge-s-di u-do-da a-le u-tsi, a-le u-da-li-i a-dla-wa-s-di-s-ge-s-di;

021008.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᏌᏉᏉ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏇᏓᎸᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᏱᎩ, ᏌᏉᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏂᏇᏓᎸᎢ.
021008.english: And they twain shall be one flesh: so then they are no more twain, but one flesh.
021008.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no a-ni-ta-li sa-quo-quo ge-se-s-di u-ni-que-da-lv-i; na-s-gi-no v-tla hna-quo a-ni-ta-li yi-gi, sa-quo-quo-s-gi-ni u-ni-que-da-lv-i.

021009.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᏭᏓᏔᏅᎯ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏴᏫ ᏧᎦᎴᏅᏔᏅᎩ.
021009.english: What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.
021009.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-wu-da-ta-nv-hi tle-s-di yv-wi tsu-ga-le-nv-ta-nv-gi.

021010.cherokee: ᎦᎵᏦᏕᏃ ᎠᏂᏯᎥ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎢᎬᏩᏛᏛᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
021010.english: And in the house his disciples asked him again of the same matter.
021010.phonetic: Ga-li-tso-de-no a-ni-ya-v gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi ta-li-ne i-gv-wa-dv-dv-ne na-s-gi u-gv-wa-li.

021011.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎩᎶ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ ᎠᏓᎡᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᎠᏓᏰᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏓᏲᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏅᏤᎮᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ.
021011.english: And he saith unto them, Whosoever shall put away his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her.
021011.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, Gi-lo u-da-li-i a-da-e-ge-s-di, a-le nv-wa-da-le a-da-ye-ge-s-di, na-s-gi a-da-yo-ne-he-s-di a-s-ga-nv-tse-he-s-di na-s-gi.

021012.cherokee: ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎤᏰᎯ ᎠᏓᎡᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᏓᎾᏤᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏓᏲᏁᎮᏍᏗ.
021012.english: And if a woman shall put away her husband, and be married to another, she committeth adultery.
021012.phonetic: I-yu a-le a-ge-yv u-ye-hi a-da-e-ge-s-di, a-le nv-wa-da-le da-na-tse-ge-s-di, na-s-gi a-da-yo-ne-he-s-di.

021013.cherokee: ᏧᎾᏍᏗᏃ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᏕᎬᏩᏘᏃᎮᎴᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏒᏂᏍᏗᏱ; ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᏚᏂᎬᏍᎪᎸᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎾᏘᏃᎸᎯ.
021013.english: And they brought young children to him, that he should touch them: and his disciples rebuked those that brought them.
021013.phonetic: Tsu-na-s-di-no di-ni-yo-li de-gv-wa-ti-no-he-le-i, na-s-gi tsu-sv-ni-s-di-yi; gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no du-ni-gv-s-go-lv-ne na-s-gi tsu-na-ti-no-lv-hi.

021014.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏒᏍᎦ ᎤᏰᎸᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏕᏤᎵᏎᎮᏍᏗ ᏧᎾᏍᏗ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᎬᎩᎷᏤᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏗᏥᏅᏍᏓᏕᎮᏍᏗ; ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏄᎾᏍᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ.
021014.english: But when Jesus saw it, he was much displeased, and said unto them, Suffer the little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.
021014.phonetic: Tsi-sa-s-gi-ni u-go-hv u-tsa-ti sv-s-ga u-ye-lv-ne-i, a-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, U-ne-la-gi de-tse-li-se-he-s-di tsu-na-s-di di-ni-yo-li gv-gi-lu-tse-di-yi, a-le tle-s-di yi-di-tsi-nv-s-da-de-he-s-di; na-s-gi-ye-no nu-na-s-di U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-go-hi.

021015.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏂᏓᏓᏂᎸᎬᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎠᏲᎵ ᏥᏓᏓᏂᎸᎪᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎾᎿᎭᏱᏮᎬᏴᎭ.
021015.english: Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall not enter therein.
021015.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, i-yu-no gi-lo U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv ni-da-da-ni-lv-gv-na i-ge-se-s-di na-s-gi-ya a-yo-li tsi-da-da-ni-lv-go-i, v-tla na-hna yi-wv-gv-yv-ha.

021016.english: And he took them up in his arms, put his hands upon them, and blessed them.

021017.cherokee: ᎤᏂᎩᏒᏃ ᎦᏅᏅ ᏭᎷᏨ, ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏗᏠᎡᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎵᏂᏆᏅᏁᎸᎩ, ᎤᏛᏛᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎰᏍᏛ ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᎦᏙ ᏓᎦᏛᏁᎵ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎠᏆᏤᎵ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ?
021017.english: And when he was gone forth into the way, there came one running, and kneeled to him, and asked him, Good Master, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?
021017.phonetic: U-ni-gi-sv-no ga-nv-nv wu-lu-tsv, gi-lo i-yu-s-di u-di-tlo-e-lv-gi, a-le du-li-ni-qua-nv-ne-lv-gi, u-dv-dv-ne hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, Ho-s-dv Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, ga-do da-ga-dv-ne-li u-li-s-qua-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na gv-ni-dv a-qua-tse-li i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi?

021018.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎰᏍᏛ ᎢᏍᏉᏎᎭ ᎠᏴ?  ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏱᎩ, ᏌᏉ ᎤᏩᏒᎯᏳ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ.
021018.english: And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? there is none good but one, that is, God.
021018.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, Ga-do-no ho-s-dv i-s-quo-se-ha a-yv?  V-tla gi-lo o-s-dv yi-gi, sa-quo u-wa-sv-hi-yu, na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi.

021019.cherokee: ᏕᎯᎦᏔᎭ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏣᏓᏲᏁᎸᎩ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏣᏓᎸᎩ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏣᏃᏍᎩᏒᎩ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᎦᏰᎪᎩ ᏣᏃᎮᎸᎩ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏣᏓᎶᏄᎮᎸᎩ, ᏣᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᏣᏥ ᏕᎯᎸᏉᏕᏍᏗ.
021019.english: Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Defraud not, Honour thy father and mother.
021019.phonetic: De-hi-ga-ta-ha di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di, na-s-gi, Tle-s-di tsa-da-yo-ne-lv-gi, Tle-s-di tsa-da-lv-gi, Tle-s-di tsa-no-s-gi-sv-gi, Tle-s-di ga-ye-go-gi tsa-no-he-lv-gi, Tle-s-di tsa-da-lo-nu-he-lv-gi, Tsa-do-da a-le tsa-tsi de-hi-lv-quo-de-s-di.

021020.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏓᎩᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏅ ᏥᏧᏣ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ.
021020.english: And he answered and said unto him, Master, all these have I observed from my youth.
021020.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-no hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, hi-a na-s-gi ni-ga-dv da-gi-s-qua-ni-go-ta-nv tsi-tsu-tsa ge-sv nv-da-gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv.

021021.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏅ, ᎤᎨᏳᎯᏳ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏑᏓᎴᎩ ᏣᎷᎳ; ᎮᎾ ᏫᎾᏚᎦ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏣᎾᎥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᎩᏁᎸᎭ, ᏦᏒᏃ ᏫᏣᎮᏍᏗ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ; ᎠᎴ ᏖᏒᎭ, ᏧᏓᎿᎭᏩᏛ ᎯᏴᎲᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏍᎩᏍᏓᏩᏕᏒᎭ.
021021.english: Then Jesus beholding him loved him, and said unto him, One thing thou lackest: go thy way, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, take up the cross, and follow me.
021021.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no du-ka-hna-nv, u-ge-yu-hi-yu nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, Su-da-le-gi tsa-lu-la; he-na wi-na-du-ga ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di tsa-na-v-i, a-le u-yo i-yu-na-dv-hna-de-gi gi-ne-lv-ha, tso-sv-no wi-tsa-he-s-di tsu-gv-wa-lo-di; a-le te-sv-ha, tsu-da-hna-wa-dv hi-yv-hv-ha, a-le s-gi-s-da-wa-de-sv-ha.

021022.cherokee: ᎤᏲᏃ ᎤᏰᎸᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏥᏪᏎᎸ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᎯᏍᏗ ᎬᏩᏓᏅᏘ ᎤᏓᏅᏎᎢ; ᎤᏣᏘᏰᏃ ᎤᎿᎭᎡᎢ.
021022.english: And he was sad at that saying, and went away grieved: for he had great possessions.
021022.phonetic: U-yo-no u-ye-lv-ne na-s-gi na-tsi-we-se-lv, a-le e-hi-s-di gv-wa-da-nv-ti u-da-nv-se-i; u-tsa-ti-ye-no u-hna-e-i.

021023.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏚᏫᏛ ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏂᏙᎸ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎾᏍᏓᏯ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎤᏂᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᎲᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏭᏂᏴᏍᏗᏱ.
021023.english: And Jesus looked round about, and saith unto his disciples, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!
021023.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-du-wi-dv du-ka-hna-ni-do-lv, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, Na-s-da-ya tsu-gv-wa-lo-di u-ni-hi U-ne-la-hv-hi u-tse-li-go-hi wu-ni-yv-s-di-yi.

021024.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏪᏒᎢ.  ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎢᎤᏁᏨ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏗᏥᏲᎵ.  ᎾᏍᏓᏯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᎦᏍᏙᏗᏍᎩ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏭᏂᏴᏍᏗᏱ.
021024.english: And the disciples were astonished at his words. But Jesus answereth again, and saith unto them, Children, how hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter into the kingdom of God!
021024.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no gv-wa-s-qua-ni-go-se-le na-s-gi nu-we-sv-i.  A-se-no Tsi-sa ta-li-ne i-u-ne-tsv, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Di-tsi-yo-li.  na-s-da-ya na-s-gi ge-nv-i-s-di ge-sv a-na-li-s-ga-s-do-di-s-gi, U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-go-hi wu-ni-yv-s-di-yi.

021025.cherokee: ᎤᏟ ᎠᎯᏗᏳ ᎨᎻᎵ ᏴᎩ ᎦᏌᏁᎾᏛᏗᏱ ᎤᎦᏛᎴᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᏃ ᎤᏪᎿᎭᎢ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏭᏴᏍᏗᏱ.
021025.english: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
021025.phonetic: U-tli a-hi-di-yu ge-mi-li yv-gi ga-sa-ne-na-dv-di-yi u-ga-dv-le-hi-s-di-yi, a-no u-we-hna-i yv-wi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-go-hi wu-yv-s-di-yi.

021026.cherokee: ᎤᎶᏒᏍᏔᏅᎯᏃ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏍᎨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏓᎾᏓᏪᏎᎮᎢ, ᎦᎪᎨ ᏰᎵ ᎦᏰᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗ?
021026.english: And they were astonished out of measure, saying among themselves, Who then can be saved?
021026.phonetic: U-lo-sv-s-ta-nv-hi-no u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-s-ge-i, hi-a ni-da-na-da-we-se-he-i, Ga-go-ge ye-li ga-ye-tsi-s-de-lv-di?

021027.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏅ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᏴᏫ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏛᏁᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏝ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏰᏃ ᎨᏒ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎢᎬᏛᏁᏗ.
021027.english: And Jesus looking upon them saith, With men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.
021027.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no na-s-gi du-ka-hna-nv, hi-a nu-we-se-i, Yv-wi ge-sv na-s-gi i-gv-dv-ne-di ni-ge-sv-na, U-ne-la-nv-hi-s-gi-ni ge-sv v-tla; U-ne-la-nv-hi-ye-no ge-sv ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di i-gv-dv-ne-di.

021028.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏴ ᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᎣᎬᏕᏦᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏨᏍᏓᏩᏕᏅ.
021028.english: Then Peter began to say unto him, Lo, we have left all, and have followed thee.
021028.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Qui-da u-le-nv-he hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, Gv-ni-yu-quo a-yv ni-ga-di-yu o-gv-de-tso-nv, a-le ni-hi i-tsv-s-da-wa-de-nv.

021029.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏰᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏮᏕᏨᎯ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᏓᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏙᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏪᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎶᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᏴ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ,
021029.english: And Jesus answered and said, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my sake, and the gospel's,
021029.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-i; U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, v-tla gi-lo ye-ha na-s-gi u-wv-de-tsv-hi i-ge-se-s-di a-da-ne-lv-i, a-le a-na-li-nv-tli, a-le tsu-do, a-le u-do-da, a-le u-tsi, a-le u-da-li-i, a-le tsu-we-tsi, a-le du-lo-ge-sv-i, a-yv ge-sv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di, a-le o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di,

021030.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏥᏁᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᎢᏳᏩᎫᏗ ᎤᎶᏒᏍᏗ ᏓᏓᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏪᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎦᎶᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏔᎵ ᎤᏛᏗ; ᏐᎢᏱᏃ ᏗᎨᏒ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎬᏂᏛ.
021030.english: But he shall receive an hundredfold now in this time, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions; and in the world to come eternal life.
021030.phonetic: Na-s-gi go-hi ge-sv a-tsi-ne-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui i-yu-wa-gu-di u-lo-sv-s-di da-da-ne-lv-i, a-le a-na-li-nv-tli, a-le tsu-do, a-le tsu-tsi, a-le tsu-we-tsi, a-le de-ga-lo-ge-sv-i, a-gi-li-yo-i-s-di ge-sv ta-li u-dv-di; so-i-yi-no di-ge-sv u-li-s-qua-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na gv-ni-dv.

021031.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏔ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏥᎩ, ᎣᏂ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎣᏂᏃ ᏥᎩ, ᎢᎬᏱ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
021031.english: But many that are first shall be last; and the last first.
021031.phonetic: A-se-no u-ni-tsa-ta na-s-gi i-gv-yi tsi-gi, o-ni ge-se-s-di; o-ni-no tsi-gi, i-gv-yi ge-se-s-di.

021032.cherokee: ᎦᏅᏅᏃ ᎠᎾᎢᏎ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏣᏂᎦᏖᎢ; ᏥᏌᏃ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏚᏪᏅᎡᎴᎢ; ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᏃ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏩᏗᏒ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎮᎢ.  ᏔᎵᏁᏃ ᏚᏯᏅᎲ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ, ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᏚᏃᏁᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏓᏁᎵᏓᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
021032.english: And they were in the way going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus went before them: and they were amazed; and as they followed, they were afraid. And he took again the twelve, and began to tell them what things should happen unto him,
021032.phonetic: Ga-nv-nv-no a-na-i-se Tsi-lu-si-li-mi tsa-ni-ga-te-i; tsi-sa-no i-gv-yi du-we-nv-e-le-i; u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-no; na-s-gi-no a-na-li-s-da-wa-di-sv a-ni-s-ga-i-he-i.  Ta-li-ne-no du-ya-nv-hv ta-la-du i-ya-ni-dv, u-le-nv-he du-no-ne-le na-s-gi i-yu-li-s-da-ne-li-da-s-di ge-sv-i.

021033.cherokee: [ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎨᎢ;] ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏫᏗᎦᏘ; ᎠᎴ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏙᏓᏰᏥᏲᏏ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᏙᏓᎨᏥᏲᎯᏎᎵ; ᎠᎴ ᏙᏓᎬᏭᎪᏓᏁᎵ ᎤᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏫᏙᏛᏂᏲᎯᏎᎵ;
021033.english: Saying, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests, and unto the scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles:
021033.phonetic: [Hi-a ni-ga-we-s-ge-i;] Gv-ni-yu-quo Tsi-lu-si-li-mi wi-di-ga-ti; a-le yv-wi U-we-tsi do-da-ye-tsi-yo-si nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv a-ne-lo-hi a-le di-no-we-li-s-gi do-da-ge-tsi-yo-hi-se-li; a-le do-da-gv-wu-go-da-ne-li u-yo-hu-hi-s-di-yi, a-le tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi wi-do-dv-ni-yo-hi-se-li;

021034.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏓᎬᏩᏐᏢᎢᏍᏔᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎬᏩᎵᎥᏂᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᏓᎬᏩᎵᏥᏍᏈ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎬᏩᎵ; ᏦᎢᏁᏃ ᎢᎦ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏙᏛᎠᎴᏂ.
021034.english: And they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.
021034.phonetic: A-le da-gv-wa-so-tlv-i-s-ta-ni, a-le da-gv-wa-li-v-ni-li, a-le do-da-gv-wa-li-tsi-s-qui, a-le da-gv-wa-li; tso-i-ne-no i-ga ta-li-ne do-dv-a-le-ni.

021035.cherokee: ᏥᎻᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏣᏂ, ᏤᏈᏗ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᏍᎩᎾᏚᎵᎭ ᎢᏍᎩᎾᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎢᏍᏛᏔᏲᏎᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
021035.english: And James and John, the sons of Zebedee, come unto him, saying, Master, we would that thou shouldest do for us whatsoever we shall desire.
021035.phonetic: Tsi-mi-no a-le Tsa-ni, Tse-qui-di tsu-we-tsi gv-wa-lu-tse-le-i, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i, Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, s-gi-na-du-li-ha i-s-gi-na-dv-ne-di-yi nu-s-dv i-s-dv-ta-yo-se-di ge-sv-i.

021036.english: And he said unto them, What would ye that I should do for you?

021037.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏍᎩᎾᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏏ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏦᎩᏂᏗᏱ, ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎯᎦᏘᏏᏗᏢ, ᏐᎢᏃ ᎯᎦᏍᎦᏂᏗᏢ, ᏣᏤᎵ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ.
021037.english: They said unto him, Grant unto us that we may sit, one on thy right hand, and the other on thy left hand, in thy glory.
021037.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i, S-gi-na-li-s-go-lv-da-si, na-s-gi tso-gi-ni-di-yi, a-si-yv-wi hi-ga-ti-si-di-tlv, so-i-no hi-ga-s-ga-ni-di-tlv, tsa-tse-li ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv-i.

021038.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᏍᏗᎦᏔᎭ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎢᏍᏗᏔᏲᎯᎲᎢ.  ᏥᏌ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎨᏍᏓᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎤᎵᏍᏈᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏆᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᏥᎩ?  ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᏰᏍᏓᏬᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎥᏆᏬᏍᏙᏗ ᏥᎩ?
021038.english: But Jesus said unto them, Ye know not what ye ask: can ye drink of the cup that I drink of? and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?
